authors: Sayed Ashraful Islam Opin, Raiyan Ahmed Thakur, Ahsanul Karim Raiyan, Muhammad Rafsan Kabir, Shafin Rahman
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Financial institutions and regulators produce large volumes of complex, multilingual documents, making access to critical financial information challenging. Limited financial literacy further exacerbates this issue in countries like Bangladesh. This study introduces NextRAG, a bilingual Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) framework for financial knowledge retrieval and question answering in the Bangladeshi context. Unlike conventional RAG pipelines, NextRAG employs specialized preprocessing for noisy financial PDFs using OCR. The framework integrates sparse retrieval with BM25, probabilistic query modeling via Dirichlet smoothing, and fuses results using Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF), followed by cross-encoder re-ranking. It also supports Bangla and English interactions through languageaware prompting. Evaluation on a diverse bilingual financial Q&A dataset shows that NextRAG consistently outperforms conventional RAG, achieving a mean cosine similarity of 0.77 and an F1 score of 68.3%. These results demonstrate the effectiveness of hybrid retrieval in handling complex financial terminology, reducing retrieval errors and hallucinations in a low-resource bilingual setting.

title: Evaluating the Role of Board Characteristics in Driving Return on Assets and Financial Decisions in Bahrain’s Financial Sector
authors: Mohammed H. M. Qeshta, Hafnida Hasan, Hisham Madi, Mohamed Sobhi Abdelaziz
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This research examines the relationship between board characteristics and the return on assets (ROA) of companies in the financial sector listed on the Bahrain Bourse (BHB). This research examined all financial companies listed in Bahrain to fulfill its objectives. The sample for this study included 20 companies and encompassed 120 observations from 2017 to 2022. Data were collected manually from annual reports and official disclosures available on the BHB website. The study relied on specific board characteristics —such as size, meetings, professional certifications, academic specializations, gender, and independence —along with ROA as the dependent variable. To evaluate the study's hypotheses, a descriptive and multiple regression approach was employed. The study found no correlation between board characteristics and ROA. This research will be of immense value to board members, practitioners, and relevant authorities who wish to improve institutional performance in the Kingdom of Bahrain. The results also have implications for policymakers at financial sector companies listed on the BHB.

title: Detecting User Stances on AI-Generated YouTube Content
authors: Rohit Raj Gunti, J.M.Shalani Dilinika
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: There is a surge of digital users on several online platforms relying on AI-generated content to engage with other users or their followers. On video platforms, there are neutral or mixed opinions, leading to an uncertain user stance on the perception of AI-generated content. Understanding user stances on platforms like YouTube is essential for interpreting public discourse accurately. Hence, in this study, we analyze the viewer engagement to reflect real societal perspectives. Specifically, we collect several user interactions of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 7}$</tex> highly subscribed channels that primarily rely on AI-generated content. Then, we manually code five percent of the data collected for each channel in seven themes. Parallelly, we employ a BERT-based model for semantic topic discovery and an ensemble of Large Language Models for selective stance disambiguation. Using this automated coding, all the user interactions are coded in three stances, i.e., Infavor, Against, and Unclear. Finally, we compare manual coding with automated coding to validate the reliability of automated classifications. Findings showed that there is strong evidence of reliability for themes coded and stances detected while also revealing additional user perspectives on AI on YouTube. These perspectives will contribute to enhancing the existing AI techniques employed to identify user perceptions and misinformation risks on online platforms.

title: Exploring Regional Variation in Bangla: Automating Dialect Classification Using Deep Learning
authors: Mahmudul Hasan Badhan, Rubel Ahmod, Promit Purkayastha, Sandwip Dash Talukder, Munshaibur Rahman Mahin, Md. Abu Naser Mojumder
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Language plays an important role in expressing human emotions and identity, with dialects serving as rich reflections of cultural and regional diversity. Although Bangla is the official language of Bangladesh, numerous dialects are spoken across different regions, often posing challenges in mutual understanding and communication. This research proposes a deep learning-based approach for Bangla dialect classification using character-level sequences and attention mechanisms. We manually collected a dataset from various online sources and native speakers, then preprocessed and balanced it to ensure consistent representation. Multiple deep learning models—CNN, GRU, LSTM, and BiLSTM—were implemented and evaluated. The BiLSTM with Attention model achieved the highest performance, reaching 88.43% accuracy and an F1 score of 0.88, demonstrating its effectiveness in capturing both past and future context. This research contributes to the preservation of dialectal variation in Bangla, offers a strong baseline for future studies in low-resource NLP tasks, and highlights the importance of attention-enhanced architectures in dialect classification.

title: Fast CU Splitting in VVC: A DCT-Based Homogeneity-Aware Approach for Encoding Complexity Reduction
authors: Md. Zahirul Islam, Fatema Tuz Zohra, Noman Amin
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The surge in demand for audiovisual content has exceeded the capabilities of existing communication systems, prompting the development of the Versatile Video Coding (VVC) standard. Compared to its predecessor, High Efficiency Video Coding (HEVC), VVC offers approximately 40% bitrate savings; however, this benefit comes at the cost of significantly increased encoding complexity. To address this challenge, we propose a Discrete Cosine Transform (DCT)-based framework aimed at enhancing the efficiency of Coding Unit (CU) partitioning. Within this framework, DCT proves highly effective in intra-frame signal representation by partitioning frames into non-overlapping homogeneous and non-homogeneous rectangular regions. Leveraging DCT's ability to capture spatial homogeneity and its computational efficiency, this work introduces a homogeneity-driven method for predicting CU split structures in VVC. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed approach achieves up to a 22.29% reduction in intra-mode encoding time relative to the standalone VVC encoder, with minimal impact on bitrate and perceptual quality, as evaluated by Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR), Structural Similarity Index Measure (SSIM), and Video Multi-Method Assessment Fusion (VMAF) metrics.

title: A Deep Bi-LSTM-Based Motor Imagery Braincomputer Interface for Wheelchair Navigation
authors: M.T.A. Karunarathne, J.S. Aththanayaka, S.M.U.S. Samarakoon, M.I. Uvais, H.M.K.K.M.B. Herath, S.L.P. Yasakethu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Brain-Computer Interface (BCI) systems provide autonomy to individuals with severe motor impairments, yet practical adoption is hindered by a significant performance gap in reliability. This paper addresses these limitations by presenting a BCI system for wheelchair navigation. We propose a deep Bi-LSTM network optimized to classify four navigation commands and a rest state as a default fail-safe from motor imagery EEG signals. Using the public PhysioNet dataset, seven sensorimotor channels were filtered in the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$8-30 ~\text{Hz}$</tex> range to isolate <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{m u}$</tex> and beta rhythms, achieving a mean accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 9 4}$</tex> (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 0.018$</tex>). The evaluation rigorously assesses the model using subject-independent splits and precision, recall, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex> scores to ensure deployment readiness. The system employs a cloud platform for high-fidelity command generation, while an ESP32-based hardware layer ensures responsive execution via MQTT. Validated through simulation and a physical prototype, this framework bridges the gap between the complexity of deep learning and the constraints of embedded systems, providing a robust command-generation module for assistive mobility.

title: Non-Intrusive Speech Intelligibility Measures Using Multi-Metric Approach
authors: Refatul Islam Roni, Israt Jahan Muna, Nursadul Mamun
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Most existing objective speech intelligibility measures require access to a clean reference signal, which limits their use in practical acoustic environments where clean speech is usually unavailable. This motivates the need for nonintrusive intelligibility assessment methods that operate directly on noisy signals. In this work, we focus on two complementary non-intrusive intelligibility metrics-Short-Time Objective Intelligibility (STOI) and the Spectrogram Orthogonal Polynomial Measure (SOPM) and highlight their conceptual similarity in capturing speech degradation under noisy conditions. Building upon this relationship, we propose a deep learning framework that jointly predicts STOI and SOPM using an encoder, convolutional feature extractors, Bidirectional Gated Recurrent Units (BiGRU), and a self-attention mechanism to model spectral-temporal dependencies. Experimental evaluations demonstrate strong predictive performance when each metric is estimated individually (SRCC: 0.9672 for STOI and 0.9650 for SOPM), while joint prediction yields even higher correlation (SRCC: 0.9747) and lower error (MSE: 0.0019). These findings reveal that combining both intelligibility metrics improves prediction accuracy compared to learning them independently. Overall, the proposed non-intrusive approach bridges the gap between traditional reference-based evaluation and real-world acoustic conditions while demonstrating the benefit of integrated intelligibility estimation.

title: xLSTM-Based Framework for Accurate Rainfall Forecasting Across Bangladesh: A Comparative Analysis with LSTM and GRU
authors: Rakib Ahammed Diptho, Pial Ghosh, Sarnali Basak
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Bangladesh is extremely vulnerable to flood and drought due to its unpredictable monsoon rainfalls. As such, accurate forecasts of rainfall will greatly aid in disaster management and agricultural planning. The study used three different advanced deep learning architectures, namely xLSTM, Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU), and Long Short Term Memory (LSTM), to predict rainfall based on a 70 -year historical weather data set which was gathered at 36 different locations across Bangladesh. We also addressed the common problem of class imbalance in rainfall data by using the Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique (SMOTE). Experimental results show that xLSTM achieved the best performance with 93.86 % accuracy, 98.39 % precision, 93.96 % recall, and a 96.12 % F1 score, while the other two architectures performed lower overall. This performance improvement results from xLSTM's enhanced ability to capture long-term dependencies and nonlinear relationships within meteorological variables. Its strong predictive capability highlights its potential for developing more accurate and reliable rainfall forecasting models. Overall, our findings reflect the model's strengths and the growing advancement of deep learning in environmental prediction.

title: Physics-Informed Neural Network for Inverse Design of LDMOS Devices with Ultra-high FOM via Wafer-Level Validation
authors: Yixian Song, Yan Pan, Qiao Teng, Xiaoyun Huang, Dawei Gao, Kai Xu
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: The design of Laterally Diffused MOSFETs (LDMOS) inherently faces a trade-off between breakdown voltage (V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">B</inf>) and specific on-resistance (R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on,sp</inf>). This work proposes a Physics-Informed Neural Network (PINN) framework that embeds governing partial differential equations （PDEs） directly into the loss function, enabling accurate prediction of V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">B</inf> and R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on,sp</inf> with significantly reduced data requirements. Compared to conventional neural networks, the PINN achieves superior accuracy and generalization, maintaining interpolation and extrapolation errors below 3%. Furthermore, the differentiable nature of the model allows an inverse design approach, which efficiently identifies optimal device geometries and implantations. Finally, the model's strong practical applicability is rigorously confirmed through 12-inch full-wafer tape-out data. It achieves a superior trade-off between V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">B</inf> (66.5 V) and R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on,sp</inf> (22.6 mΩ•mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>), resulting in the state-of-the-art Baliga’s figure of merit (FOM，V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">B</inf> <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>/R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on,sp</inf>) of 19.56 MV/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, demonstrating substantial reductions in both development time and computational cost.

title: Bangla Visual Question Answering Using Gated Cross-Attention and Reinforcement-Optimized Fusion
authors: Md. Fariaz Islam, Md. Mostafizur Rahman, Mohammad Isfar Nayen, Md. Mahmudul Arif, Hasib Hasnat, Refatul Islam Roni, Tonmoy Das, Md. Emran Hossain Dipu
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Visual Question Answering (VQA) in low-resource languages like Bangla poses dual challenges: effectively modeling complex linguistic structures and integrating visual and textual information to generate accurate answers. Traditional supervised methods often fail to capture intricate multimodal dependencies and rely heavily on large labeled datasets, which are scarce for Bangla. To address these challenges, we propose a reinforcement learning-based Bangla VQA system that combines MobileViT for visual feature extraction and Bangla BERT for question understanding, fused via a gated cross-attention mechanism to capture fine-grained interactions between image and text. The model is trained using a hybrid strategy: supervised pretraining with cross-entropy loss followed by self-critical REINFORCE optimization, allowing iterative improvement through reward-driven feedback. Experiments on the BVQA dataset demonstrate superior performance across all question types, achieving 75.60% overall accuracy and outperforming state-of-the-art baselines. This approach provides an efficient solution for low-resource, multimodal learning in Bangla.

title: Artificial Intelligence Based Smart Glass for Navigating Vision Impaired Individuals
authors: MGT Sandaruwan, MKP Madushanka
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: The proposed research is an AI-based indoor navigation system that helps visually impaired people navigate with the assistance of object detection, depth estimation, and natural-language guidance. The system operates on a stereo-vision camera with a YOLOv8 model which has been trained to locate two important objects of the interior space, namely, tables as a navigation object and chairs as an obstacle object, to model mobility in controlled conditions. The depth estimation is carried out based on stereo disparity mapping, which allows measuring the distance and obstacle avoidance in real-time. A rule-based navigation module converts spatial knowledge to step-by-step voice instructions produced by a Rule-based language model and passed on to TTS. Several indoor configurations of different table-chair layouts were used to test the system with different multiple configurations to assess the performance of the system in terms of accuracy in detection, reliability in navigation and performance in terms of user-safety. The findings indicate that the system has high accuracy in object detection and is able to circumvent obstacles and direct users to the target point. This prototype shows that it is possible to use vision-based indoor mobility aids at low cost and give them a basis to be expanded to multi-object environment, autonomous path finding, and wearable embedded implementation.

title: A Grapheme-Aware LLM Integrated Hybrid Framework for Automated Text-to-Chart Conversion in Bengali Language
authors: Samia Rahman, Momtazul Arefin Labib
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: In domains like economics, health, and journalism, text embedded with numerical data is common, yet readers often struggle to derive insights. Converting such texts into charts enhances comprehension but is typically labor-intensive and domain-dependent. Despite progress in English, there is no prior dataset for Bengali. To fill this gap, we have introduced BETAR, a BEngali Text-to-chARt dataset comprising 3,519 annotated texts. We also propose BN-GraBERTNet, a grapheme-aware hybrid model that combines BanglaBERT, BiLSTM, and a fully connected layer to identify <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$x$</tex>-axis and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$y$</tex>-axis entities in text. To handle complex numerical reasoning, we selectively employ open-source large language models (LLMs) to simplify sentences when necessary. These simplified sentences are then processed by our sequence tagging model. The primary goal of this work is to develop a lightweight, user-friendly, and cost-free Bengali test-to-chart system that performs competitively. Although we also evaluated open-source, purely LLM-based approaches, our proposed architecture outperformed them, achieving a weighted average <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex> score of 0.93 on the test set.

title: A Narrative Review of Decision Support Approaches for Teaching Mode Selection in Higher Education: Evidence From Sri Lankan and South Asian Contexts
authors: P.A. Janendra Chamodi, Dinesh Asanka
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: The COVID-19 pandemic accelerated the largescale adoption of online learning in higher education, intensifying institutional challenges related to selecting appropriate teaching modes for diverse academic modules. While online learning offers flexibility and accessibility, concerns regarding student engagement, learning effectiveness, and contextual suitability remain prominent when compared with traditional physical instruction. This study presents a structured narrative literature review examining how student feedback and module-specific characteristics, including credit count, interaction level, class size, and assessment type, can inform structured decision support for teaching mode selection in higher education. This study presents a structured narrative review examining how student feedback and module-specific characteristics, including credit count, interaction level, class size, and assessment type, can inform decision support for teaching mode selection in higher education. Relevant literature was identified from major academic databases and synthesized to capture key trends and insights. The reviewed studies are organized into four thematic areas: (1) student feedback and perceptions, (2) comparative effectiveness of online and physical learning, (3) analytical and computational approaches in education, and (4) Sri Lankan and South Asian contextual studies. The findings indicate that student feedback is primarily used for descriptive evaluation rather than as an input for structured decision-making. Furthermore, institutional approaches rarely integrate pedagogical parameters in a systematic manner. This review highlights the need for a feedback-driven decision support perspective that incorporates both student perceptions and module characteristics. The study contributes a conceptual foundation for developing contextaware and evidence-informed approaches to teaching mode selection in higher education.

title: X-RF-DETR: A Framework for Explainable and Trustworthy PPE Decision Support
authors: Ezzaldeen Al-Tayar, Saleem Ahmed Al-Azazi, Mogeeb A.Saeed
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Hazardous industries such as construction report high accident rates, where non-compliance with Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) is a significant contributing factor. While AI-powered computer vision systems are increasingly used for automated monitoring, their "black box" nature often impedes trust, user adoption, and accountability. This paper proposes a novel conceptual framework, X-RF-DETR, designed to provide not only accurate but also explainable and trustworthy real-time PPE compliance monitoring to aid decision-making in occupational health and safety (OHS). The proposed framework integrates the RF-DETR Transformer-based detector with a dedicated Explainable AI (XAI) module. This module generates visual evidence to explain the model's detection results, thereby enhancing transparency and interpretability. Although conceptual, the framework’s feasibility is supported by a proposed validation plan using public PPE data and standard explainability metrics. The primary contribution is the synthesis of a high-performance detection model with an XAI layer to address the critical gap of trustworthiness in AI safety systems, aligning with AI risk management standards like ISO/IEC 23894:2023. Ultimately, the X-RF-DETR framework promises to enhance human-AI collaboration, facilitate more informed decisions for safety managers, and increase the acceptance of automated monitoring technologies in high-risk environments.

title:
A Novel Ferroelectric-based SnO
                    <sub>x</sub>
                    FET with Non-volatility and Symmetrical Ambipolarity for In-Memory-Searching toward Edge AI
authors: Runze Han, Zening Gao, Weikai Xu, Bingrui Song, Jin Luo, Qianqian Huang, Qian Xin, Ru Huang
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: In this work, a novel ferroelectric (FE) based non-volatile ambipolar FET (NV-AFET) with tin monoxide (SnO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</inf>) channel is proposed and experimentally demonstrated with enhanced symmetry and back-end-of-line (BEOL) compatibility for area-efficient in-memory-searching (IMS) system. The NV-AFET leverages the symmetrical symmetric transport properties of SnO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</inf> oxide semiconductor to achieve non-monotonic ambipolarity, enabling XNOR operator-based content addressable memory (CAM) cell within only one transistor. Moreover, the ambipolar symmetry of NV-AFET is further enhanced via synergistic optimization of the gate-channel interface and area ratio, boosting matching accuracy and enabling multi-level expansion for high area-efficiency IMS. It presents a promising solution for edge AI hardware.

title: Zero-Knowledge Consent: Auditable and Private Data Permission Management via Blockchain
authors: Filippo Scaramuzza, Marco Tonnarelli, Damian A. Tamburri, Willem-Jan van den Heuvel
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Software Analysis, Evolution and Reengineering - Companion (SANER-C)
abstract: The challenge of achieving auditable, GDPR-compliant consent management while preserving true data subject privacy persists, as current blockchain-based solutions compromise anonymity through public ledgers. This paper addresses this by introducing a novel, privacy-by-design IT artefact built on the Ethereum platform that employs Zero-knowledge Succinct Non-Interactive ARgument of Knowledge (zk-SNARKs) to enable truly anonymous and irrefutable validation of data processing permissions. Implemented and evaluated through a Design Science Research (DSR) approach, the artefact demonstrated a high degree of functional and GDPR compliance, confirming its capacity to generate trustless, auditable on-chain proof of permission status. The proof of concept successfully implements core privacy-by-design principles through anonymity and encryption, with technical benchmarks indicating acceptable performance for the artefact's intended use despite the computational intensity of proof generation.

title: IPnest: A Permissioned Blockchain Framework for Secure and Transparent Intellectual Property Management Using Hyperledger Fabric, NFTs, and IPFS
authors: Gazi Maliha Raisa Noor, Md. Ashikul Islam, Saha Reno, Saba Al Mukter Mahin
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Managing intellectual property (IP) in the digital age presents significant challenges, including unauthorized duplication, unclear ownership, and insufficient verification mechanisms. This paper proposes a blockchain-based framework that integrates Hyperledger Fabric, Interplanetary File System (IPFS), and Non-Fungible Tokens (NFTs) to ensure secure, transparent, and tamper-proof IP management. Hyperledger Fabric serves as the permissioned blockchain platform, using the RAFT (Replicated And Fault-Tolerant) consensus protocol to achieve fast, fault-tolerant agreement with low latency and moderate resource consumption. IP assets are stored off-chain using IPFS, with their metadata encapsulated in NFTs for unique identification, traceability, and automated licensing via smart contracts. The system enables decentralized IP registration, verification, and transfer while preserving content integrity. Performance analysis shows that RAFT consistently outperforms PoW, PoS, and Clique in throughput, latency, and CPU usage, making it well-suited for high-performance enterprise applications. A cost breakdown also confirms the system's affordability and scalability. Finally, command-line validation demonstrates correct ledger operations and smart contract functionality. The proposed solution offers a practical and efficient alternative to traditional IP systems, with future improvements targeting enhanced access control and support for large-scale IP ecosystems.

title: A Hybrid Machine Learning Framework for Early Detection and Heuristic Severity Prediction of Autism Spectrum Disorder Using Q-CHAT-10
authors: Md. Mahbubur Rahman, Md. Mehedi Hasan Shihab, Mohammad Mahfuzul Islam
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Today's renowned problem Autism Spectrum Disorder is not any typical disease. It's totally developmental and neurological disorder. This problem typically appears in early childhood. If autism spectrum disorder (ASD) is diagnosed early, the treatment can be less painful for the kids which also helps them to lead a near to normal life. This study proposes a hybrid ensemble framework which combines Logistic Regression, Support Vector Machine (SVM), and KNearest Neighbors (KNN) with a LightGBM meta-classifier for ASD classification. Also shows an arithmetic calculation based way for severity prediction. The model was validated through stratified train-test splits, 15-fold cross-validation, and external testing. Severity was categorized into None, Mild, Moderate and Severe based on Q-CHAT scores. Results show 99.51% internal and 97.44% external accuracy for binary classification. 100% accuracy for severity prediction using arithmetic operation (not clinically validated). The approach demonstrates feasibility for resource-constrained and community-level screening.

title: Water Quality Monitoring and Detection Using Machine Learning and Deep Learning Techniques with IoT Sensor-Based Data
authors: Md. Jakir Hossen, Riadul Islam Rabbi, Sutiyo, Hilal H. Nuha
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Water quality monitoring is crucial for safeguarding public health and ensuring safe drinking water. Traditional methods for testing water quality are slow, expensive, and require manual labor, making them unsuitable for continuous monitoring. This study explores the integration of Internet of Things (IoT) technology with machine learning and deep learning techniques for automated water quality monitoring and anomaly detection. We compared the performance of six distinct algorithms: Decision Tree, Random Forest, XGBoost, Deep Neural Network (DNN), Artificial Neural Network (ANN), and 1D Convolutional Neural Network (1D-CNN). The study utilized real water quality data, incorporating parameters such as <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p H}$</tex>, temperature, turbidity, and dissolved oxygen. To address data imbalance, the Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique (SMOTE) was employed, and an 80:20 train-test split was used for evaluation. The results indicate that traditional machine learning methods outperformed deep learning approaches. Random Forest achieved the highest accuracy of 85 %, followed by XGBoost at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 2 \%}$</tex> and Decision Tree at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 5 \%}$</tex>. In contrast, the deep learning models exhibited lower performance: the DNN reached an accuracy of 67 %, the ANN attained 68 %, and the 1D-CNN recorded <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 4 \%}$</tex>. The traditional machine learning models also demonstrated superior precision, recall, and F1-scores compared to the deep learning methods. These findings suggest that, for water quality monitoring applications, traditional machine learning algorithms are likely to be more suitable than complex deep learning models, particularly in IoT environments with limited computational resources. The study offers valuable insights into the selection of appropriate algorithms for realworld water quality monitoring systems.

title: A Graph Transformation-Driven TCAD Method for Efficient Device Characteristic Prediction
authors: Minrong Zhang, Yuxuan Zhai, Ziyi Hu, Dashan Shang, Rui Chen, Ling Li
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: Deep learning continues to make significant breakthroughs in computational science, demonstrating immense potential for applications in semiconductor device simulation. This study proposes a deep learning model based on the Graph Transformer for the rapid prediction of the electrical characteristics of TCAD devices. Considering the importance of the topological relationships between nodes in the device's physical structure, a positional encoding is incorporated into the model to endow the network with a perception of the device's spatial layout. Experimental results demonstrate that our model, even when trained on a small dataset (less than 1000 samples), yields predictions with a high degree of consistency with commercial TCAD software (MSE: 8×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−4</sup>). This provides a viable new pathway to accelerate the iterative design and optimization process for semiconductor devices.

title: Power Management Circuit Building Blocks Based on 2D Molybdenum Disulfide
authors: Jingde Xu, Xinrong Shan, Jieya Shang, Weijie Sun, Zhengjie Sun, Zhejia Zhang, Mingrui Ao, Xinliu He, Haojie Chen, Yuchen Tian, Yufei Song, Yuexi Wang, Yue Zhang, Zihan Xu, Xiangqi Dong, Wenzhong Bao
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: This study employs a top-gate process to fabricate monolayer MoS<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> transistors. By leveraging the work function difference between Au gate (5.1 eV) and Al gate (4.08 eV), enhancement-mode (E-mode) and depletion-mode (D-mode) devices are realized, enabling precise control over the threshold voltage (V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">TH</inf>). Using these devices, we fabricated a five-transistor operational transconductance amplifier (OTA), a 3-bit resistor-divider digital-to-analog converter (DAC), as well as 2-stage and 4-stage positive/negative charge pumps. The 3-bit DAC exhibits excellent linearity, while the E-D NMOS-based five-transistor OTA can be flexibly controlled via bias signals applied through the DAC. The MoS<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> diodes exhibit a rectification ratio >10⁴. The 2-stage charge pump achieves stable output within 0.1 s under a 100 Hz clock signal, while the 4-stage charge pump demonstrates excellent stability with output fluctuation as low as 0.012 V. This research provides a viable solution for integrating two-dimensional materials into power management circuits, advancing the development of low-power system-on-chip (SoC) applications for 2D semiconductors.

title: Explainable AI (XAI) Driven Neural–Machine Learning Ensemble for Business Customer Churn Prediction
authors: Munshaibur Rahman Mahin, Abdullah Al Prince, Arif Rahman Samad, Md Rajib Ahmed, Shajedul Hasan Arman, Md Mahadi Hasan
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Customer churn prediction is a critical challenge in the telecommunications industry, where retaining subscribers is far more cost-effective than acquiring new ones. Traditional models such as XGBoost, LightGBM, and Random Forest achieve competitive accuracy but often lack interpretability and struggle with complex non-linear relationships. To overcome these limitations, this paper introduces the proposed stacked ensemble framework, which integrates tree-based learners with a neural Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP) as the meta-classifier. This design exploits the complementary strengths of machine learning and deep learning, combining robust decision boundaries with enhanced feature representation. To mitigate class imbalance, the Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique (SMOTE) is applied. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed model achieves superior performance, with an accuracy of 85.2%, precision of 71.7%, recall of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 5. 4 \%}$</tex>, F1-score of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 3. 5 \%}$</tex>, and ROC-AUC of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 9. 1 \%}$</tex>. Furthermore, the proposed model delivers explainable predictions via SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP), enabling identification of key churn drivers. By unifying predictive strength with interpretability, the proposed framework provides both global and local insights, offering an accurate, transparent, and business-ready solution for customer retention in telecommunications.

title: Machine Learning Approaches to Classify and Predict Water Quality Status in the Surma River, Sylhet, Bangladesh
authors: Md. Anamul Hossain Chowdhury, Nishad Mahmud Opu, Md Fazla Rabbi, Nabil Mahmud Sakib, MD Shofiur Rahman, Pujan Dey Anik, Rayhan Mahmud Rakib
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Water is essential for sustaining human, animal, and ecological life. However, river water quality in Bangladesh has deteriorated in recent years, posing risks to public health, aquatic ecosystems, and long-term development. Accurate prediction of water quality is therefore critical for effective management. This study evaluates several machine learning (ML) models for water quality classification (WQC) of the Surma River in Sylhet using 669 samples collected from three sites over 18.5 years (January 2007-July 2025). Eight physicochemical parameters (pH, DO, BOD, COD, TDS, SS, EC, and temperature) were used to train six ML classifiers: KNN, SVM, Random Forest, Logistic Regression, XGBoost and LightGBM. LightGBM achieved the best performance with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 5. 5 2} \boldsymbol{\%}$</tex> accuracy, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 5. 5 3 \%}$</tex> precision, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 5. 5 2 \%}$</tex> recall, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 5. 5 0 \%}$</tex> F1-score. To ensure robustness, 5-fold cross-validation was conducted, and a temporal hold-out validation (training on 2007-2020 and testing on 2021-2025) was performed to assess predictive capability on future data. Results indicate that the river's overall water quality is marginal, with suspended solids and temperature identified as the most influential factors affecting WQI. This study is the first to apply ML-based models to the Surma River and provides a data-driven framework to support policymakers in designing effective pollution control and sustainable water resource management strategies.

title: Digital Leadership and Crisis Management: Lessons from Higher Education Institutions in the Kingdom of Bahrain During the COVID-19 Pandemic
authors: Ahmed Arbab, Osama El Abdein, Siddiq Babikir
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: The COVID-19 pandemic profoundly disrupted higher education systems worldwide, compelling universities to adopt rapid digital transformation strategies to ensure educational continuity. This study examines the role of digital leadership in mitigating the educational risks of the COVID-19 crisis within higher education institutions in the Kingdom of Bahrain. Through a conceptual and analytical framework, the paper explores three core dimensions: the concept and principles of digital leadership, the nature of risks and crises with a focus on the COVID-19 pandemic, and the practical measures undertaken by Bahraini universities to sustain learning during the crisis. The findings reveal that the pandemic accelerated the transition from traditional and semi-digital leadership models to fully digital leadership approaches in Bahraini universities. Institutional leaders effectively utilized digital tools, risk management frameworks, and e-learning systems to maintain academic quality and operational resilience. Moreover, the Higher Education Council’s (HEC) policies and guidelines played a critical role in ensuring the effectiveness of digital adaptation. The study concludes that digital leadership has become an essential framework for managing crises, enhancing institutional agility, and ensuring the sustainability of higher education in the digital era. It recommends continued investment in digital infrastructure, leadership development, and the institutionalization of digital leadership practices across universities.

title: MedViT: A Vision Transformer-Based Model for Kidney Disease Detection
authors: P H T Mandhika, S Sotheeswaran
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Kidney disease remains a major global health challenge, where early and accurate diagnosis is critical for effective treatment. However, conventional diagnostic methods are often time-consuming and subject to interpretive variability, highlighting the need for automated, reliable alternatives. This study investigates the potential of transformer-based deep learning models for automated kidney disease detection, utilizing a publicly available Kaggle dataset comprising four categories: normal, cyst, tumour, and stone. We propose a MedViT transformer model-a hybrid architecture that integrates convolutional layers with transformer blocks to efficiently capture both local and global features in medical images. This strategic combination within a unified architecture enables efficient capture of fine-grained details and long-range dependencies in medical images, delivering robust classification performance across various diagnostic tasks. The model was evaluated using a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0}$</tex>-fold cross-validation scheme, achieving a mean validation accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$99.66 \% (\pm 0.0036)$</tex> and a validation loss of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.0109 (\pm 0.0120)$</tex>, demonstrating consistent robustness across all folds. Class-wise evaluation yielded high precision, recall, and F1-scores, confirming the model's ability to reliably distinguish among cysts, stones, tumours, and normal tissue with minimal variability. Comparative analysis against other transformer architectures-including ViT, Swin, and Bi-Former-shows that MedViT delivers comparable accuracy while offering notable improvements in model stability and generalization. Additionally, data augmentation was employed to enhance generalization, the dataset is limited to CT images from a single source, which may restrict the model's performance in real-world clinical settings. Future work will explore advanced augmentation strategies, multimodal data integration, and enhanced attention mechanisms to further improve classification reliability and clinical applicability.

title: Real Time Sinhala Lecture Transcription for Political Science
authors: G.W.A.J.C. Prabhashwara, W.A.C. Weerakoon
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: This research aims to develop an optimized automatic speech recognition system for transcribing Sinhala language lectures in the subject of Political Science. Current Automatic Speech Recognition systems, designed to work with any type of audio, often do not perform well in capturing domain-specific vocabulary; therefore, the models currently available will not provide robust transcription for lectures presented in the field of Political Science. The research executed a comparison study of Automatic Speech Recognition frameworks and chose the Wav2Vec2-XLS-R-300M model as the reference point because of its good equilibrium of precision and computational efficiency on CPU hardware. A significant gap in performance was identified when comparing the model's results to a domain-specific dataset, leading to a multi-staged improvement effort to close that gap. This approach consisted of adjusting the acoustic model for a specific Political Science dataset along with the addition of a 5-gram KenLM language model for phonetic error correction as a result of decoding. Following thorough refinement of the system's decoding parameters and the configuration for beam search, the system's transcription accuracy increased significantly. Ultimately, the final optimized system produced an average Word Error Rate of approximately 10.58% and an average Real Time Factor of 0.85, indicating that this Automatic Speech Recognition system is suitable for use in live academic environments.

title: A Hybrid Machine-Learning-Based Framework for Geometry-Temperature-Aware General Compact Model Parameter Extraction
authors: Wenrui Zhang, Shuhan Wang, Zheng Zhou, Fei Liu, Jinfeng Kang, Xiaoyan Liu
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: This work proposes a MAR (Masked-Autoencoder-Regressor) algorithm for the semiconductor device compact model parameter extraction. This algorithm architecture aims to address challenges in compact model parameter extraction for real devices characteristics measuring from experiment and it also can be used as general model parameter extractor. The algorithm architecture consists of data mask preprocessing and a self-normalized autoencoder-regressor hybrid parameter extraction model. This algorithm was verified by extracting the DSOI MOSFETs’ model parameters under various conditions, including multiple device’s sizes, multiple temperatures, and different backgate biases. The final results show that the error under all conditions is below 10%, meeting industry requirements for compact model parameter extraction.

title: MQTT Routing Optimizing based Intrusion Detection for Internet of Things Leveraging Hybrid Deep Learning Model
authors: Haider K. Hoomod, Mohammad Alaa Al-Hamami, Wasan Alaa Al-Hamami, Aqeel Kamil Kadhim, Jolan Rokan Niaf, Qutaiba Humadi Mohammed
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: As the Internet of Things dawned, a new universe unfolded where every object could be distinctly identified, monitored, and orchestrated by the unseen forces of computer programs. These objects, akin to sentient beings, autonomously configure themselves through a predefined communication protocol, while other software programs synchronize seamlessly via MQTT or MQSeries components, creating a digital symphony. This domain, often referred to as business integration software and middleware, is where the MQTT protocol excels with its lightweight, easy-to-implement, and low-bandwidth system, opening up a world of possibilities, including the captivating realm of home automation. In our pursuit of enhancing power efficiency in MQ-service routing, we introduced an intelligent optimization model for MQTT routing by decreasing the intrusion network attacks. Tailored for the Internet of Things (IoT), this model unveils an optimized routing framework based on a two of hybrid deep learning-based anomaly detection models (CLGM). It enables IoT devices to extend their network reach while conserving energy with the precision of a seasoned athlete. To further refine IDS identification and achieve optimized network selection routes, we utilized two datasets (DNN-EdgeIIoT and NF-UNSW-NB15-v2). This model acts as a vigilant guardian, adept at detecting attacks that lead to network congestion, causing delays in connection response and data transfer within the IoT network.

title: Designing an Intelligent Protection Scheme for Solar Power Plants Based on Power Prediction and Asymmetrical Fault Detection
authors: Nima Kadivaryan, Milad Jaliliyan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Protection and Automation of Power Systems (IPAPS)
abstract: In recent years, the increasing penetration of solar power plants into distribution networks has posed new challenges to the performance of protective relays. The limited current injection behavior of inverters, rapid power fluctuations, and the occurrence of asymmetrical faults make digital relay settings unreliable, especially under conditions such as reverse power flow and high-impedance faults. This research proposes an adaptive protection scheme for a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 M W}$</tex> solar power plant in Samangan-e-Sahneh, which is based on a combination of short-term power output prediction and negative sequence component analysis. The proposed adaptive logic is able to synchronize relay settings with the operating conditions of the network in real-time and distinguish between actual faults, transient fluctuations, and reverse power flow phenomena. The system modeling and simulation were performed in PowerFactory software based on actual data for the transformer, lines, relays, and the power profile of the power plant. The results show that the proposed method increases the speed of fault detection, reduces unnecessary tripping due to reverse power flow, and significantly improves accuracy in identifying high-impedance faults. This protective structure can be implemented in Iranian distribution networks without requiring additional hardware, by utilizing the capabilities of existing digital relays.

title: AI-Assisted IoT Framework for Water Quality Monitoring with Real-Time WQI Prediction and Cleaning Forecasting
authors: Tushar Sadanand Alande, Varsha K. Harpale, Aryan Anand Bharaswadkar, Siddhant Shantkumar Babanagar
publish: 2026 IEEE Bangalore Humanitarian Technology Conference (B-HTC)
abstract: Public health depends on the quality of their drinking water. But continuous access to systems that monitor quality of drinking water is not readily available widely. The common quality checking methods are based primarily on laboratory testing and manual sample collection. This is time consuming and does not ensure precise data. This leads to poor planning of maintenance and slow reaction to the contamination. To address these issues, we propose an IoT framework featuring a dual-node architecture that monitors water quality at the input and output nods of the storage system for increased assessment reliability. The system monitors the real-time values of necessary water parameters like pH, TDS, turbidity and electrical conductivity. The proposed framework uses an SVR model for predicting realtime WQI value and predictive scheduling of water tank cleaning. This combined monitoring-and-forecasting approach makes it suitable for optimal maintenance planning of water storage tanks. The results and real-time tracking are easily accessible using a mobile application and a web dashboard for remote supervision. The proposed solution provides a low-cost, scalable, and intelligent approach that is suitable for both urban and rural water storage environments.

title: Personalized Tabular Transformer with Explainable AI for Mental Health Assessment
authors: Amit Hasan Sikder, Sumya Akter, Md Amir Hamja, Md. Iftekhar Hossain Tushar, Rokaiya Tasnim, Mahmudul Hasan
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: In technology workforce the mental health issues are continuously growing and impacting both employee wellbeing and organizational productivity. This study presents a predictive framework for mental health assessment using a publicly available dataset from Kaggle. A preprocessing pipeline is developed to handle missing values, normalize categorical responses, and construct composite indicators. Various ML models (LR, RF, GB, SVM, NB, DT, KNN) and DL models (MLP, LSTM, BiLSTM, TabT) are utilized, however results show only moderate performance (accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx \mathbf{7 0}-\mathbf{7 3} \boldsymbol{\%}, \mathbf{F 1} \approx \mathbf{0. 6 7}-\mathbf{0. 7 2}$</tex>) due to weak correlations in self-reported features. To mitigate this, we propose the Personalized Tabular Transformer (PTabT), which integrates feature specific embeddings, attention pooling, and token level personalization to capture heterogeneous feature interactions. PTabT achieves superior performance over others with 0.86 accuracy with minimal variance (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 0.02$</tex>) in cross validation, F1score of 0.86, and Cohen's Kappa of 0.70. Beyond prediction, both levels of explainability are emphasized, with SHAP used for global and LIME for local interpretation. SHAP identified family_history, work_interfere, care_options, and benefits as the most influential global predictors, while LIME highlighted how protective and risk factors balance differently across individuals. These findings demonstrate that PTabT, combined with XAI, provides both reliable predictions and interpretable insights, supporting proactive decision making for HR professionals and mental health practitioners.

title: Electronic Nose-Driven Classification of Sri Lankan Teas: An Ensemble Learning Approach Based on Aroma Profiles
authors: Bhashitha Kaveesh, Thosini Kumarika, Dakshika Wanniarachchi, Tharaga Sharmilan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Geographical authentication of tea is critical for quality assurance, brand protection, and fraud prevention in the global tea market. However, conventional approaches based on sensory evaluation and chemical analysis are often subjective, labor-intensive, time-consuming, and costly, limiting their scalability for routine authentication. This study proposes an integrated electronic nose (E-Nose) and machine learning framework for the geographical origin classification of Sri Lankan tea using aroma profiles. Aroma data were collected from 367 dry tea samples representing seven major tea-growing regions using a custom-developed metal oxide semiconductor (MOS)-based Digi-Nose device equipped with seven gas sensors. The acquired sensor responses were digitally recorded and structured for supervised learning analysis. To ensure reliable and unbiased model training, the dataset was preprocessed and balanced prior to classification. Eight supervised machine learning models, such as ensemble-based (Extra Trees, Random Forest, XGBoost, CatBoost, LightGBM), distance-based (K-Nearest Neighbors), kernel-based (Support Vector Machine), and neural network-based (Multi-Layer Perceptron) approaches, were experimentally evaluated. Model performance was assessed using multiple metrics, including accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, confusion matrices, learning curve analysis, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0}$</tex>-fold cross-validation to verify robustness and generalization. Among all evaluated models, the Extra Trees classifier demonstrated superior performance, achieving an overall classification accuracy of 98.63 %, along with consistently high precision and recall across all tea regions. The findings confirm that the integration of MOS-based E-Nose sensing with ensemble learning techniques provides a reliable, non-destructive, and deployment-ready solution for aroma-based geographical authentication of tea, with strong potential for real-world industrial and regulatory applications.

title: Dataset for DDoS Detection in 5G-Enabled Connected and Autonomous Vehicle Systems
authors: Mirza Akhi, Md. Shalha Mucha Bhuiyan, Alfonso Ariza, Lubna Luxmi Dhirani
publish: 2026 Global Conference on Wireless and Optical Technologies (GCWOT)
abstract: The intelligent Autonomous Vehicles (AVs) rely on 5G connectivity to exchange positional data, perception outputs, and control commands in real time. This communication enables coordinated motion and safety-critical response. However, it also increases the environment's exposure to Distributed Denial-ofService (DDoS) attacks, disrupting message flow and impairing operational safety. To investigate this risk, a simulated driving-and-DDoS-attack scenario is developed in the CARLA simulator under a 5 G -enabled AV setting. This setup yields the UL-ECE-5G-AV-DDoS2025 dataset, which contains regular driving traffic and DDoS attack traffic. The dataset includes Global Positioning System (GPS) coordinates, speed, acceleration, throttle, steering, braking values, and network-layer metrics. Hence, this UL-ECE-5G-AV-DDoS2025 dataset supports reproducible benchmarking for Generative-AI (Gen-AI), machine learning, deep learning, and traffic-flow analysis in 5 G -connected transport environments.

title: DABBiT: A Drift-Adaptive Behavioral Biometrics Framework for Continuous Authentication in Tanzanian Internet Banking
authors: Festus Edward Ndalama, Yuzo Taenaka, Patrick Mutabazi, Youki Kadobayashi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: Tanzania’s internet banking sector has seen rapid growth but also an 84% rise in digital fraud, exposing the weakness of static authentication. Behavioral biometrics, which are statistical patterns in how a user interacts with a device (e.g., keystroke dynamics and mouse movements), provide continuous verification but suffer from concept drift, where user behavior changes over time or under attack. We present DABBiT (DriftAdaptive Behavioral Biometrics Framework), a system that integrates anomaly-based drift detection with adaptive classifiers to sustain authentication accuracy. Natural concept drift was evaluated using the Probabilistic Concept Drift Measure (PCDM), revealing moderate stability in keystrokes ($\mathbf{8 8 9}$ drifts; false alarm rate (FAR)=4.45 %, recovery accuracy (RA)=2.20 %, average error rate (AER)=9.01% but high volatility in mouse dynamics (8,054 drifts; FAR=3.19 %, RA=1.48 %, AER=26.40%). Adversarial drift simulations showed that the adaptive Hoeffding Adaptive Tree (HAT) and strong non-adaptive models, namely k-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) and eXtreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost), consistently outperformed a static Support Vector Machine (SVM) baseline; under drift, keystrokes achieved accuracy above 0.98 (false rejection rate (FRR) ≈ 4-7%) with faster recovery for HAT, while mouse dynamics incurred longer adaptation delays (up to $\sim 250,000$ samples) and higher error rates. A survey of 107 Tanzanian users highlighted strong privacy concerns (43%) but clear support (83 %) for multi-factor authentication. These findings demonstrate that drift-aware biometrics not only detect and adapt to evolving behaviors but also enhance fraud resilience while aligning with user expectations in high-risk financial environments.

title: Benchmarking ML and DL Models for Bangla Sentiment Analysis in Student Movement
authors: Chayti Saha, Zannatul Mawa Koli, Dristi Saha, Yadab Sutradrar, Md Fayejullah, Umme Ayman
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Sentiment analysis of social movements offers important insights into public opinion, collective feelings, and speech that is motivated by equality. Nonetheless, although being spoken by more than 230 million people, Bangla is still underrepresented in Natural Language Processing (NLP), particularly when it comes to scenarios including activism. We provide Echoes of Equity, a manually curated Bangla sentiment analysis dataset centered on Bangladesh's Anti-Discrimination Student Movement, in order to fill this vacuum. The dataset is a multilingual resource for scholars, comprising <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3, 1 6 2}$</tex> statements that are categorized as positive, negative, or neutral and accompanied by English translations. Using this dataset, we evaluate a variety of deep learning (ANN, BiLSTM) and machine learning (Naïve Bayes, Logistic Regression, SVM) models. The findings indicate that while neural architectures, especially BiLSTM, enhance classification to 79%, they have trouble with neutral and context-dependent words, whereas classic machine learning techniques achieve competitive performance (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$73-76 {\%}$</tex> accuracy). This paper illustrates the function of natural language processing (NLP) in computational social science, provides a new dataset and baseline assessments, and provides tools to better understand how collective emotions are expressed in activist movements. Echoes of Equity establishes the groundwork for future studies on transformer-based sentiment models, fairness-aware AI, and cross-lingual activism by making it possible to systematically analyze texts pertaining to activism in Bangla.

title: Explainable Fake News Detection in Bengali via LLM-Guided Hybrid Representations
authors: Tania Sultana Tamanna, Jerin Sultana, Tasnim Fateha, Farjana Akter Tandra, Md Mahbubur Rahman
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Detecting fake news on social media is particularly challenging for low-resource languages like Bengali, where annotated data and pretrained tools are scarce. Existing approaches are generally extracting local features (e.g., TF-IDF, CNN) or global semantics (e.g., BERT), but rarely their combination while being interpretable. We propose an explainable two-stage framework for Bengali fake news detection. In the first stage, an LLaMA3 module gives formalized explanations (analysis, indicators, conclusion) of every article. These are embedded with Bangla-BERT to yield semantic vectors. In the second stage, the explanation embeddings are fused with TF-IDF and CNN-based local features into a hybrid representation, allowing the model to learn both the content and its rationale. Evaluated on BanFakeNews and BFRD, our system achieves state-of-the-art performance (95.3% accuracy on BanFakeNews) while providing interpretable outputs. The system is suitable for real-time tasks in low-resource settings that are sensitive to misinformation.

title: Investigation of Coupled RRAM and MOSFET Degradation in 1T1R Memory Cell
authors: Yuhang Yang, Yingchen Ji, Ruiqing Xie, Lin Bao, Zongwei Wang, Yimao Cai
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: Resistive random-access memory (RRAM) implemented in one-transistor–one-resistor (1T1R) cells has been adopted in system-on-chips (SoCs) across multiple technology nodes (40 nm, 28 nm, and 22 nm) for high-density non-volatile memory. However, reliability becomes increasingly critical due to the coupled degradation of the RRAM and the access transistor, particularly in advanced nodes. Existing studies typically evaluate RRAM endurance/retention and MOSFET hot-carrier injection (HCI) degradation independently, overlooking their synergistic interaction in 1T1R cells. In practice, RRAM resistance drift during cycling amplifies the stress on the MOSFET, forming a degradation-accelerating positive feedback loop. Based on fabricated 28 nm TaO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</inf>-based 1T1R arrays, this work systematically characterizes standalone RRAM (linear Set/Reset voltage drift) and MOSFET HCI (power-law current degradation), and establishes a coupled degradation model. The proposed model accurately predicts accelerated transistor degradation under time-varying stress and enables co-design optimization, such as MOSFET gate-width tuning, to meet specific end-of-life power and reliability targets—providing a crucial framework for robust, high-density 1T1R memory integration.

title: Beyond Binary Alerts: A Multi-Faceted Framework for Clinically Actionable Sepsis Prediction
authors: Fariha Jahan, Nusrat Hossain, Rashedul Arefin Ifty
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Sepsis is still a leading cause of death in intensive care units (ICUs), where prompt intervention depends on early and precise detection. The preemptive utility of current machine learning models is limited because they frequently generate binary results or only identify sepsis at the time of clinical diagnosis. This work addresses key research gaps by developing and validating a multi-faceted framework for early sepsis prediction. A rigorous comparative analysis of four predictive models—Gated Recurrent Unit, Transformer, LightGBM, and Logistic Regression was conducted on the PhysioNet/CinC Challenge 2019 dataset. The models are tasked with predicting sepsis onset up to six hours in advance. A thorough clinical utility analysis is then conducted on the top-performing architecture, a GRU. This includes an unsupervised clustering pipeline to find clinically significant sub-phenotypes and the creation of continuous dynamic risk scores to track patient trajectories. Furthermore, an alarm fatigue analysis demonstrates that the model can achieve over 95% sensitivity while generating fewer than one false alarm per patient-day. With an area under the ROC curve of 0.995, the GRU model significantly outperforms the baselines. These results establish that the proposed framework provides a robust and clinically actionable decision-support tool that enhances predictive performance, interpretability, and practical usability for early sepsis management.

title: Dual-Band Slot-Loaded Microstrip Patch Antenna for 5G mmWave and Ka-Band Applications
authors: Mehlaqa, Md. Farhad Hossain, Debprosad Das, Md. Azad Hossain
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: This paper presents a dual-band slot-loaded microstrip patch antenna designed for 5 G millimeter-wave (mmWave) and satellite Ka-band applications. The antenna employs a quarter-wave transformer for effective impedance matching and integrates two F-shaped slots and one I-shaped slot on the radiating patch to realize dual resonances. The proposed design achieves resonant frequencies at 33.8 GHz and 45.53 GHz with reflection coefficients of -36.39 dB and -42.68 dB, respectively. It achieves fractional bandwidths of 6.78% and 9.14%, and peak gains of 4.59 dBi and 7.32 dBi. Radiation efficiencies of 76.2% and 79.34% confirm its effectiveness for high-frequency operation. Equivalent RLC circuit validation further supports the dual-band characteristics. Owing to its wide bandwidth, reliable gain, and stable radiation performance, the proposed antenna is well-suited for integration into nextgeneration high-speed communication systems.

title: On the Energy Consumption of Continuous Integration in Open-Source Java Projects
authors: Robert Arntzenius, Xutong Liu, Andy Zaidman
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Software Analysis, Evolution and Reengineering - Companion (SANER-C)
abstract: Continuous integration is essential for software quality, yet the energy footprint associated with its frequent execution has largely remained invisible. We provide the first comprehensive baseline of CI energy use through a largescale study of 204 open-source Java projects with repeated measurements under Maven and Gradle. Our results show that energy use is highly skewed: while most projects consume energy modestly, a small number of “CI-intensive” systems can reach annual CI energy footprints of hundreds of kilowatt-hours, which is comparable to a quarter of an average EU household's electricity use. We further show that immediate, practical savings are possible: simply enabling dependency caching cuts energy by 30 % on average in some Maven projects and by over 90% in some Gradle cases. These findings matter not only for individual developers, but also for large organizations that run thousands of builds. In those settings, even small inefficiencies can add up to very large energy costs. By exposing where energy is consumed and how to reduce it, our study establishes an actionable foundation for greener CI pipelines.

title: Position-Aware Beam Steering and Antenna Adaptation Using LiDAR Sensing for 6G Wireless Links
authors: Praveen Kumar Sharma, Mitesh Solanki, Rashmi Jangid, Tapan Nahar, Chandrasinh Parmar
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: Wireless communication systems are rapidly moving towards 6G networks, which provide ultra-reliable, high-speed, and low-latency connectivity, however signal degradation, interference, and physical barriers at high frequencies cause serious issues in urban areas. The proposed research integrates LiDAR (Light Detection and Ranging) technology with beamforming techniques to enable dynamic beam steering, optimize antenna positioning, and improve line-of-sight (LoS) predictions. An improvement in the signal to noise ratio and coverage has been shown with MATLAB simulations and the obtained results have been validated with the hardware implementation through the ESP32 microcontroller that is interfaced with a VL53L0X LiDAR sensor. The proposed approach has been very beneficial for the antenna positioning and beamforming optimization for applications such as smart cities and autonomous vehicles as it is improving the network connectivity in the extremely populated urban environments.

title: Classifying Lemon Leaf Diseases Using MobileNetV3-Large for Better Crop Health Monitoring
authors: Tanya Gera, Noopur Tyagi, Sharnjeet Kaur, Sonam Jain, Diya Agnihotri, Ashish Kumar Singh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This research explores the use of the MobileNetV3-Large model in classifying five diseases in lemons: Anthracnose, Citrus Canker, Deficiency Leaf, Healthy Leaf, and Spider Mites. It worked with a dataset of 795 images, with a testing set of 20% and a training set of 80%. Preprocessing steps like grayscale, resizing, Gaussian filtering, and histogram equalization were applied to enhance features and improve performance. The model was trained for 20 epochs and achieved a total test set accuracy of 86%. Precision, recall, and F1-scores indicated balanced performance with Spider Mites and Healthy Leaf being most commonly misclassified. From the confusion matrix, it was established that the model correctly classified most diseases with minimal errors in Citrus Canker and Deficiency Leaf. This study demonstrates that deep learning, in this instance MobileNetV3-Large, can effectively be deployed in automated disease classification in lemons and can be a valuable tool in monitoring and managing diseases in agriculture.

title: Steering the Beam of Smallest Aperture Horn Using Metallic Metasurfaces
authors: Foez Ahmed
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The miniature phase gradient metallic metasurfaces (MMs) are presented to steer the beam of the smallest aperture horn antenna. The near-field phase transformation theory is used in the construction of the MMs to generate the necessary phase gradients at 12.5 GHz. The MMs are designed from low-cost, off-the-shelf thin copper sheets using a slot-based design approach. The lack of dielectrics in the design lowers the cost and allows them to be used in extreme environments, like high-power and space applications. A commercial electromagnetic simulator is used to model the antenna and verify its beam-steering concept numerically. Excellent beam steering performance is indicated by this preliminary investigation. With MM1, the beam can be steered at 43° as expected. When MM1 is fixed and MM2 is rotated at an angle of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$180^{\circ}, 90^{\circ}$</tex> and 80°, the beam-steering angles in the elevation plane are <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0^{\circ}, 55^{\circ}$</tex> and 42° at 12.5 GHz. Each MM is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.77 \lambda_{0}$</tex> thick and overall antenna profile is only <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.77 \lambda_{0}$</tex>.

title: Resilient Communication Hubs Over LEO Satellite Networks
authors: Yue Cui, Tian Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Satellite Internet (SAT-NET)
abstract: In severe hazardous situations, reliable network communication is essential for conveying vital, real-time information from field-deployed sensors and devices. Critical communications must be received quickly to support life-saving decisions and situational awareness, yet maintaining continuous connectivity is challenging when terrestrial infrastructure is impaired. To ensure seamless, fault-tolerant, and secure communication, we propose a new system: Resilient Communication Hubs (RC Hubs) over Low-Earth-Orbit (LEO) satellite networks. In this architecture, reliability is improved by using Multi-Path TCP (MPTCP) across diverse satellite paths and optional terrestrial fallbacks, maintaining end-to-end connection semantics while rapidly recovering from subflow failures. LEO satellite networks provides beyond-line-of-sight coverage but network transmission can be expensive, so the hub supports cost-aware path selection and policy controls to minimize satellite airtime when alternatives exist. The system dynamically selects an edge node as the active satellite gateway, and MPTCP preserves session continuity across gateway switches by maintaining parallel subflows through the old and new gateways. We have implemented the RC hub with integrated LEO terminals and conducted experiments under varied mobility and impairment conditions. Results show that the proposed architecture is rapidly deployable and sustains reliable communications despite partial failure or loss of terrestrial infrastructure.

title: Comparative Analysis of Configurable Logic Block Using Static CMOS, Transmission Gate and Gate Diffusion Input Techniques
authors: Md. Mahidul Haque, Md. Mahfuzur Rashid, Saquib Farhan, Md Tawfiq Amin
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Configurable Logic Block (CLB) is the core building element that determines the overall speed, power and area of a device. Conventional CLB implemented using Static CMOS logic offer robustness and reliability yet they occupy larger silicon area and consume more energy which limits efficiency in low power applications. The objective of this research work is to explore alternative logic styles for CLBs and to evaluate their impact on key performance metrics. The study investigates Static CMOS logic, Transmission Gate Logic (TGL) and Gate Diffusion Input (GDI) logic using a 90 nm process in Cadence Virtuoso with schematic design, layout implementation and post layout simulation analysis. Metrics such as state transition delay, setup time, clock to Q delay, maximum operating frequency, layout area, energy per cycle, and a composite figure of merit are analyzed. The results show that Static CMOS delivers the lowest delay of 55.49 ps but requires the largest area of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2176.52 \mu ~\mathrm{m}^{2}$</tex> and the highest energy of 171.6 fJ. By contrast, TG Logic reduces the area to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2025.08 \mu ~\mathrm{m}^{2}$</tex>, provides moderate delay, and achieves the lowest energy consumption at 139.1 fJ, while GDI offers the most compact design with a minimal area of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$804.824 \mu ~\mathrm{m}^{2}$</tex>. The findings highlight the trade offs among logic styles and provide guidelines for CLB design in future FPGAs where speed, area, and energy efficiency are critical.

title: Team Synergy-Aware Workforce Scheduling: Integrating Interpersonal Compatibility and Endogenous Turnover Dynamics
authors: Aijia Liu, Chaeeun Hong, Fumin Chang, Shizuka Ebisudani, Sejun Woo, Kiyotaka Shin, Hiroshi Takahashi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: In the labor-intensive service industry, workforce stability is paramount for sustaining competitive advantage, yet traditional scheduling models predominantly focus on cost minimization and demand coverage, neglecting the critical social dimension of employee-employee synergy. This oversight frequently creates a vicious cycle of dissatisfaction, turnover, and the subsequent erosion of service quality. Addressing this deficiency, this study proposes an extended optimization framework that quantitatively evaluates the impact of interpersonal compatibility on long-term organizational viability. A distinguishing feature of our approach is the endogenous modeling of workforce dynamics: we introduce a stochastic affinity generation mechanism to model realistic personality interactions and an endogenous turnover function that explicitly links cumulative team incompatibility to the attrition of experienced staff. Through rigorous multi-period dynamic simulations, we demonstrate that a synergy-prioritized strategy effectively disrupts the cycle of skill drain, maintaining superior service quality compared to traditional baseline methods. The quantitative results reveal significant revenue divergence between the two strategies, providing compelling evidence that optimizing for measurable team synergy yields substantial long-term financial benefits and transforms team composition from a mere operational constraint into a strategic asset for sustainability.

title: Cyber Physical Hardening of BLE Microsensor Systems Through Systematic VAPT and IDS Based Mitigation
authors: Ramya K
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
