abstract: BLE enabled microsensor platforms are increasingly integrated into cyber physical systems (CPS), yet their constrained architectures and wireless interfaces introduce cross-layer attack surfaces. This work presents a unified Vulnerability Assessment and Penetration Testing (VAPT) framework that evaluates the sensing element, embedded controller, and BLE telemetry as a single cyber-physical chain. A custom inkjet-printed electrochemical sensor interfaced with an ESP32-S3 BLE module is used as the experimental system and subjected to firmware, physical, and wireless-layer attacks. Results indicate that environmental and EMI disturbances induced up to ±23% variation in sensor output without triggering alarms, while controlled signal injection produced 100% acceptance of artificial values by the ADC. Firmware extraction and modification were successful via unsecured bootloader access in under 40 seconds, and BLE spoofing achieved unauthorized connection with a 92% success rate. Replay attacks consistently bypassed freshness validation with 100% packet acceptance, while BLE fuzzing induced intermittent instability in 31% of test cycles. Mitigations including secure boot, authenticated firmware updates, BLE encryption, and hardened pairing were applied, after which replay and spoofing attempts were reduced to 0% success, and firmware modification was fully blocked. These results demonstrate the need for holistic cyber-physical hardening of BLE microsensors. The proposed framework provides a repeatable methodology for securing microsensor CPS deployments.

title: Unveiling Gate Leakage Dynamics in Nanosheet FETs at Cryogenic Temperature: A TCAD Study
authors: Prabhat Singh, Malvika, Mohd. Shakir, Sandeep Kumar, Sunil Rathore, Navjeet Bagga, S. Dasgupta
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: In this paper, we investigate the gate-leakage mechanism at cryogenic temperature (CT) in the Nanosheet FET (NSFET) using well-calibrated TCAD models. Using the Global TCAD Solution (GTS) tool, the carrier transport at cryogenic temperatures could be precisely captured by the Quasi-Fermi Transport (QFT) model. However, for evaluating gate leakage or OFF current, two possible models are available, i.e., the Tsu-Esaki and Fowler-Nordheim models, which require careful attention for accurate simulation. Thus, this study aims to explore the underlying behavior of the Cryo-NSFET characteristics with an appropriate modeling setup. As the temperature decreases to a deep-cryogenic range, the Fermi-Dirac distribution, i.e., the Fermi tail, becomes narrower, implying that fewer carriers have high energy, and thermal-assisted tunneling decreases. This results in a reduction in gate leakage current at all gate voltages. Our findings indicate that under CT, the absence of thermal excitation results in a shift in the transport mechanism to quantum tunneling. Additionally, gate tunneling is enhanced by a higher drain bias, which allows for the control of the potential barrier profile.

title: Proposed Framework of Sustainable Value Co-Creation in Agricultural Supply Chain: A Bibliometric Perspective
authors: Masayu Rosyidah, Elisa Kusrini, Widodo Brontowiyono
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Sustainable value co-creation (SVC) has emerged as a strategic approach for enhancing resilience and sustainability within agricultural supply chains. Drawing on a bibliometric review of 366 Scopus-indexed journal articles published between 2012 and 2025, this study identifies four major thematic clusters and 44 high-frequency keywords that shape the intellectual structure of SVC research in agriculture. Descriptive trends reveal a sharp increase in publication activity after 2019, reflecting growing academic and practical attention to integrating social, environmental, and digital dimensions in agri-food systems. The bibliographic coupling analysis (threshold: Total Link Strength = 26) highlights four key clusters—digital collaboration and agri-food innovation, cooperative-driven sustainability, circular innovation and industry 4.0, and IoT-enabled co-creation—that collectively represent the evolving research landscape. Based on this synthesis, a conceptual framework is proposed integrating Green Intellectual Capital (GIC), Green Transformational Leadership (GTL), Digital Transformation (DT), and Dynamic Capabilities (DC) as critical antecedents of SVC. This framework advances the Resource-Based View (RBV), Service-Dominant Logic (SDL), and Dynamic Capabilities (DC) theory, emphasizing how green resources, leadership, and digital enablers foster adaptive, multi-actor collaboration for sustainability. In practical terms, it offers guidance for agricultural stakeholders to strengthen knowledge-based resources, embrace digital transformation, and enhance adaptive capabilities to achieve sustainable performance aligned with SDG 8, SDG 9, SDG 12, and SDG 13.

title: The Role of Artificial Intelligence in Enhancing Decision-Making Processes
authors: Ammar Salamh Alrawahna, Hasan Alhanatleh, Hussain Awad, Amro Alzghoul, Imad Al Muala
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: The adoption of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in decision-making processes has revolutionized organizational operations across all industries, facilitating more informed, efficient, and precise decision-making. This document analyzes the role of AI in improving decision-making, emphasizing its applicability, advantages, and related ethical implications. Artificial intelligence methodologies, including machine learning, natural language processing, and reinforcement learning, provide many decision-making approaches, ranging from aided to entirely automated systems. These methods enhance operations by facilitating real-time data analysis, limiting human error, and improving resource allocation, resulting in substantial cost savings and increased operational efficiency. The integration of AI in decision-making presents ethical obstacles, such as algorithmic bias, transparency, and data privacy issues, which must be resolved to guarantee equitable and responsible AI implementation. The future of artificial intelligence in decision-making is contingent upon upcoming technologies such as quantum computing and edge AI, which provide accelerated and more advanced data processing capabilities. Moreover, a collaborative framework that integrates human creativity with AI's analytical prowess highlights the necessity of ethical governance to address the intricacies of AI-driven decision-making. This synthesis utilizes recent research (2020-2025) that emphasizes AI's significant influence on decision-making and highlights the necessity for ethical supervision to optimize its potential while preserving public confidence and accountability.

title: Anomaly Detection Approach for Remote Beehive Health Assessment via Environmental Patterns
authors: Nisal Liyanage, T. A. H. Dilpriya
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: This study presents the use of an anomaly detection approach for remote beehive health assessment using temperature and humidity data collected from sensors placed inside and outside of a beehive. A One-Class Support Vector Machine (SVM) was trained exclusively on data samples recorded when the hive was in a healthy condition, enabling the model to learn colony- specific environmental regulation patterns and detect deviations that may indicate potential health issues. The study adopts an individualized modeling strategy, where a separate health assessment model is trained for each hive using its own healthy samples. This hive-specific approach captures each colony's unique environmental behavior and avoids the overgeneralization limitations of threshold-based methods introduced in previous literature. The trained model was deployed through a Flask-based server and integrated with a mobile application to provide real-time hive health assessments. Experimental evaluation showed that a tuned decision threshold reduced false alarms to approximately 1 % on healthy data while reliably detecting artificially induced unhealthy conditions, with all anomaly samples falling below the optimized threshold. These results confirm that the proposed framework offers an accurate, lightweight, and practical solution for real-time hive health monitoring, supporting proactive and scalable precision apiculture.

title: LED-Tuned HR-Si Monopole Antenna for K-Band Reconfiguration
authors: Fatma Elhouni, Yi Huang, Yaochun Shen
publish: 2026 IEEE International Workshop on Antenna Technology (iWAT)
abstract: This paper presents a photoconductivity reconfigurable K-band monopole antenna based on high-resistivity silicon (HR-Si), optically controlled via high-power LED illumination. Using a material-informed model and measured optical intensity from high power LED, we estimate a photoconductivity range of 20–100 S/m. A 1.5 mm HR-Si monopole exhibits a resonant frequency shift from 27 GHz to 24 GHz as conductivity increases. This behavior is attributed to changes in effective permittivity <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\varepsilon_{\text{eff}}$</tex>, which alters the guided wavelength and impedance matching. A photoconductive model is used to capture responses of the photoconductive layer. Simulated results improve from S11-3dB to −6dB across the conductivity range. To confine LED illumination to the active region and prevent parasitic photoconductive activation of the surrounding HR-Si wafer a contact optical mask is proposed, and future extensions include pixel-level optical reconfiguration using a DLP projector. This work demonstrates a compact, bias-free approach to tunable silicon antennas driven by optical intensity alone.

title: A scalable neuromorphic platform with four neural motifs featuring <1 ms Per-Neuron Latency
authors: Xiaolong Ma, Ziye Di, Jing Tian, Xianghong Zhang, Shuiyuan Wang, Peng Zhou
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: The first electronic neural circuit motifs are demonstrated by integrating a 1 KB multi-gate 2D ferroelectric floating-gate transistor (MG 2D FeFGT) synapse array (20 ns switching, yield of ~99.6%) with leaky-integrate-and-fire (LIF) neuron circuits. The architecture combines four fundamental neural motifs (feedforward excitation/inhibition, feedback inhibition, mutual inhibition) and exhibits <1 ms neuron signal processing latency (NeuroSP Latency, approaching biological timescales), feature extraction, dynamic gain, and rhythm regulation. Devices show excellent uniformity (>6-bit weight states) and robust operation from -25 to 85 ℃. This work establishes a scalable platform for bionic neuromorphic systems with biological-timescale signal processing capabilities.

title: Rain Streak Removal: A Streamlined Hierarchical CNN-Based Approach for Image Enhancement
authors: Nushrat Tarmin Meem, Sk. Md. Masudul Ahsan
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Rain streaks can potentially degrade the image quality acquired during outdoor scenes and lead to poor visibility and low performance of downstream vision tasks. The rain removal task aims at recovering clean images by effectively eliminating rain artifacts and thereby improving visual quality and ensuring the reliable operation of vision-based systems in adverse weather conditions. On this basis, this paper has proposed a new Hierarchical CNN-based ResNet architecture for image de-raining that generalizes a weakly supervised data distillation approach to improve the performance in smalldata scenarios. The first-stage encoding network can effectively capture spatial and contextual features with eight dilated convolutional blocks with residual connections and a two-layered convolutional sub-network for feature transformation within each block. To fight against data scarcity, wanted rainy-clean image pairs were created by retrieving real rain streaks from real-world rainy images, applying geometric augmentations and merging them with synthetic rain patterns to create realistic and diverse training samples. This approach enables better generalization while being data-efficient. Real-world rain generation enhances the model's ability in handling natural rainy images significantly. The performance of the model is increased with PSNR and SSIM rising from 25.72 to 26.41 and 0.78 to 0.82, respectively. The approach achieves high computational efficiency with per-image times of 0.027 seconds (CPU) and 0.006 seconds (GPU).

title: Dual-Band Dual-Polarized Shared Aperture Traveling-Wave Antenna Using Hybrid Dual-Mode Floquet Excitation for Satellite ISAC Systems
authors: Dongseop Lee, Wonbin Hong
publish: 2026 IEEE International Workshop on Antenna Technology (iWAT)
abstract: This paper presents a new concept of a shared aperture traveling wave antenna (SATWA) using Hybrid Dualmode Floquet (HDMF) excitation for satellite Integrated Sensing and Communication (ISAC). A single periodic unit cell simultaneously excites the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{n}=-\mathbf{1}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{n}=-\mathbf{2}$</tex> spatial harmonics to enable dual-band operation over upper-mid (UM) band and Kaband with dual-linear polarization. Surface-wave suppression and optimized fringing-field coupling are achieved by a via-wall structure. To cover multi-target/multi-channel communication, the exemplified 4 × 4 array is designed and achieves twodimensional (2D) wide spatial beam scanning across a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2: 1$</tex> frequency ratio without additional feed-network structure. The proposed antenna concept is the first to demonstrate dual-band <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(12-17 / 24-32 \text{GHz})$</tex>, dual-polarized operation with a peak gain greater than 12 dBi, aperture efficiency exceeding 45%, and 2 D beam steering of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 50^{\circ} / \pm 43^{\circ}$</tex>, all within the compact footprint of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.105 \lambda^{3}$</tex>.

title: Approximation and Implementation of the Lung Bio-Heat Transfer Electrical Model
authors: Ifigeneia-Galini Koulentianou, Julia Nako, Costas Psychalinos
publish: 2026 Panhellenic Conference on Electronics &amp; Telecommunications (PACET)
abstract: The lung bio-heat transfer electrical equivalent which is described by a fractional-order impedance is approximated through the utilization of a curve-fitting based method. The resulting rational integer-order impedance function is realized using a Foster type-I RC network. The derived MATLAB and OrCAD PSpice simulation results confirm the validity of the introduced approximation concept as well as the correct operation of the presented circuitry.

title: Automated Cooked Rice Dispenser with Weight Accuracy and Spoilage Monitoring
authors: D. M. K. I. Dissanayake, H. L. Subasinghe, Kaushalya Wijayasekara, I.R Muthumala, R.D.S.K. Karunathilaka, I. M. Jafran, K. A. M. D. Jayaweera
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Cooked rice is widely served in institutional settings, yet manual portioning commonly used in cafeterias and hospitals leads to inconsistent servings, hygiene risks, and reduced operational efficiency. This study presents an IoT enabled automated cooked rice dispenser designed to address these limitations by integrating precision weight-based dispensing, thermo-regulated storage, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CO}_{2}$</tex>-based spoilage detection, ultrasonic volume sensing, and an intuitive HMI interface. The screw-based dispensing mechanism, controlled through real-time load-cell feedback, ensures accurate and repeatable portions, while a PID-regulated heating system maintains rice more than 65° C to prevent microbial growth. An MH-Z19B CO2 sensor enables early detection of spoilage, enhancing food safety. Experimental results demonstrate high performance, achieving <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 1.3 \mathrm{g}$</tex> dispensing accuracy, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 1{}^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex> thermal stability, and early microbial activity detection at around six hours. The system reduces serving time by over 50 % and significantly improves hygiene and consistency, offering a robust solution for modern institutional food-service automation.

title: Investigating Feature Selection Techniques to Enhance the Performance of Parkinson's Disease Detection Using Tablet Data
authors: Abu Saleh Musa Miah, Md. Maruf Al Hossain, Mohammad Hasan, Md. Abedur Rahman, Fahim Ashhab, Muhammad Nazrul Islam
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Parkinson's disease (PD) is the second most common neurodegenerative disorder, particularly affecting the elderly. Handwriting analysis has shown promise in detecting PD, but effective classification requires extracting and selecting relevant features. In this study, we extracted 30 features using kinematic, temporal, and statistical methods, and applied six dimensionality reduction algorithms to select efficient features. Two classifiers—Random Forest and Decision Tree were then used to distinguish PD patients from healthy controls. Experiments were conducted on a spiral handwriting dataset containing samples from 62 PD patients and 15 healthy individuals. Our model achieved higher accuracy (by over 4 %) compared to existing methods, as demonstrated by confusion matrices, accuracy tables, and comparative analysis.

title: Real-Time Breast Cancer Classification Employing GMM-HMRF Segmentation and PCA-Optimized Machine Learning Algorithms
authors: Mohammad Asif Hasan, Nafis Ahmed, Maruf Ahmed
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Holding one of the highest mortality rates among cancer-related deaths, breast cancer still necessitates rigorous research into advanced diagnostics. This work presents a comprehensive pipeline for breast cancer classification using mammographic imagery through classical preprocessing, intricate segmentation, extensive feature extraction, dimensionality reduction, and diverse machine learning classifiers. Encompassing two distinct datasets, the initial image refinement incorporated Adaptive Median Filtering (AMF) and Contrast Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization (CLAHE), augmenting lesion segmentation and image clarity. Advanced segmentation is achieved via Gaussian Mixture Models conjoined with Hidden Markov Random Fields (GMM-HMRF), fostering spatial coherence. Texture, shape, and frequency domain features are extracted and reduced via PCA while retaining 95% variance. Comparative classifier evaluation and ablation studies featuring Probabilistic Neural Network (PNN), Support Vector Machine (SVM), Random Forest (RF), and Enhanced Neural Network (ENN) are performed. Results indicate significant accuracy improvements through the application of segmentation and PCA. Additionally, an interactive Streamlit-based web application manifests real-time predictive proficiency, bolstering prospects for clinical integration. While peak CNN accuracies in literature are higher, our method achieves competitive performance (e.g., 83.3% SVM accuracy), with strong generalizability, minimal computational cost, and improved feature-space explainability, which are critical for practical deployment in limited-resource clinical setups. The SVM classifier showed the best accuracy of 83.3%, with a 0.98 AUC, while the ENN reached 75% accuracy, with a p-value of 0.05.

title: A Comprehensive Study of AI in Engineering Organizations’ Decision-Making: Capabilities, Ethics, and Performance
authors: Adel Mahmoud Al Samman
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This article explores the role of AI in decision-making within one of the construction companies (n = 297). Descriptive and regression models suggest that the AI capabilities and task automation have stronger impacts on decision effectiveness (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.288; β = 0.611, p < 0.001), whereas learning approaches and architectural techniques impact it weakly. The paper brings up ethical issues such as bias, transparency, job removal, and suggests XAI, mechanisms of fairness, and upskilling of the workforce. Limitations are single-industry scope and mediocre goodness of fit; future studies need to increase sample size and study other organizational moderators.

title: A Capacitor-Less 22nm FDSOI-Based Event-Driven Pixel Utilizing Tunable Photoresponse Speed
authors: Nan Tang, Yi Xiao, Lianliang Wu, Jiaqi Li, Ao Shi, Zheng Zhou, Lifeng Liu, Xiaoyan Liu, Jinfeng Kang, Peng Huang
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: In this work, we demonstrate a capacitor-less pixel fabricated in a 22nm fully depleted silicon-on-insulator (FDSOI) process for event-based vision sensors (EVS). The proposed pixel leverages the photovoltaic-like effect of the negatively biased p-type well to enable electrical readout of light intensity directly from the MOSFET channel current. By engineering the exposure region of the FDSOI phototransistor, we achieve tunable photoresponse speed, as confirmed by both TCAD simulations and experimental results. Based on these characteristics, we designed and fabricated a capacitor-less event-driven pixel that generates voltage spikes only in response to changes in light intensity. Owing to the adjustable photoresponse speed and active nature of FDSOI phototransistors, the proposed design achieves spike output suitable for downstream circuit processing with a compact and simplified circuit structure.

title: Graphene Inversion Layer for Enhanced Photoresponse in MXene-Si Schottky Photodiodes
authors: Muhammad Malik, Xuan Ye, Muhammad Abid Anwar, Yu Bin, Wenzhang Fang, Srikrishna Chanakya Bodepudi, Yang Xu
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: MXene-semiconductor heterojunctions are highly promising platform for realizing cost-effective, broadband and multifunctional photodetectors. However, the presence of functional groups and polar surface-terminations in MXenes can induce non-uniform interface potential and large leakage current when placed over bulk semiconductors. In this work, we proposed a van der Waals interfacial engineering approach, employing CVD graphene as a p-type inversion layer between MXene (Ti<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>T<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</inf>) and the n-type Si interface. This modified structure (MXene/Gr-Si), significantly suppressing carrier recombination and enlarging built-in potential, leading to low dark current and enhanced photoresponse. This simple van der Waals integration of 2D layered materials with silicon open new pathways for high-speed, low-noise optoelectronic devices.

title: SDNet: Interpretable Transformer-Based Captioning for Yield-Aware Nanoscale Defect Diagnosis
authors: Ruyue Jing, Yibo Qiao, Yuening Luo, Yining Chen
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: An accurate and interpretable diagnosis of wafer-level defects is essential for yield improvement in semiconductor manufacturing. Current practices still rely on manual scanning electron microscopy (SEM) image inspection, while automated semantic captioning remains limited due to the lack of domain-specific datasets. We propose the Semiconductor Diagnosis Net (SDNet), a lightweight and interpretable vision-language framework for nanoscale SEM defect diagnosis. SDNet employs a Vision Transformer encoder with a GRU decoder, enhanced by spatial and channel attention, to generate precise diagnostic captions. To support this task, we introduce SSID, the first expert-annotated SEM defect captioning dataset with 4,425 images and 7,375 sentences from a 55nm production line, enabling structured and process-aware defect understanding. The validation of SDNet on the SSID dataset has been achieved with 99.1% Top-5 Accuracy (Top-5 Acc), BLEU-1 of 0.9488, BLEU-4 of 0.8894 and Batch Time of 0.039s, demonstrating SDNet features high precision, low latency, and visual interpretability, which can achieve the information connection between low-level checks and production perception reports, thereby meeting the real-time deployment needs of wafer fabrication plants.

title: Comparative Analysis of Machine Learning Models for Accurate Solar Energy Forecasting
authors: Md.Iftakhar Ahsan Jarif, Abir Tirtha Das, Bishwajit Banik Pathik
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: To keep the grid stable, to improve storage and to help the energy market work, accurate solar power prediction is needed. The Calgary dataset was used in the study to compare machine learning and deep learning models—XGBoost, CatBoost, LightGBM, Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) and Temporal Convolutional Networks (TCN)—for short-term energy forecasting. The study also follows a method that includes preprocessing, feature engineering and model training to show the weather and time relationships. The coefficient of determination (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex>), the Symmetric Mean Absolute Percentage Error (sMAPE), the Mean Absolute Error (MAE) and the Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE) were used to assess the models. The findings show that gradient boosting techniques regularly beat deep learning models in this setting. XGBoost got the highest accuracy, recording an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> of 0.997 and an RMSE of 3.64 kWh. It outperforms CatBoost and LightGBM. CatBoost and LightGBM which had higher error rates. Conversely, LSTM and TCN exhibited subpar performance, suggesting challenges in managing feature-sparse datasets with restricted temporal data. The findings indicate that gradient boosting techniques, especially XGBoost, are resilient, effective and ideally suited for enhancing renewable energy integration.

title: A Transformer-Based Aspect-Level Sentiment Analysis Framework for Analyzing Public Discourse on Generative AI
authors: Neltjie Mendy Martutty, Muhammad Nur Rizal, Guntur Dharma Putra
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: The rapid adoption of Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI), particularly systems such as ChatGPT, has generated large-scale public discourse on social media, presenting both opportunities and challenges for computational analysis. From a machine learning perspective, modeling such discourse remains difficult due to the informal and short-text nature of social media data, class imbalance, and the need to associate sentiment with specific semantic aspects. Existing studies predominantly rely on global sentiment classification or coarsegrained topic modeling, while recent transformer-based approaches mainly focus on improving classification accuracy without explicitly modeling aspect-specific cognitive dimensions.To address these limitations, this study proposes a transformer-based aspect-level sentiment analysis (ABSA) framework for fine-grained modeling of GenAI-related public discourse using large-scale data from X (formerly Twitter). The framework integrates data-driven aspect extraction with supervised aspect and sentiment classification, enabling structured representation of cognitively relevant aspects. A subset of 2,000 tweets is manually annotated to construct a ground-truth dataset for training and evaluation. Multiple machine learning and deep learning models are benchmarked, among which a hybrid BERT-GRU architecture demonstrates superior performance by combining contextual semantic representations with sequential dependency modeling.Experimental results show that the proposed hybrid model outperforms baseline approaches, achieving an accuracy of 85.25% and a Macro F1-score of 0.8496 under class imbalance conditions. Aspect-level sentiment inference further reveals heterogeneous sentiment distributions across cognitive aspects, indicating that sentiment polarity varies significantly depending on the semantic context being modeled.Overall, the proposed framework demonstrates the effectiveness of transformer-based ABSA for fine-grained sentiment modeling on short and informal social media texts and provides a scalable machine learning approach for analyzing complex, aspect-dependent public discourse on emerging AI technologies.

title: An Effective Cardiovascular Risk Prediction Using PCA and mRMR Feature Selection with Multi-Model Explainable AI Framework
authors: Md Ishtiaq Ahmed, Razi Amin Rizvee, Moumita Tasmim Labonno, Arpon Sarkar Choity, Tanver Ahmed, Sadekatul Shihab
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Cardiovascular disease (CVD) continues to be the leading cause of death worldwide, claiming around 18 million lives each year. Predicting risk early and intervening promptly is especially important in resource-limited healthcare settings. This study introduces a machine learning framework for cardiovascular risk prediction using the Framingham Heart Study dataset, which includes <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4, 2 4 0}$</tex> records. The proposed framework combines dimensional reduction technique PCA (Principal Component Analysis) and feature selection method mRMR (minimum Redundancy Maximum Relevance) with seven machine learning classifiers Extra Trees Classifier, Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, CatBoost, XGBoost, Decision Tree, and Multilayer Perceptron. To make the predictions interpretable for clinicians, SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) and LIME (Local Interpretable Model-Agnostic Explanations) were applied as a dual explainable AI (XAI) framework. Overall, the framework performed best when PCA was combined with the Extra Trees Classifier, reaching 0.9763 accuracy, 0.9797 recall, 0.9780 precision, 0.9762 F1-score, and 0.9763 ROC-AUC using 10 -fold stratified cross-validation with standard deviation. XAI analysis highlighted age, systolic blood pressure, and glucose as the most important predictors. By balancing high predictive performance with interpretability, this framework provides a transparent and scalable tool for cardiovascular risk assessment, which could be particularly useful in developing regions.

title: Non-Volatile Polarization-Reconfigurable SSPP Antenna
authors: Ziqi Ren, Xiaoyu Xiao, Zhirun Hu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Workshop on Antenna Technology (iWAT)
abstract: A non-volatile polarization reconfigurable spoof surface plasmon polariton (SSPP) antenna is presented. Twelve bistable and non-volatile Nafion memristive switches are placed across the single gap of each circular slot element patterned along the SSPP line. By controlling the switches into different resistive states, the antenna can realize circular, elliptical, and linear polarization states without any static bias power. Three switching configurations are implemented: all-OFF, all-ON, and an alternating complementary pattern. Simulated and measured <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$S_{11}$</tex> results confirm consistent impedance characteristics across the operating band, while the simulated radiation patterns verify stable beam directions and clear polarization reconfigurability. The proposed design provides an energy-efficient solution for adaptive and programmable microwave systems.

title: CodeScent: A Deep Ensemble and LLM-Based Framework for Python and Java Code Smell Detection with Android Integration
authors: Tasnia Haque Kheya, Shaikh Prothomaa Binte Minhaz
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Code smells are recurring design flaws that negatively affect software maintainability, readability, and overall quality. Automated detection remains challenging, particularly across heterogeneous programming languages. In this study, we propose a multi-stage framework of code smell detection in Python and Java with Android aggregation. This framework includes feature-based heuristics, deep learning, ensemble models, and LLM-assisted analysis. The system was designed for testing on both binary (smelly and non-smelly) and multi-class smell detection. It identified common smells like Long Method, Long Parameter List, Data Class, Large Class, Feature Envy, and Duplicate Code. Our experimental results show strong performance, with test accuracies around <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$94-96 \%$</tex>, ROC-AUC of 0.98, precision between 0.82 and 0.84, recall reaching 0.98, F1-scores around 0.90, and Matthews correlation coefficients (MCC) ranging from 0.83 to 0.87. To improve understanding, the framework generates confidence scores and human-readable explanations. A major contribution of this work is the development of an Android application that implements the detection pipeline. This app allows users to input source code and get instant feedback on detected smells, along with explanations and confidence levels. This deployment shows the practical use of our approach and actively demonstrates the potential for scalable, cross-language tools to improve software quality.

title: Hybrid Vision Transformer Framework for Brain Tumor Detection and Classification
authors: Md Naymur Rohman, Rakibul Hasan Efty, Ashfaqul Islam, Abdul Kadar Muhammad Masum, Khandaker Mohammad Mohi Uddin
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Brain tumor, a common type of uncontrolled cell growth in the central nervous system, is very difficult to medically diagnose and treat. Early diagnosis is critical for effective treatment. This paper aims to improve the detection and classification of brain tumor in the Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) scan using a vision transformer (ViT) based innovative framework. This paper created a generic image processing pipeline with resize, augmentation, and normalization, fine-tuning four ImageNet-pretrained backbones (ViT, DeiT, Swin, and CaiT) by freezing most of the layers, while only unfreezing the last few blocks and adding a new classification head. AdamW and cosine learning-rate scheduler are used and mixed-precision calculations. The predictions are combined with a soft voting ensemble, which takes the average of the model logits. Performance is presented in terms of accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score. ViT can achieve an accuracy of 97.25 %; the all-models ensemble achieves an accuracy of 95.88 %. The best performing configuration is a two-model ensemble (ViT+DeiT), which resulted in 97.71 % accuracy, 97.70 % precision, 97.57 % recall, and a 97.59% F1-score, outperforming the single backbones while being lightweight. The framework is evaluated and the experimental results show that the proposed framework can achieve robust yet interpretable MRI brain tumor classification and that the trained transformer encoders and selective ensembling are well-suited for clinical triage & deployment.

title: Tomato Leaf Disease Classification Using RHN-EfficientNet: A Regularized Deep Learning Architecture
authors: Md. Emran Biswas, Tangina Sultana, Tanbin Yasmin Tanny, Jahidatunnaher Tani, Md. Tanin Hossain, Hasi Saha, Ashis Kumar Mandal, Md. Delowar Hossain
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The timely and accurate identification of tomato leaf diseases is crucial for ensuring global food security and sustainable agricultural productivity. Manual visual inspection is often slow and error-prone, creating a pressing need for robust automated solutions. This study proposes a novel deeplearning model, the Regularized Head-Normalized EfficientNet (RHN-EfficientNet), for the automated classification of tomato leaf diseases. The model leverages transfer learning by integrating a pre-trained EfficientNetB0 backbone as a feature extractor with a custom-designed, heavily regularized classification head comprising Batch Normalization and Dropout layers to enhance generalization and prevent overfitting. The model was trained and evaluated on a curated dataset containing four classes: Healthy, Leaf Mold, Mosaic Virus, and Septoria Leaf Spot. The proposed RHN-EfficientNet achieved a state-of-the-art test accuracy of 97.92%, significantly outperforming several benchmark architectures including VGG16, ResNet50, and MobileNetV2. Visual analysis via confusion matrix and ROC curves, where all classes attained a perfect AUC of 1.00, further confirmed the model's exceptional discriminatory power and robustness, with only a single misclassification observed. These results conclusively demonstrate that the RHN-EfficientNet architecture is a highly effective and reliable tool for automated tomato leaf disease diagnosis, with the potential to be deployed in real-world agricultural applications.

title: Multi-Band Patch Antenna Array for Out-of-Band Aided Millimeter Wave Communication
authors: Faruk Pasic, Jure Soklič, Robert Langwieser, Stefan Schwarz, Christoph F. Mecklenbräuker
publish: 2026 IEEE International Workshop on Antenna Technology (iWAT)
abstract: Future wireless communication systems will integrate both sub-6 GHz and millimeter wave (mmWave) frequency bands within multi-antenna architectures to meet the increasing demand for high data rates. In such multi-band systems, reliable information obtained from the sub-6 GHz band can be exploited to support communication at mmWave frequencies. To ensure that both systems experience similar multi-path propagation effects, the sub-6 GHz and mmWave antenna arrays have to be colocated and precisely aligned. However, such a configuration may adversely alter the radiation characteristics of the arrays, potentially degrading their performance. In this paper, we investigate the impact of positioning a mmWave antenna structure in front of a sub-6 GHz antenna structure. Through both simulations and measurements, we evaluate how the presence of the mmWave structure affects the radiation pattern of the csub-6 GHz one. The results demonstrate that the influence of the mmWave structure on the sub-6 GHz performance is minor, indicating that co-located configurations are feasible with negligible degradation.

title: Compact Transparent Dual-Band Antenna with Artificial Magnetic Conductor for Wearable Devices
authors: Anxiu Zhang, Zhenzhen Jiang, Heming Yang, Yuanrui Wang, Arjun Kumar, Mark Leach
publish: 2026 IEEE International Workshop on Antenna Technology (iWAT)
abstract: A dual-band coplanar waveguide (CPW)-fed transparent antenna integrated with a flexible artificial magnetic conductor (AMC) reflector is proposed for wearable applications. The antenna uses polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS) as a flexible, low-loss substrate and MT150 film as a transparent conductive layer. A compact 3 × 2 AMC array provides in-phase reflection, suppresses backward radiation, and enhances forward gain. The prototype achieves bandwidths of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$130 \text{MHz}(2.37-2.50 \text{GHz})$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1400 \text{MHz}(5.10-6.50 \text{GHz})$</tex>, covering the 2.45 and 5.8 GHz bands. Realised gains increase to 6.2 and 8.2 dBi at 2.45 and 5.8 GHz, respectively. The antenna maintains stable performance under bending and exhibits a low specific absorption rate, offering a compact, high-gain solution for wearable communication systems.

title: E80: An Open-Source 8-bit CPU and Toolchain based on Textbook Digital Design Theory
authors: Panos Stokas, Vassilis Fotopoulos, Konstantinos Giannakopoulos
publish: 2026 Panhellenic Conference on Electronics &amp; Telecommunications (PACET)
abstract: Educational soft-cores often obscure hardware execution through high-level behavioral abstractions. We present the design methodology of the E80, a single-cycle 8-bit CPU developed to bridge the gap between digital design theory and computer architecture. We discuss the decision to rely on structural VHDL without arithmetic libraries, a choice that prioritizes gate-level transparency over synthesis efficiency. Furthermore, we detail the choices we made for our instruction set architecture, our assembly language and the development of a toolchain to automate the flow of the assembler, simulator, and waveform viewer in a free open-source package, to provide “one-click” hardware simulation for novices. Synthesis across three distinct platforms validates the design’s portability and quantifies the resource-performance trade-offs inherent in an implementation that impedes the inference of vendor components.

title: Unlocking AI Adoption in Future Accounting Professionals: The Mediating Influence of User Perceptions
authors: Akila Jayawardhana, Neranjana Ekanayake, Wayomi Wijesundara
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: This study investigates the determinants influencing the adoption of artificial intelligence (AI) among accounting undergraduates, the future accounting professionals in Sri Lanka, with a distinct focus on technology readiness, perceived usefulness, and perceived ease of use. As digital competencies progressively shape the existing accounting profession, the study acknowledges both the practical and empirical gaps emerge from the limited incorporation of AI-related content in local accounting curricula and the lack of evidence from the Sri Lankan higher education context. Using a quantitative research approach, data were collected from Accountancy and Finance undergraduates enrolled in state universities in the Western Province through a structured questionnaire comprising 30 items administered via Google Forms. A stratified sampling technique was used, and the data were analysed using correlation analysis, multiple linear regression, and mediation analysis with SPSS. The findings explore that technology readiness, perceived usefulness, and perceived ease of use exert a significant and positive influence on students' intentions to adopt AI. In addition, perceived usefulness and perceived ease of use partially mediate the relationship between technology readiness and AI adoption, suggesting that students' perceptions and attitudes play a vital role in translating technological readiness into actual adoption intentions. These results depict the importance of strengthening curriculum design by embedding practical AI applications and learning experiences that promote confidence and perceived value. The study offers useful insights for educators, curriculum developers, and policymakers seeking to enhance AI readiness and acceptance within accounting education. Overall, the findings bestow ongoing efforts to prepare future accounting professionals in Sri Lanka to adapt effectively to an increasingly digital business environment.

title: Freelancer-Client Matching System with AI-Based Skill Assessment
authors: Md. Takbir Al Tanim, Md. Ataur Rahman, Mahady Hasan
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Freelancing platforms have constantly faced issues like mismatched skill and job, fake or inflated profiles, the recruiting lag, and the bias in hiring processes. In an attempt to resolve the above issues, this paper proposes an intelligent freelancer-client matching system with skill assessment. This system uses natural language processing and machine learning models to evaluate freelancer profiles and job descriptions as well as their performance data to optimize matching on freelancer job platforms. An autonomous verification module is incorporated to validate as well as authenticate the skill attributes while a real time recommender system is integrated to offer personalized job and freelancer sponsorships. Proprietary fairness-aware algorithms are incorporated to eliminate any form of demographic and geographic bias for equal opportunity employment. Other system enhancing products include conversational AI to facilitate client-freelancer communication, content analysis to determine response sentiment to evaluate and enhance trust scores, and the use of blockchain technology to issue certificates for increased trust and privacy. With a modular and scalable architecture, the system is able to migrate and customize for diverse industry demands and global markets. This approach combines accuracy, fairness and efficiency, which aims to increase client-freelancer collaborations, lower the recruitment expenditure, and improve the trustworthiness of the digital freelancing ecosystem.

title: A Novel Dopant-Segregated Si FeFET with a Superlattice Gate Stack Featuring Low-Temperature Gate-First Compatibility and Enhanced Memory Window
authors: Shaodi Xu, Yue Yu, Qianqian Huang, Ru Huang
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: In this paper, a novel dopant-segregated (DS) Si FeFET featuring low-temperature gate-first compatibility is experimentally demonstrated for the first time. The implant into silicide (IIS) process under optimized conditions is adopted for the dopant segregation, leading to a pronounced dopant pile-up and a high on/off current ratio. Concurrently, the gate stack was co-optimized with the IIS process, where a thin interfacial layer (IL) and superlattice (SL) HZO ferroelectric (FE) layer were fabricated, and the rapid thermal annealing (RTA) temperature is optimized. The DS FeFET shows improved memory window (MW) of 1.3V at low operation voltage (V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">OP</inf>). This work provides effective solutions of integrating front-end-of-line (FEOL) Si HZO FeFETs and expands their potentials for high-reliable embedded memory applications.

title: Balancing Accuracy and Efficiency: CNN Fusion Models for Diabetic Retinopathy Screening
authors: Md Rafid Islam, Rafsan Jany, Akib Ahmed, Mohammad Ashrafuzzaman Khan
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Diabetic retinopathy (DR) remains a leading cause of preventable blindness, yet large-scale screening is constrained by limited specialist availability and variable image quality across devices and populations. This work investigates whether featurelevel fusion of complementary convolutional neural network (CNN) backbones can deliver accurate and efficient binary DR screening on globally sourced fundus images. Using 11,156 images pooled from five public datasets (APTOS, EyePACS, IDRiD, Messidor, and ODIR), we frame DR detection as a binary classification task and compare three pretrained models (ResNet50, EfficientNet-B0, and DenseNet121) against pairwise and tri-fusion variants. Across five independent runs, fusion consistently outperforms single backbones. The EfficientNet-B0 + DenseNet121 (Eff+Den) fusion model achieves the best overall mean performance (accuracy: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 2. 8 9 \%}$</tex>) with balanced class-wise F1-scores for normal (83.60 %) and diabetic (82.60 %) cases. While the tri-fusion is competitive, it incurs a substantially higher computational cost. Inference profiling highlights a practical trade-off: EfficientNet-B0 is the fastest (approximately 1.16 ms/image at batch size 1000), whereas the Eff+Den fusion offers a favorable accuracy-latency balance. These findings indicate that lightweight feature fusion can enhance generalization across heterogeneous datasets, supporting scalable binary DR screening workflows where both accuracy and throughput are critical.

title:
DC bias-tunning strategy to optimize Sc
                    <sub>0.3</sub>
                    Al
                    <sub>0.7</sub>
                    N cantilever-type MEMS Speakers performance and wafer-level uniformity
authors: Prakasha Chigahalli Ramegowda, David Sze Wai Choong, Shyam Trivedi, Yul Koh
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: The performance of Lead Zirconate Titanate (PZT) and Scandium-doped Aluminum Nitride (ScAlN) Piezoelectric Micromachined Ultrasonic Transducers (pMUTs) and speakers is heavily influenced by residual stresses from thin-film fabrication. In cantilever-based designs, these stresses induce significant out-of-plane deflection, causing the membrane to vibrate around a biased offset. This displacement creates structural gaps that facilitate air leakage, leading to substantial acoustic loss and degraded Sound Pressure Level (SPL). Due to the residual stress variation of a piezoelectric layer in a wafer, this offset deflection is one of the bottlenecks for the high yield manufacturing. To address this problem, this study demonstrates that applying a DC bias effectively counteracts residual stress, flattening the membrane and restoring acoustic performance. To validate this, we fabricated Sc<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</inf>.<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>Al<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</inf>.<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">7</inf>N triangular cantilever-type speakers exhibiting high tensile residual stress. This stress resulted in upward tip deflections of 68–100 µm across the wafer, creating leakage paths. We show that a DC bias (-125 V to -175 V) can compensate for this stress, flattening the membrane at a rate of 0.5 µm/V. Experimental results confirm that optimal DC bias tuning achieves a 2× increase in tip displacement and ~12 dBSPL sound pressure increases under 2Vpp AC excitation at resonance. While DC bias significantly enhances the sound pressure and allows for frequency tuning from 13.8 kHz to 12.6 kHz, we observe that polar directivity remains largely unaffected due to diffraction at the sharp cantilever tips. This study provides a comprehensive wafer-level analysis essential for improving the manufacturability and acoustic reliability of PiezoMEMS speakers.

title: WGB-VM: Uncertainty-Aware Virtual Metrology Framework for Semiconductor Manufacturing
authors: Weiping Xie, Zixuan Huang, Yining Chen
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: In semiconductor manufacturing, virtual metrology (VM) techniques have been widely adopted to enhance process monitoring and reduce measurement costs. However, most existing approaches primarily focus on point predictions while neglecting the reliability of predictions. To address this gap, we propose an uncertainty-aware VM framework based on Wasserstein Gradient Boosting Trees (WGB-VM). Experimental validation on 1329 wafers from a HDP-CVD system demonstrates the effectiveness of the proposed approach, achieving an R-squared value of up to 0.616 and a negative log-likelihood score of 4.372, thereby confirming its strong predictive accuracy and uncertainty quantification capability.

title: Resonance Stability and Bandwidth Variation in Fabricated X-Band Microstrip Resonator
authors: Shah Faisal, Arbab Abdur Rahim, Aneesa Razaq, Tahseen Amin Khan Qasuria, Husnul Maab
publish: 2026 Global Conference on Wireless and Optical Technologies (GCWOT)
abstract: This paper presents the fabrication and experimental characterization of a microstrip resonator designed for X-band applications. The structure was previously simulated, exhibiting a narrow bandwidth of 152 MHz at a resonance frequency of 10.64 GHz. In this work, the same design was fabricated on a single side copper coated FR4 substrate and tested using a Vector Network Analyzer in transmission mode. The measured results show excellent agreement with the simulated data in terms of absorption and resonance frequency, while revealing a significant increase in bandwidth. Various factors contributing to this bandwidth broadening are analyzed and discussed in detail, emphasizing the influence of practical fabrication parameters on the performance of the microstrip resonator.

title: XGBoost and Temporal Feature Engineering for Precise Co Emission Forecasting in Dhaka
authors: Md. Asif Hossain, Aymanur Rahman, Taslim Sarker, Afroza Nahar, Kamruddin Nur
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Carbon monoxide (CO) is a toxic air pollutant with serious negative impacts on human health and environmental sustainability. As an overpopulated country such as Bangladesh, rapid urbanization, industrialization, and increasing vehicle emissions have rapidly increased carbon monoxide (CO) emissions, particularly in Dhaka City. In this study, a machine learning-based forecasting framework has been developed using hourly air quality data. Multiple pollutants (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{O}_{3}, \text{NO}_{2}, \text{SO}_{2}$</tex>, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{P M}_{10}, \text{P M}_{2.5}$</tex>) and time-based features (lag, rolling, and cyclical features) were used as inputs. The XGBoost model was chosen for the forecast, as it can efficiently capture non-linear and time-dependent relationships. The results showed that the model produced highly accurate predictions in the testing phase (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{R}^{2}{=}$</tex> 0.9954, root mean square error (RMSE) <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.0417$</tex>), demonstrating that proper feature engineering and robust ensemble models are highly effective in predicting CO emissions. This research suggests that in a fast-urbanizing nation such as Bangladesh, precise carbon monoxide forecasts can contribute significantly to policy development and safeguard public health.

title:
A BSIM4-based SPICE Model for MoS
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Transistors Incorporating Trap Effects for Integrated Circuit Applications
authors: Jiangchuan Tian, Yu Geng, Kuanglei Chen, Xiaoyu He, He Jiang, Jiankun Xiao, Xiankun Zhang, Zheng Zhang, Yue Zhang
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: For the first time, we employ the industry-standard BSIM4 framework to develop a compact model for two-dimensional (2D) molybdenum disulfide (MoS2) field-effect transistors. We present the first integration of dynamic trap effects into 2D semiconductors compact model by incorporating a dedicated RC subcircuit. This approach accurately describes the capture and emission processes of interface traps, enabling highly precise transient circuit simulations. Such simulations lead to the largest number of logic circuit types reported to date in the field of 2D integrated circuits. Furthermore, we successfully construct a comprehensive MoS2 logic cell library, demonstrating the potential of the model for the large-scale integrated circuit design. This work establishes a novel methodology for developing compact models tailored to emerging semiconductor technologies.

title: Educational Data Mining of VLE Interaction Logs for Explaining Student Assessment Outcomes: A K-Means Clustering Study
authors: Yasanthika Mathotaarachchi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Virtual Learning Environments (VLEs) generate extensive traces of learner interaction; however, educators and institutions often lack interpretable analytical approaches to determine which online learning activities meaningfully contribute to assessment success. This study applies Educational Data Mining (EDM) techniques to investigate the relationship between students' online learning behaviors and assessment outcomes using large-scale VLE interaction data. The Open University Learning Analytics Dataset (OULAD), containing detailed student, assessment, and activity records across multiple course presentations, is used as the empirical basis of the analysis. A consolidated learner–activity–assessment dataset is constructed through relational integration and preprocessing of multiple OULAD tables. K-Means clustering is applied to identify distinct engagement–performance profiles without relying on predefined outcome labels. The analysis results in four clusters with heterogeneous engagement characteristics, among which one cluster emerges as dominant, capturing the highest proportion of meaningful learning interactions and demonstrating a strong association with higher assessment performance. Further analysis examines activity-type distributions across clusters to evaluate the relative influence of different online learning activities. The results indicate that engagement with structured learning resources and core content activities exhibits the strongest relationship with academic success, while activities supporting formative practice and interaction contribute positively when combined with contentfocused engagement. In contrast, excessive interaction with navigational or supplementary activities shows limited association with improved performance. These findings suggest that engagement quality and activity semantics are more informative indicators of learning effectiveness than overall activity volume. The study provides interpretable, cluster-based evidence to support data-driven instructional decision-making and highlights the importance of guiding students toward highimpact learning activities in higher education contexts.

title: Harmonizing Nature: Real-Time Plant Signal Analysis for Music Generation, Interactive Sound, and Moisture Monitoring
authors: V Shankar, Venkat Raghavendra A H, V Hemanth, Sushanth M. G., Ashwini Bhat
publish: 2026 IEEE Bangalore Humanitarian Technology Conference (B-HTC)
abstract: This project presents a cost-effective system for analyzing plant responses by integrating hardware and software. An Arduino measures plant capacitance, which is processed in Python to generate ambient music, while a Max-Patch transforms plants into interactive instruments that respond to touch. A notable feature is a water alarm that alerts users when moisture levels drop, supporting timely irrigation and plant health. This real-time monitoring setup enables precision watering and highlights creative applications in Plant Signal Extraction. Designed for affordability and scalability, the system provides an accessible tool for plant signal analysis, interactive sound generation, and moisture detection, suitable for both research and artistic exploration.

title: Zinc Oxide Thin Film Piezoelectric Devices on Silicon and PDMS Substrates: Structural and Electrical Characterisation
authors: Syazwani Izrah Binti Badrudin, Rhonira Latif, Jumril Yunas
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: ZnO thin film piezoelectric devices are promising for flexible electronics, yet direct device-level comparisons between rigid and flexible substrates remain limited. In this work, ZnO thin film devices were fabricated on rigid silicon and flexible polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS) substrates using RF magnetron sputtering in metal–insulator–semiconductor (MIS) and metal–insulator–metal (MIM) configurations. The effects of substrate type and sputtering power (150 and 200 W) on crystallographic orientation and electrical leakage behavior were investigated. X-ray diffraction (XRD) shows stronger c-axis orientation for films on rigid substrates, while electrical measurements indicate that ZnO/Si devices exhibit the lowest leakage current. Leakage in PDMS-based devices decreases with increasing sputtering power, highlighting key trade-offs in flexible device integration.

title: Monte Carlo-Based EM Lifetime Modeling of Power Delivery Network with Redundant BGA Pins
authors: Jae-Gyung Ahn, Omkar Gupte, Jim O'Donnell, Jonathan Chang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Reliability Physics Symposium (IRPS)
abstract: High-power Integrated Circuits (ICs) with redundant Ball-Grid Arrays (BGAs) demand accurate Electro-Migration (EM) lifetime estimation. This work presents a Monte Carlo methodology that incorporates lognormal variability, the physics of parallel resistance networks, and dynamic current redistribution to realistically model EM behavior in Power Delivery Networks (PDNs). Applied to 20- and 38-pin PDNs, the approach captures the impact of redundancy on stress currents, IR drop, and temperature increase due to Joule heating (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\Delta \mathrm{T}_{\text{JHE}}$</tex>), demonstrating that leveraging redundancy can increase the PDN EM lifetime by up to a factor of four compared with the conventional series-system models.

title: Malicious Command Line Detection
authors: Emilie Coote Atlas, Taylor Perkins, Brian Lachine, Chandra Majumdar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Systems Conference (SysCon)
abstract: Given the significant amount of encrypted traffic used in enterprise environments, host-based monitoring is a necessity in any intrusion detection strategy. Important hostbased artifacts are the command lines executed on host systems, whether they are used for regular system operation and administration or for malicious purposes. Researchers have investigated various machine learning approaches to improve malicious command-line detection. This work sought to achieve detection capabilities comparable with contemporary research that could also be realistically used in a production SOC environment that continuously processes significant amounts of host data. The aim of this research was to develop a light weight malicious command line detector with effective detection performance that could be leveraged in a production SOC environment. This research used malicious command line samples from two open-source datasets and host-based data from two different corporate production networks. The developed solution did not require any de-obfuscation and first conducted data preprocessing using Bloom filters and fuzzy matching for efficient whitelisting, then used RoBERTa for text representation followed by k-NN clustering to identify malicious command lines. Four different experiments were conducted, including separate client and malicious combinations between training and testing to validate and demonstrate the generalization of the proposed solution. The implemented detector achieved results comparable to contemporary research, achieving F1 scores ranging from 97.9% to 99.3% when tested on enterprise production network data.

title: Socio-Economic-Technical Synthesis: A Novel Triple Bridge Framework for Quantifying the Impact of Organizational Knowledge Gaps on Technical System Failures
authors: Raymond K. Jonkers
publish: 2026 IEEE International Systems Conference (SysCon)
abstract: The primary challenge in managing systemic risk is the policy-to-physics gap; the inability to quantitatively link high-level organizational decisions to low-level technical system outcomes. This paper addresses this gap by proposing the SD-DoE-ML Triple Bridge that shows how project failures are symptoms of a deeper systemic failure: the inability of traditional methodologies to effectively integrate and manage socio-economic-technical factors and risks. Furthermore, engineering disasters, from the Challenger space shuttle to the Titan submersible, confirm that fragmented information and organizational blind spots are the ultimate cause of failures. Preventing project failures and engineering disasters requires a fundamental paradigm shift in methodology. This paper proposes the SD-DoE-ML Triple Bridge, a novel approach to enable robust design optimization (RDO), improve enterprise communication, and establish systemic resilience. This Triple Bridge fuses system dynamics (SD), design of experiments (DoE), and AI machine learning (ML) to create a high-fidelity predictive digital twin of a coupled management flight simulator (MFS) and model-based systems engineering (MBSE) ecosystem. This expanded ecosystem is uniquely capable of achieving causal discovery by translating non-linear socio-economic-technical factors into quantitative integrated DoE and ML features. These features play into standard project management (PM) and systems engineering (SE) management curves. The proposed framework is validated through non-linear response surface methodology (RSM) that maps the interaction between management-side knowledge investment and system-level probability of failure. Results indicate that strategic gaming serves as a critical policy lever, where earlystage resource allocation yields a significant reduction in systemic risk. By quantifying the diminishing returns of these interventions, the model provides a decision-support system (DSS) for balancing organizational policy with technical resilience requirements. This creates a bidirectional digital thread that provides prescriptive policy levers to ensure systemic robustness and resilience against escalating project stressors

title: Decentralized Online Learning with Hard Constraints: A Doubly-Bounded Queue Approach
authors: Yituo Liu, Weiyi Qin, Wei Bao, Juncheng Wang, Jianxiong Guo, Min Zhou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Networking
abstract: We consider decentralized online convex optimization with time-varying constraints and conduct performance analysis using two stringent metrics: network dynamic regret with respect to the online global solution benchmark, and hard constraint violation that does not allow any compensated violation over time and learners. We propose an efficient algorithm called Constrained Online Learning with Doubly-bounded Queue (COLDQ), which introduces a novel virtual queue that is both lower and upper bounded, allowing tight control of the constraint violation without needing the Slater’s condition. We prove via a new Lyapunov drift analysis that COLDQ provides O(T 1+Vx/2 ) network dynamic regret and O(min{T 3+Vx/4 ; TVg }) hard constraint violation, where Vx and Vg capture the dynamics of the loss and constraint functions. For the first time, the two bounds smoothly approach to the best-known O(T 1/2 ) regret and O(1) violation, as the dynamics of the losses and constraints diminish, under both centralized and decentralized settings. For strongly convex loss functions, COLDQ provides O(log T) static regret and O(min{T1/2 (log T)1/2 , TVg }) hard constraint violation. Simulation results based on synthetic and canonical datasets demonstrate that COLDQ substantially outperforms the state-of-the-art approaches for both convex and non-convex loss functions.

title: Laser-Guided Control for Multicopter UAVs: Preliminary Design and Testing
authors: Ruaa Nakkar, Hassan Abid, Syed Saad Azhar Ali, Mohamed Ismail
publish: 2026 IEEE International Systems Conference (SysCon)
abstract: This paper presents a preliminary study of a laserguided, vision-based framework for evaluating and tuning multicopter trajectory tracking. A floor line and a UAV-projected laser dot are captured by a downward-facing onboard camera, and an offline image-processing pipeline computes the signed lateral deviation between them for proportional-integral-derivative (PID) tuning. We compare the vision-derived error directly against OptiTrack on straight X/Y trajectories at fixed altitude. Experiments show that the vision metric gives more repeatable and less variable lateral-error signals than OptiTrack, with mid-range proportional and derivative gains yielding the most stable tracking, while excessive integral action degrades performance.Overall, the method delivers a practical, vision-based means for controller tuning and lays the groundwork for future real-time visual control of UAVs.

title: Topography-Aware Adaptive Fusion for Multi-Scale Super Resolution of Multi-Source DEMs
authors: Haiyan Pan, Jianqiao Ji, Zhonghua Hong, Kuifeng Luan, Jiang Tao, Chenchen Jiang, Han Zhang, Yanling Han, Yun Zhang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Super resolution (SR) of digital elevation models (DEMs) is crucial for numerous geospatial applications, yet existing methods still face severe challenges when dealing with multi-scale reconstruction and multi-source data. In particular, mainstream DEM SR approaches primarily rely on convolutional neural networks (CNNs), whose inherent local receptive fields limit their ability to model the global structural consistency of large geomorphic units such as mountain ranges. As a result, when handling terrains with spatial heterogeneity, it is difficult to simultaneously ensure the fidelity of local details. To address this feature representation bottleneck, this paper proposes a unified framework named TAAF-SRNet. The framework integrates collaborative learning with meta-learning-based upsampling to enable flexible multi-scale reconstruction. At its core are the enhanced feature extraction module (EFEM), which adopts a parallel CNN-Transformer architecture, and a novel terrain aware adaptive fusion mechanism. This mechanism can dynamically assess local terrain complexity and adaptively adjust the weighting between the local textures captured by CNNs and the global context modeled by Transformers. The terrain aware strategy enables a unified model to adapt to diverse landforms, ranging from vast abyssal plains to rugged terrestrial mountain ranges. Extensive experiments on different DEM datasets covering three representative heterogeneous geomorphologies, namely land, land-sea transition, and seabed, demonstrate that TAAFSRNet not only outperforms current state-of-the-art (SOTA) methods on image quality metrics such as PSNR and LPIPS, but also better preserves terrain fidelity, as reflected by lower MAE and RMSE in elevation, slope, and aspect estimation. The code will be available at: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/bbqboy666/TAAF-SRNet</uri>.
