title: First Wall Design of a Tokamak Pilot Plant Using a Monte Carlo Model for 3-D Heat Flux Deposition
authors: J. H. Nichols, K. Borowiec, J. D. Lore, E. A. Unterberg, J. W. Bae, F. Oz, A. Sircar
publish: IEEE Transactions on Plasma Science
abstract: We present a method for calculating the heat fluxes deposited on nonaxisymmetric tokamak first wall components, allowing for a first-of-its-kind model for power handling in the tokamak far scrape-off layer (SOL). The DIV3D Monte Carlo model features strict global power conservation and can calculate the finite cross-field plasma transport into magnetically-shadowed regions, which is significant when dealing with meter-scale shadows introduced by components such as poloidal limiters or antennas. As a case study, we apply the DIV3D model to inform the distribution of first wall poloidal limiters in an ARC-class reactor device. We demonstrate that discrete protection limiters can efficiently reduce peak heat fluxes on recessed breeder wall components in the presence of significant far-SOL plasma fluxes. By varying the toroidal periodicity and radial standoff depth of the limiters, we demonstrate one of the tradeoffs that must be considered in first wall design: more limiters provide greater protection, but at the cost of reduced breeding performance. We also present the impact that radial misalignments between limiters would have on first wall power loading.

title: A Compensated Mini-Permeameter for Localized Magnetic Loss Measurements on Magnetic Laminations
authors: Olivier de la Barriére, Gulaly Khan, Carlo Stefano Ragusa, Frédéric Mazaleyrat, Luigi Solimene, Alessandro Prete
publish: IEEE Transactions on Magnetics
abstract: Accurate characterization of magnetic losses in permeameter-based systems is essential for reliable evaluation of magnetic materials. However, the magnetomotive force (MMF) drop in the flux-closing yokes can significantly affect the measurements, particularly in compact geometries where only a limited portion of the specimen is magnetized, leading to systematic overestimation of the losses. This paper presents a compensated mini-permeameter in which the MMF drop in the yokes is counteracted by an auxiliary winding driven by a feedback control scheme. The proposed approach enables the accurate determination of the magnetic field in the magnetized region directly from the magnetizing current. Experimental validation is performed through comparison with local tangential H-coil measurements on 0.35-mm-thick non-grain-oriented and 0.29-mm-thick grain-oriented laminations. Magnetic loss and hysteresis loop measurements at 50 Hz demonstrate that the compensation effectively mitigates systematic loss overestimation and enables close agreement with the reference method for both NGO and GO laminations. The proposed technique provides a compact and robust solution for localized magnetic loss characterization and can be extended to both standard and non-standard single-sheet testers.

title: Full-Mesh Quantum Key Distribution Network Based on Time-Shared Entanglement Distribution
authors: Yury Kurochkin, Vadim Rodimin, Alexei Ponasenko, Attila Pereszlenyi, Vlad Revici, Rodrigo Piera, Tahar Mehri, Karen Sloyan, Jaideep Singh, James A. Grieve
publish: 2026 International Conference on Quantum Communications, Networking, and Computing (QCNC)
abstract: We report on the design and field validation of a fully connected entanglement-based quantum key distribution (QKD) network employing a time-shared photon-pair source and reconfigurable optical switching. The system eliminates the need for trusted relay nodes and enables any-to-any secure connectivity among users over metropolitan-scale fiber infrastructure. We present a three-node prototype network operating in the O-band <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(1310 / 1316 \text{nm})$</tex> which demonstrates key generation sufficient to provide pairwise 10 G encrypted connections between 32 users, and confirming practical feasibility for telecom integration.

title: A Quantum SMT Solver for Bit-Vector Theory
authors: Shang-Wei Lin, Si-Han Chen, Lei-Han Yao, Yu-Chung Chen, Yean-Ru Chen
publish: 2026 International Conference on Quantum Communications, Networking, and Computing (QCNC)
abstract: Satisfiability modulo theory (SMT), a problem of determining the satisfiability of a first-order formula with respect to a decidable first-order theory, has a wide range of applications because of its powerful ability of problem encoding, including formal verification and optimization workflows in both classical and quantum domains. Given an SMT formula <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F$</tex>, the classical lazy approach for SMT solving tries to (1) abstract <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F$</tex> to a Boolean formula <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F_{B}$</tex>, (2) find a Boolean solution to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F_{B}$</tex>, and (3) check whether the Boolean solution is consistent with the theory. If the solution found in the Boolean domain is not consistent with that in the theory domain, the SMT solver needs to perform steps (2) and (3) back and forth until a consistent solution is found between the theory and Boolean domains. This classical approach becomes unscalable when the search space is huge. In this work, we develop the first Grover–based quantum SMT solver to solve formulas on the theory of bit-vectors. With the characteristic of superposition in quantum computing, the proposed SMT solver is able to consider all the possible inputs simultaneously and check their consistency between the theory and Boolean domains in one shot, which brings significant speed-up for SMT solving.

title: Real-Time Estimation of Tropospheric Delay Measurements Using a Low-Cost Weather Station Integrated With the Hopfield Model
authors: Vitor S. Tavares, Luiz P. S. Damaceno, Luiz V. G. Tarelho, Daniel V. Magalhães
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: This study presents preliminary results of the development of a meteorological microstation that calculates and can correct the tropospheric delay in the precise point positioning method with a global navigation satellite system (GNSS). Using the Hopfield model fed with atmospheric data collected by a BME280 sensor, zenith tropospheric delay (ZTD) was estimated based on temperature and pressure, and this ZTD was compared with the ZTD measured by GNSS signals processed on the UTC(LRTE) time scale. The tests were conducted over a period of 38 days using both the GPS and GLONASS satellite constellations. Time-series analysis confirms that models based on locally collected data reduced the noise amplitude associated with the GPS constellation by 98.74% and 99.67% for the GLONASS constellation, significantly improving the accuracy of applications that require high-precision positioning, as well as time and frequency synchronization.

title: Enhancing Defense Readiness Through Lean Six Sigma: A Case Study in Serialized Materiel Management
authors: Logan D. Dosan, Patrick J. Lupfer, Matthew J. Cavoli, Charles S. Farmer, Gavin J. Shields, Turner A. West
publish: 2026 IEEE International Systems Conference (SysCon)
abstract: This case study applies Lean and Six Sigma methods to a defense logistics environment responsible for managing serialized materiel under conditions of high variability and low throughput. Serialized assets require strict identification and accountability, which creates operational complexity not typically found in commercial high-volume, standardized distribution systems. Defense logistics organizations must simultaneously maintain precise tracking and efficient flow to support readiness, creating a challenging balance between compliance and performance. The team partnered with a defense industry organization that operates a serialized materiel sustainment and fulfillment line supporting Department of War (formerly Department of Defense) requirements. The team used Lean Six Sigma tools to study how items move through storage, staging, and shipment preparation. These tools, often used in predictable manufacturing settings, were adapted to a high-mix, low-volume process characterized by irregular lead times, inconsistent documentation, and unique data constraints. The project focused on reducing variation, improving material flow, and strengthening the availability of serialized items within the logistics network. The full paper describes the application of the DMAIC framework and lessons learned from executing process improvement in a regulated environment with limited onsite access.

title: DSRF-SAR: Neural Radiance Field Representation Based on a Dual SAR Scattering Mechanism
authors: Yang Yang, Yueting Zhang, Jiayi Guo, Guangyao Zhou, Xia Ning, Yongchao Zhao
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) enables high-resolution imaging of the Earth's surface under all weather and illumination conditions. However, the high cost of data acquisition and the limited diversity of viewpoints restrict its application in data-intensive tasks. Neural Radiance Fields (NeRF) can implicitly represent scenes and synthesize novel-view images from unseen viewpoints. Existing NeRF-based methods have achieved promising results in SAR image generation. However, they often fail to capture the complex electromagnetic scattering characteristics and sharp geometric transitions. This limitation often results in blurring of target regions and the omission of background textures. To address these issues, we propose DSRF-SAR, a neural radiance field representation based on a dual SAR scattering mechanism. Specifically, the scattering is decomposed into two components: isotropic scattering, which depends solely on spatial location, and anisotropic scattering, which depends on both location and observation geometry. Additionally, directional interpolation is reparameterized by using the reflection direction of radar wave instead of the incident direction to learn a more accurate SAR scattering distribution. And the target surface is represented using a Signed Distance Function (SDF), enabling stronger geometric constraint and higher-fidelity scene reconstruction. Qualitative and quantitative experiments, including sparse-view interpolation and few-shot extrapolation, are conducted on the Moving and Stationary Target Acquisition and Recognition (MSTAR) dataset. The results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms existing approaches and generates high-quality SAR images of novel views, providing additional support for SAR image interpretation and applications.

title: A Transparent Conformal Miniaturized Slot Antenna for Wi-Fi 6E Applications
authors: Jinfeng Lin, Yejun He, Guodong Liao, Chaoyun Song, Yi Huang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Workshop on Antenna Technology (iWAT)
abstract: This article introduces a transparent conformal slot antenna designed for optimal visual clarity. It uses a polymethyl methacrylate (PMMA) substrate in combination with an indium tin oxide (ITO) conductive layer, achieving impressive electromagnetic performance while maintaining high optical transparency. The antenna effectively operates within the 5.76 to 7.21 GHz band, covering the Wi-Fi 6E spectrum. It features a return loss of less than 10 dB, a gain of bigger than 5.8 dBi, and a stable bidirectional radiation pattern, with a half-power beamwidth of approximately 60° in the E-plane. The antenna is highly flexible, maintaining its performance even when bent around cylindrical structures of various radii. Additionally, a metal back cavity is incorporated to significantly broaden the impedance bandwidth, extending the operating range from 4.6 to 7.23 GHz. This improvement enhances the antenna's versatility and effectiveness for a wider range of practical applications. The proposed antenna can be integrated in smart home systems and other applications that require discreet wireless connectivity.

title: Ensemble Machine Learning Models for Treatment Response Prediction and Adaptive Patient Allocation in Cancer Clinical Trials
authors: Umme Saima Tasnim, Shanjida Hossain, Md Mehedi Hasan
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Traditional clinical trial methodologies face limitations in predicting individual patient responses to oncological therapeutics, necessitating advanced predictive approaches for personalized treatment selection. To develop and validate machine learning models for predicting treatment response and implement adaptive randomization algorithms to optimize patient outcomes in clinical trials. This retrospective cohort study analyzed 2,156 patients from phase II-III oncology trials (20182024). Multiple machine learning algorithms including logistic regression, random forest, support vector machines, XGBoost, and ensemble methods were developed using clinical, molecular, and laboratory features. External validation was performed on 856 independent patients. The ensemble model achieved superior discriminative performance (AUC-ROC: 0.862, 95% CI: 0.8370.887) compared to traditional clinical scoring (AUC-ROC: 0.698, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{p}<0.001$</tex>). External validation maintained robust performance (AUC-ROC: 0.831). Mutation burden and performance status were the most predictive features. The adaptive randomization algorithm allocated 68.4 % of patients to optimal treatments versus 50 % with conventional randomization, improving overall response rates by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$12.3 \%(\mathbf{p}<0.001)$</tex>. Machine learning significantly enhances clinical trial efficiency and patient outcome prediction, demonstrating clear clinical utility for treatment optimization and patient stratification in precision oncology.

title: Proactive VLSI Congestion Prediction Using a Hybrid Residual U-Net with Squeeze-and-Excitation Blocks
authors: Hasibul Rahman, Zakia Sultana, Fabbiha Ishrar, Arif Istiaque
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Routing congestion is a critical challenge in modern Very Large-Scale Integration (VLSI) design, as it directly impacts timing closure, power efficiency, and manufacturability. In the placement and routing (P&R) stage of VLSI design, accurate congestion prediction is essential to ensure routability, timing closure, and efficient resource utilization. Traditional Electronic Design Automation (EDA) tools rely on computationally expensive placement and routing iterations to identify congestion hotspots, which prolongs design closure cycles and increases resource consumption. To address this problem, we propose a deep learning-based framework for early congestion prediction using features derived from the CircuitNet-N14 benchmark dataset. Our approach integrates RUDY, RUDY_pin, and macro-region features with ground truth congestion maps to construct a rich feature representation of circuit layouts. A Residual U-Net enhanced with Squeeze-and-Excitation (SE) blocks is employed to learn mappings between placement features and congestion heatmaps. Experimental results on multiple benchmark designs demonstrate that our model achieves competitive accuracy, with a Structural Similarity Index (SSIM) of 0.9724 and a normalized RMSE (nRMSE) of 0.0123, while significantly reducing prediction time compared to conventional EDA flows. These results show that our framework can serve as a fast, accurate proxy for costly P&R iterations, helping the industry accelerate design closure and reduce computational overhead.

title: A New Localization Algorithm for a Two-Layer Magnetic Sensor Array in Capsule Endoscopy
authors: Omid Yaghoobian, Khan A. Wahid
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Accurate localization of wireless capsule endoscopes (WCE) is essential for gastrointestinal (GI) diagnostics, yet natural peristalsis introduces abrupt capsule motion that challenges conventional optimization-based methods. We propose a magnetic localization framework that integrates a particle filter (PF) with a two-layer sensor array. The PF provides global search capability, reducing sensitivity to poor initial estimates caused by sudden movements, while the two-layer array improves spatial sensitivity and robustness when the capsule shifts away from one layer. Sensor placement and inter-layer separation are further optimized using the Cramér-Rao Lower Bound (CRLB) to minimize uncertainty within the sensing volume. Experimental and simulation results show that the proposed system—optimized for realistic magnet and sensor characteristics-achieves 2 mm position and 2.4° orientation accuracy across a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$15 \times 15 \times 25 \text{cm}^{3}$</tex> region. Compared with single-layer arrays and local-optimizer-based approaches, the method demonstrates superior accuracy and robustness under dynamic motion, highlighting its potential for practical WCE tracking.

title: Study on the Fluid–Structure Interaction at Different Layout in Wafer Mold Encapsulation Process
authors: Ainnur Hanim Othman, Mohammad Hafifi Hafiz Ishak, Mohd Sharizal Abdul Aziz, Muhammad Naqib Nashrudin, Muhammad Aqil Azman
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: This study investigates the effect of die aspect ratio on epoxy molding compound (EMC) flow and mechanical response during wafer-level encapsulation using a coupled CFD–FEA simulation. Two aspect ratios, 1:1 and 1:2, were analyzed by varying die width while keeping height and length constant. Results showed that the 1:1 configuration achieved faster mold filling due to lower flow resistance, while the 1:2 geometry exhibited delayed progression. Structural analysis revealed higher global deformation in the 1:2 case, but more localized deformation and sharper stress peaks in the 1:1 model. Overall, die aspect ratio strongly influences EMC flow and stress distribution, providing design guidance for optimizing die layout and improving encapsulation reliability.

title: Federated Edge Learning MAC Cross-Layer Optimization in 6G Networks : The Case for Radial Basis Functions Optimization
authors: Spyridon Louvros, Anupkumar Pandey, Brijesh Shah, Yashesh Buch
publish: 2026 Panhellenic Conference on Electronics &amp; Telecommunications (PACET)
abstract: This paper studies the impact of retransmission strategies on federated edge learning performance in wireless edge networks for 5G Advanced and emerging 6G systems. Hybrid automatic repeat request mechanisms are examined in multi device learning scenarios, where unreliable transmissions affect model convergence and latency. Retransmission selection is formulated as a global optimization problem balancing delay, energy efficiency and update reliability under uncertain channel conditions. A radial basis function-based optimization framework is proposed, showing improved learning robustness and communication efficiency compared to conventional approaches.

title: Decision Analytics in Climate Diplomacy: Pathways Toward Global Sustainability
authors: Muhammed Nawar Hussein, Abdulrhman Huseein Ali, Fouad Jameel Khalaf, Humam Mahdi Salih, Zeyad Khalaf Ismail, Marwan Mohammed Abbood
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This electronic Climate change is one of the most pressing global challenges, with profound economic, political, and environmental consequences. This study aims to analyze the role of climate diplomacy as a mechanism for enhancing international cooperation and promoting sustainability. The research hypothesis assumes that climate diplomacy is a key tool for reducing emissions, integrating climate policies into development plans, and fostering technological innovation. It relies on negotiations, agreements, and multilateral dialogues to balance national interests. The study concludes that its effectiveness remains constrained by tensions and limited financing, recommending stronger commitments, expanded climate finance, and deeper regional cooperation to ensure global sustainability.

title: Tea Leaf Disease Detection and Classification Using Modified InceptionV3 with Transfer Learning Approach
authors: E. Afreen Banu, MD.Asma, S.Dhivya, S.Nooray Sashmi, Yashika Bhaghal, Girish Kumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Disease identification and categorization of tea leaves are exceptionally vital in the management of the crop health and production in the tea farms. This research introduces an automated approach that utilizes deep learning, specifically a modified InceptionV3 architecture, to classify five distinct categories of tea leaf conditions. The diseases are Anthracnose, Bird’s Eyespot, Gray Blight, Healthy, and Red Leaf Spot. The study used a dataset of 517 images, aggressive preprocessing, and careful data augmentation to improve the performance of the model. The model architecture used in this work is a transfer learning architecture with some modifications, such as adding dense layers, dropout, and batch normalization, and it has an overall accuracy of 91%. The model was able to accurately identify all classes with F1 scores between 0.89 and 0.93; the model performed best in the classification of healthy leaf samples. The results of the evaluation using the confusion matrix and the classification metrics also indicated that there was very low cross-classification between the disease categories, and thus, the model is suitable for practical use. This research finding is very important in the field of agricultural automation in that it provides an optimal, precise, and scalable approach to tea leaf disease identification with the potential of minimizing losses and enhancing disease control in tea growing.

title: CertiChain: A Cryptographic Ledger for Immutable Academic Credential Verification
authors: Gimhani Samindika Dissanayake, Sudesh Chamara Jayathunge Bandara, Shyamali Saranga Karunadasa, Bevan Kanishka Liyanage, Lakshika Ruwani Samarasinghe
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Academic credential fraud is a pervasive issue undermining the integrity of higher education institutions globally. Traditional methods of verification, relying on centralized databases or paper certificates, are vulnerable to specialized forgery and internal data manipulation. This paper presents “CertiChain,” a cryptographic ledger simulation designed to demonstrate the application of blockchain principles to credential storage. Built using React and the Web Crypto API, CertiChain implements a tamper-evident linked list structure secured by SHA-256 hashing. The system provides an interactive visualization of the “Avalanche Effect,” allowing auditors to observe how a minor alteration in student data catastrophically breaks the cryptographic chain of trust. This report details the mathematical foundations of the ledger, analyzes the TypeScript implementation of the hashing protocol, and evaluates the system's efficacy as security control against data tampering.

title: Extending UTAUT for Blockchain Adoption in Power Plant Asset Management: Towards UTAUT4-BPP
authors: Miftahol Arifin, Elisa Kusrini, Winda Nur Cahyo, Imam Djati Widodo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: The escalating digitalization of power-plant operations has heightened the necessity for transparent, secure, and compliant asset management systems. Blockchain technology offers promising capabilities for traceability, auditability, and operational efficiency. However, existing adoption frameworks such as UTAUT, UTAUT2, and UTAUT3 do not sufficiently capture the behavioral and institutional complexities inherent in regulated energy sectors. This paper introduces UTAUT4-BPP (Unified Theory of Acceptance and Use of Technology for Blockchain in Power Plants)—a conceptual extension of UTAUT formulated to elucidate blockchain adoption within power-plant asset management. The model amalgamates elements from the Theory of Planned Behavior and institutional theory, while incorporating three novel constructs: Transparency and Data Provenance Security (TDPS), Regulatory and Compliance Alignment (RCA), and Operational Risk and Efficiency (ORE). Collectively, these constructs embody transparency-driven trust, regulatory legitimacy, and operational resilience, crucial factors for technological adoption in asset-intensive environments. The proposed framework delineates eight theoretical hypotheses connecting UTAUT’s core constructs with these contextual extensions. It proposes a three-phase validation roadmap comprising expert judgment, PLS-SEM testing, and agent-based simulation. UTAUT4-BPP redefines technology acceptance as a socio-technical-institutional phenomenon that links performance expectancy, regulatory alignment, and risk governance. This framework advances theoretical understanding in technology adoption research and provides practical guidance for utilities, regulators, and suppliers aiming to implement blockchain for sustainable and compliant asset management.

title: A Multi-Criteria Decision Framework for Selecting Sustainable Local Materials in Post-Crisis Reconstruction
authors: Sufyan Sufyan, Amin Saif, Farag Qasem, Aq Ahdal
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Post-crisis reconstruction often defaults to imported materials, undermining long-term sustainability and local economic recovery. Although vernacular materials can reduce embodied emissions and strengthen local livelihoods, decision makers lack a transparent way to weigh competing criteria and justify choices. This paper presents a four-phase quantitative decision framework that integrates the Analytic Hierarchy Process to derive criteria weights from experts and the Technique for Order Preference by Similarity to Ideal Solution to order prospective materials. The framework is demonstrated on a flood-affected housing scenario comparing bamboo and rammed earth. The analysis quantifies decision thresholds and shows how shifts in priorities—such as higher emphasis on initial cost versus durability—change the preferred option. Sensitivity analysis tests robustness and clarifies the drivers of each recommendation. By making trade-offs explicit, the framework operationalizes the "build back better" principle, enabling stakeholders to make defensible, locally appropriate material selections for a resilient and sustainable recovery.

title: Enhancing Bangla Sentiment Analysis Through Data Balancing and Preprocessing: A Comparative Insight from Classical and Deep Learning Approaches
authors: Md. Anowar Hossen Labu, Most. Tazfia Sultana, Md. Mahmudul Hassan, Md. Afridi Chowdhury, Adiba Mahjabin Nitu, Md. Arshad Ali
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Sentiment analysis in Bangla remains a challenging task due to the scarcity of labeled data, class imbalance, and the noisy nature of user-generated social media text. Unlike the languages with extensive resources such as English, Bangla lacks large-scale, high-quality datasets that can support robust natural language processing models. In this study, we focus on the SentNoB dataset, which contains 12,575 comments across 13 domains, annotated into positive, negative, and neutral classes. A key limitation of this dataset is the scarcity of neutral samples, which significantly affects model performance and generalization. Initial experiments confirmed these challenges, with Support Vector Machine (SVM) achieving 66 % accuracy and Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) reaching only 57.86 % of accuracy. These results highlight the adverse effects of both class imbalance and noisy textual features. To address these issues, the authors enriched the dataset with 2,000 additional neutral and 200 negative samples collected from social media, followed by extensive preprocessing that included text cleaning, normalization, stopword removal, and noise reduction. Retraining with this improved dataset yielded substantial gains, with SVM achieving 76.94% and BiLSTM reaching 78.37 % accuracy. The authors findings demonstrate that dataset balancing and robust preprocessing are critical for Bangla sentiment analysis and emphasize the complementary strengths of classical machine learning and deep learning in low-resource contexts.

title: Attention-Guided Invariant Feature Learning for Clothes-Changing Person Re-Identification
authors: Raisa Begum, Kaushik Deb
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Person re-identification is crucial in advanced surveillance systems, striving to identify and track a query person across cross-camera views, times, and locations within a gallery of images. The presence of occlusion, multiple camera views, background interference, etc., induce further complexities in the approach. Nonetheless, notable progress has been procured on conventional person re-id tasks inadvertently presuming that query's clothes are static during the entire retrieval procedure within a short time period. However, we cannot overlook addressing the cloth-changing aspect in case of re-id over a long period of time. Cloth-invariant feature learning is the key to withdrawing its bias while handling clothing changing Re-ID (CC Re-ID). To improve the reliability of surveillance systems, in this paper, we propose an innovative framework called Non-Cloth Relevant Feature Mining (NCR-FM) to decouple clothing information and ID-inherent details in order to secure robust re-id of individuals regardless of alterations in their clothing. Separate feature mining modules are employed to squeeze the precise identity and clothing information. The mined pieces of information are further polished and disjointed by aiding feature blending and level-based pyramid approaches followed by specialized classifiers. The end-to-end collective training with the weighted sum of the multi-functional criterion functions gradually strips the clothing bias from potential biometric clues. Extensive experiments on publicly available benchmark cloth-changing dataset, PRCC further validates the disentanglement potency of the proposed framework over the recent approaches, achieving <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{5 2. 9 \%}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{5 3. 1 \%}$</tex> of Top-1 and mAP CC Re-ID scores respectively.

title: Big Data Genomic Analysis and Understanding the Pathogenicity of Protozoan Naegleria Genome Through Insilico Approach (Karachi Strain)
authors: Rabia Faizan, Rafia Wasi, Fareen
publish: 2026 Global Conference on Wireless and Optical Technologies (GCWOT)
abstract: This study provides a comprehensive genomic and proteomic characterization of the Naegleria fowleri Karachi_NF001 strain, revealing critical insights into its pathogenic mechanisms, evolutionary lineage, and immunogenic potential. Phylogenetic analysis demonstrated a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$90-100 \%$</tex> sequence similarity with other N. fowleri strains, supporting conserved genetic lineage and regional adaptation. Eight Open Reading Frames (ORFs) were identified, with ORF135 being the longest at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6, 6 3 3 ~ b p}$</tex> and potentially encoding a protein of approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 4 0 ~ k D a}$</tex>. Conserved domain analysis revealed functionally significant domains, including nucleotide transferase-related (pfam10127, COG3641), cytochrome oxidase assembly (SURF1, Shy1), calcium signaling-related (pfam06449, pfam01365), and membrane interaction (MIR domain, pfam02815), all with E -values <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<1.0 \mathrm{E}-10$</tex>, indicating strong functional conservation. The protein-protein interaction (PPI) network comprised 232 interactions among 51 proteins, with a high clustering coefficient of 0.751 and key hubs involved in protein kinase activity (e.g., D2V5S4_NAEGR) and cyclic AMP turnover. Functional enrichment highlighted Reactome pathway involvement in heme biosynthesis and iron metabolism, KEGG pathway activation in purine/pyrimidine metabolism, and STRING clusters related to phosphodiesterases and transporters. Immunogenicity prediction identified multiple peptides with potential vaccine value, including one with high HLA-DR7 (Class II) binding affinity of 0.87 and several with Class I affinities ranging from 0.25 to 0.34; these peptides were also non-toxic and nonallergenic with immunogenicity scores <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>0.6$</tex>. These findings collectively advance the understanding of N. fowleri's molecular biology, support future epitope-based vaccine development, and provide a reference for targeted therapeutics in the context of Primary Amoebic Meningoencephalitis (PAM).

title: Predicting Enhancer DNA Utilizing $k$-mer Features and Multi-Headed CNN
authors: Priya Barua, Faisal Ahmed, Muhammad Anwarul Azim, Mohammad Khairul Islam
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Enhancers are non-coding DNA elements that play a critical role in regulating gene expression. Accurately predicting DNA enhancers is essential for understanding genetic regulatory mechanisms and their implications for health and disease. However, enhancer identification remains challenging due to the heterogeneous and complex nature of sequence motifs. To address this, we propose a novel DNA sequence encoding technique based on multi-k-mer features and a multi-headed Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) architecture that processes multi-scale features simultaneously with four parallel branches to capture complementary local and global sequence patterns for enhancer prediction. Empirical results on a test set show the superiority of our method, achieving 77.25% accuracy, 80% sensitivity, 74.5% specificity, AUC of 0.84, and MCC of 0.55. These findings indicate the effectiveness of multi-scale k-mer feature representations and a CNN architecture for DNA enhancer prediction.

title: A Comparative Analysis of Deep Learning Methods with Enhanced Feature Extraction to Detect Deepfake Images
authors: Fahim Foysal Abak, M. Morsedur Rahman, S. M. Mahedy Hasan
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The rapid advancement of generative models has accelerated the spread of deepfake technology, enabling the creation of highly realistic synthetic images that threaten digital content security, privacy, and trust. While deep learning (DL) approaches have shown promise in deepfake detection, conventional methods often fall short in robustness and accuracy. This study presents a comparative analysis of eight convolutional neural network (CNN) architectures, such as EfficientNetB4 (EB4), EfficientNetB7 (EB7), DenseNet121 (DN121), DenseNet169 (DN169), Xception (XP), InceptionV3 (IV3), MesoNet (MN), and NasNet (NN) for deepfake image detection. A large-scale Kaggle dataset derived from the OpenForensics benchmark is utilized for training and evaluation. To enhance discriminative feature learning, preprocessing techniques such as Local Binary Patterns (LBP) and Discrete Wavelet Transform (DWT) are applied, alongside a Convolutional Block Attention Module (CBAM) for adaptive attention. Experimental results demonstrate that EfficientNetB7 achieves the highest performance and outperforms other CNN architectures, with 93.99 % accuracy and an AUC of 0.9812. These findings highlight that integrating advanced preprocessing and attention mechanisms with CNN architectures significantly improves the reliability of deepfake detection.

title: Assessment of YOLO Models for Real-Time Vehicle Detection in the Complex Urban Settings of Bangladesh
authors: Ha Meem Hossain, Pritam Nath, Md Imtiaz Uddin, Ishrat Jahan Eiste, Mahitun Nesa Mahi, Syed Nasibur Rahman Ratul, Md Naim Uddin Mozumdar, Asif Mohammed Saad
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: This study assesses the efficacy of YOLOv8 and YOLOv11 architectures, including medium (m) and extra-large (x) variants, for real-time vehicle detection in Bangladesh's complex urban environments. A custom dataset of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1920 \times 1080$</tex> images was compiled, annotated with 23 classes encompassing local vehicles such as Desi Nosimon, Leguna, and Battery Rickshaw. Data were collected using mobile cameras, annotated with LabelImg, and divided into 75 % training, 5 % validation, and 20 % testing sets. Models were trained at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$640 \times 640$</tex> resolution and evaluated using precision, recall, F1 score, mean average precision (mAP@0.5), and inference time. Results indicate that YOLOv8x achieves the highest <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{mAP} {@} 0.5$</tex> of 0.733 and F1 score of 0.681, with an inference time of 40.255 ms, while YOLOv8m balances accuracy (mAP@0.5: 0.709) with a faster 12.1 ms. YOLOv11m variants exhibit competitive performance but higher latency (e.g., 16.6 ms for YOLOv11m). Medium variants are recommended for real-time applications, enhancing road safety through autonomous driving and traffic enforcement. Qualitative analysis reveals challenges with rare classes, necessitating dataset refinement. Future research will explore additional models and optimize datasets to improve detection accuracy in diverse Bangladeshi road conditions.

title: First-Principles DFT+U Study of Orthorhombic Rivalry in Ferroelectric Hafnia–Zirconia
authors: Mochamad Januar, Min-Hung Lee
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: Ferroelectric Hf<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1−x</inf>Zr<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> (HZO) is a leading candidate for silicon-technology-compatible non-volatile memory, yet the microscopic origin of its polarization remains under debate due to the near-degeneracy of polar orthorhombic phases. Here, we employ first-principles DFT+U calculations to systematically compare the structural, electronic, and ferroelectric properties of the Pca2<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</inf> and Pmn2<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</inf> polymorphs across a range of Hf/Zr stoichiometries. Equation-of-state analysis confirms mechanical stability for both phases, with Pmn2<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</inf> exhibiting slightly larger lattice constants. Energetic trends reveal that phase stability is highly sensitive to Zr content, whereas the spontaneous polarization (∼56–60 µC cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−2</sup>) and wide band gap (∼6 eV) remain remarkably robust. This resilience originates from the fact that substitutional Zr modifies orbital hybridization but preserves the underlying O−2p to Hf/Zr−d charge-transfer mechanism in both polymorphs. These findings show that stoichiometry dictates phase stability, while ferroelectric functionality remains robust against cation ratio and polymorphic competition, offering symmetry- and orbital-guided principles for designing hafnia–zirconia in future memory and logic devices.

title: Post-Disaster Damage Assessment using Deep Learning Techniques
authors: C Valliyammai, A K Vaishnavi, Sri Vaishnavi Devi, D Vijay
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: The damage assessment is significant for the response and recovery after a disaster to avoid the time-consuming process of manually assessing and validating property damage before providing relief to affected people. To forecast the grade of damage caused by natural disasters, an efficient damage assessment system is proposed. The multi-class classification using deep learning estimates the degree of damage to structures with fundamental factors from the Gorkha earthquake dataset, including building location, age, construction details, and secondary usage. The features that are important in identifying building seismic risks are extracted using Spearman Correlation Coefficient, Extra Tree Classifier, and Mutual Information. The Principal Component Analysis is used to identify the important features that help in damage assessment. The deep learning models CNN, LSTM, and RNN are used to predict the damage grade of the buildings after the disaster. The experimental results prove that RNN combined with Mutual Information achieves $97.58 \%$ training accuracy and 99% testing accuracy when compared with other deep learning models, which helps in planning efficient strategies for disaster management.

title: Breaking the Trade-Off: A Floating-Gate Field-Plated Split-Gate MOSFET for High-Frequency and High-Reliability Power Conversion
authors: Yiming Zhang, Ran Tao, Dawei Gao
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: As power electronics push toward higher frequencies and power densities, conventional split-gate MOSFETs (CSG-MOS) face a critical trade-off between low capacitance and poor reliability due to electric field crowding. This work breaks this bottleneck by introducing a floating-gate field-plated MOSFET (FGP-MOS) that retains the removed central gate region as a capacitively-coupled field plate. Unlike prior arts requiring complex process modifications, the FGP-MOS is fabricated without any additional steps. TCAD simulations confirm that the structure simultaneously achieves a record-low specific on-resistance (0.41 mΩ•cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>), superior breakdown voltage, and 15.4% lower switching loss than conventional MOSFETs, while extending short-circuit withstand time by 16% and drastically suppressing hot-carrier degradation. This design offers a scalable pathway to high-frequency, high-reliability power devices without process overhead.

title: AI-Based Urban Risk Modeling: Leveraging Physics-Informed Neural Networks for Real-Time Structural Stress Simulation of a Structure
authors: Mohammad Arafat Alamgir, Dipra Barua, MST. Khadija Khanom, Md Shahriar Ahmed, MD. Fardin Ahmed, Md. Osman Goni, Mayaz Uddin Gazi
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Accurate and efficient prediction of structural stress in urban high-rise clusters is essential for seismic risk assessment and resilience planning in urban areas. This study proposes a Physics-Informed Neural Network (PINN) framework that embeds elasticity and strength constraints directly into the learning process to ensure physically consistent and interpretable predictions. A curated dataset of 2,500 samples with 11 engineered features was used to benchmark the PINN against conventional machine learning models, including Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, XGBoost, Support Vector Regression, and Multilayer Perceptrons. The results demonstrate that the proposed PINN achieved superior performance (MSE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx \mathbf{5. 0 9}, \mathbf{R M S E} \approx \mathbf{2. 2 5}$</tex>, MAE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 1.81, \mathbf{R}^{2} \approx \mathbf{0. 9 3}$</tex>), outperforming all baselines while maintaining consistency with the established physical laws, with physics-residual violations remaining below 3%. The framework shows potential for near-real-time deployment in digital-twin or rapid assessment workflows, although further validation on larger and real-world datasets is required. Although the study is limited by the dataset size and simplified material assumptions, it establishes a robust physics-grounded baseline and outlines clear pathways for improvement using expanded and real-world datasets, ensemble or hybrid modeling, and region-specific applications. Overall, the findings highlight the novelty and practical utility of physics-informed learning for advancing scalable and reliable urban risk management systems.

title: Automated Carotid Intima-Media Complex Segmentation Using Multi-Stage Deep Learning with Attention-Guided Feature Fusion
authors: Abdus Salam, Oishi Jyoti, Md. Nahiduzzaman
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Cardiovascular disease remains the leading cause of mortality worldwide, with carotid intima-media thickness (CIMT) serving as a critical biomarker for early atherosclerotic detection. Traditional manual measurement approaches suffer from significant inter-observer variability and time-intensive analysis protocols. This study presents a novel automated segmentation framework combining YOLOv9-based region of interest detection with a hybrid ResNet-50/SelfONN-UNet++ architecture enhanced by squeeze-and-excitation attention mechanisms. Evaluation on the CUBS dataset comprising 2,176 carotid ultrasound images demonstrates robust performance metrics: Dice coefficient of 85.32%, intersection over union of 74.41%, and sensitivity of 86.87%. The proposed methodology reduces false negative rates compared to conventional approaches while maintaining clinical accuracy standards. These findings establish a foundation for standardized automated CIMT assessment in cardiovascular risk stratification protocols.

title: On the Limited Availability Group Model for Multirate Poisson Traffic
authors: Marinos Vlasakis, Maria Kourtesi, Iskanter-Alexandros Chousainov, Irene Keramidi, Dimitris Uzunidis, Orestis Zestas, Ioannis Moscholios, Michael Logothetis
publish: 2026 Panhellenic Conference on Electronics &amp; Telecommunications (PACET)
abstract: Loss models for finite-capacity systems play a fundamental role in teletraffic theory, particularly for dimensioning multirate service-classes. Among them, the Limited Availability Group (LAG) model constitutes an important extension of the classical Erlang Multirate Loss Model for systems consisting of multiple separate resources that accommodate Poisson arriving calls. This paper presents a review of the LAG model with emphasis on clarifying the model’s assumptions and the intermediate steps required for the call blocking probability determination through analytical tutorial examples. Furthermore, the accuracy of the LAG model is evaluated against simulation results for small-capacity systems, demonstrating satisfactory performance.

title: Analysis of the Impact of Large-Scale Solar Power Plant Integration on Protection, Stability, and Short-Circuit Dynamics in the National Transmission Grid
authors: Mehdi Kolahkaj, Leila Abkar, Mohammad Khazabi, Esmail Fathi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Protection and Automation of Power Systems (IPAPS)
abstract: Large-scale solar integration poses significant challenges to protection coordination, transient stability, and short-circuit levels in transmission grids. This paper analyzes these effects through detailed modeling of multiple solar penetration scenarios and proposes a multi-level optimization framework that jointly addresses operational planning, dynamic security, and adaptive protection. Results show up to a 55.3 % increase in short-circuit current at the point of connection, reduced coordination time intervals, and increased rotor angle deviations exceeding 15° under high solar penetration. The findings highlight the necessity of adaptive protection and integrated dynamic security assessment to ensure reliable operation of grids with high shares of inverter-based resources.

title: Enhanced Visual Retrieval and Decision Support
authors: Abdelkrim Saouabe, Said Tkatek, Doha Banoui, Khalid Tizra
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: The exponential growth of visual data across digital platforms, industrial monitoring, and healthcare applications has intensified the demand for intelligent and interpretable image retrieval systems. Traditional Content-Based Image Retrieval (CBIR) methods, based on handcrafted or convolutional descriptors, have laid the foundation for visual search but remain limited by low semantic interpretability, domain dependency, and restricted adaptability. In contrast, the rise of Generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) has introduced a new paradigm that redefines image retrieval as a process of semantic reasoning, synthesis, and decision support rather than simple feature matching. This paper presents a comprehensive analysis of recent advances in generative AI–driven retrieval architectures, emphasizing their ability to bridge the semantic gap, enhance multimodal understanding, and provide explainable results. The review demonstrates that generative approaches consistently improve retrieval accuracy, robustness to domain variations, and interpretability of results, outperforming traditional CBIR in multiple application scenarios. Beyond performance evaluation, this work contributes to the conceptual unification of generative retrieval systems and positions them as intelligent decision-support tools for applications in medical imaging, industrial inspection, smart surveillance, and event understanding. The findings highlight a decisive shift from perception-driven to reasoning-driven retrieval systems, underscoring the potential for adaptive, transparent, and explainable visual search frameworks that can support complex decision-making across domains.

title: Design and Performance Analysis of Grid-Connected Microgrid with PV and Battery Storage
authors: Tonmoy Roy Akash, Md. Sabbir Alam, Md. Naiemur Rahman, Bivas Sarkar, Ahmed Al Mansur
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: This paper presents an analysis and simulation of a grid-connected PV microgrid system considering real constraints like environmental conditions and consumer load consumption. As demand for renewable energy systems continues to grow, grid stability and reliability are significant challenges. The grid-connected PV microgrid system and storage are considered as a continuous power supply. The developed microgrid system has sufficient capacity to meet the load requirements without interruption. The model also follows the defector industry-standard real and reactive power injection to the grid. The paper presents a detailed design of the system's components, including passive components, the controller, PV panel sizing, and battery storage. Several case studies were presented to demonstrate the system's control functionalities in various scenarios. Simulation results show that a 50 kW PV array with a 268 kWh battery bank can reliably supply the community load, maintaining grid frequency within 59.8-60.2 Hz and stable three-phase voltages. The proposed model provides a scalable and sustainable solution for communityscale renewable integration, highlighting both its technical feasibility and its practical deployment potential.

title: Word Sense Disambiguation: Resolving Lexical Ambiguity in Word-Level Translation - Tamil-to-English
authors: Sajishvar Mohan, Joshua John Thevathas, Jananie Jarachanthan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Word Sense Disambiguation is a vital task in resolving lexical ambiguity, which is one of the major challenges in NLP. In Tamil-to-English translation, this challenge occurs when a single Tamil word has multiple English meanings. The correct meaning depends on the context in which the word appears. This research constructs a robust WSD approach for Tamil-to-English word-level translation. The model utilizes Tamil-specific syntactic and semantic structures to improve contextual understanding. By incorporating linguistic rules and context analysis, the work aims to improve translation accuracy. The main contribution of the research will be that this kind of effort has great demand in low-resource languages like Tamil due to poor availability of NLP tools. This improvement can significantly enhance translation models. It also facilitates better communication and knowledge sharing between Tamil and English speakers. Compared to existing WSD systems such as Word Overlapping <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(71 \%)$</tex>, Cumulative Similarity (84.3 %), WSDPCS (78.3 %), and Google Translate <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(79.7 \%)$</tex>, the proposed hybrid model achieved a notable improvement with 82.5 % accuracy in target word identification and 90 % accuracy in gloss sense prediction.

title: Secure Searchable Attribute-Based Encryption Scheme for Multimodal Data in Satellite Internet
authors: Ran Hou, Jiayi Liu, Zheyu Zhang, Xiaofei Zhang, Zhen Xu, Yushu Wu, Zhiquan Liu, Yongwang Liu, Tiejun Wu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Satellite Internet (SAT-NET)
abstract: With the continuous optimization of data transmission technologies in Satellite Internet, the transmission objects have expanded from initially simple text data to multimodal data, which faces multiple challenges, such as privacy protection and efficient processing. To address these issues, we propose the searchable attribute-based encryption scheme for multimodal data in Satellite Internet (SABE-MD-SI). Firstly, by specifically extracting and quantifying the features of multimodal data, the fusion of cross-modal data is achieved, effectively enabling the fusion and protection of heterogeneous multimodal data in Satellite Internet. Secondly, the scheme adopts searchable encryption technology to allow ground or satellite nodes to search multimodal data in an encrypted state, thereby enhancing the security of data transmission in Satellite Internet. Finally, the scheme incorporates an attribute update function based on attribute-based encryption technology, ensuring that data access permissions can be dynamically adjusted as required while achieving fine-grained access control. The privacy and security analysis indicates that the security of the ciphertext has been rigorously verified.

title: Design and Development of an IoT-Integrated Automated Liquid and Pill Dispensing System for Real-Time Remote Supervision by Doctors and Caregivers
authors: Muhammad Al Muktadir, Md Shihabul Islam, Rabiul Haque Sajid, Ridwan Kader, Md. Abu Shoaib, Md. Faisal Rahman Badal
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Patients requiring long-term medication for conditions such as hypertension, diabetes, or heart disease often struggle with adherence owing to forgetfulness, stress, busy schedules, illiteracy, or polypharmacy. This non-adherence increases the risk of health deterioration. To address this, this paper proposes an Automated Medicine Dispenser System (AMDS) that improves adherence and patient safety and enables real-time remote supervision by doctors and caregivers. Unlike previous systems, the proposed AMDS handles both liquid and pill medications, employing a PID controller for accurate control of liquid dispensing volumes, achieving 99% success rate for the pill type medicine dispensing and a mean volumetric delivery ratio approximately 95 % for liquid type medicine dispensing, while providing timely notifications, dose confirmation, and cloudbased monitoring at a low cost.

title: AutiScan: A Multimodal Deep Learning Framework for Early Autism Spectrum Disorder Detection
authors: Md Mahbubur Rahman, Md. Abdur Rakib, Mohammad Mahfuzul Islam
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Imagine a world where it is possible to have a quickaccess screening for Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) through the use of a smartphone, taking advantage of the combined strength of a child's face and behavior. Instead of depending on the classical and time-consuming clinical evaluation, we introduce AutiScan a novel multimodal deep learning framework based on the combination of facial image analysis and the responses to behavioral questionnaires for the early identification of ASD. The proposed approach consists of hybrid ViT and Swin Transformer for concatenated feature extraction from visual and attentive BiLSTM network for modeling behavioral pattern. Through this multimodal fusion, this system is able to attain a test accuracy of 99.32%, which is significantly better than unimodal baselines (imaged only: 93.33%, text only: 89.5%). Implemented via a mobile and web application, AutiScan enables real-time, scalable screening and provides a valid tool for early ASD detection, and reasonable in combination with standard diagnostic procedures.

title: AI-Driven Multimodal Framework for Environmental Monitoring and Conservation
authors: Jayabhaduri Radhakrishnan
publish: 2026 IEEE Bangalore Humanitarian Technology Conference (B-HTC)
abstract: Climate change-induced environmental degradation, pollution, deforestation, and ocean litter require smart, scalable, and real-time monitoring systems. Conventional methods based on manual data gathering or fixed sensor networks are usually characterized by spatially limited coverage, latency, and inefficiency in high-resolution environmental analysis. This article suggests a modular and scalable framework taking advantage of Artificial Intelligence (AI), edge computing, and multi-modal data streams-satellite imagery, unmanned aerial systems (UAS)/unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), and Internet of Things (IoT) sensors together for combined environmental monitoring and conservation. The framework has six major layers: data acquisition, ingestion and storage, preprocessing and feature engineering, AI/ML model processing, deployment, and visualization with real-time alerts. It enables the classification of water bodies and vegetation with CNNs, marine debris identification using YOLOv5, forecasting of air quality using LSTM models in time-series, and classification of pollution levels with Random Forests. Edge deployment on NVIDIA Jetson Nano allows for real-time inference with a latency of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$65 ~\text{ms} /$</tex> frame and power usage of 6.5 W, guaranteeing cost-effective field deployment. From the cloud perspective, Google Vertex AI allows for scalable model retraining and inference in sub-second latency. Visualization dashboards created with Grafana and Google Data Studio provide minute-by-minute reports, whereas the alert system provides 95.2 % accuracy with response times less than three seconds. The suggested system showcases real-world relevance through several case studies and proves its ability to enable responsive, energy-conscious, and scalable environmental intelligence. The current work provides a foundation for future AI-driven conservation projects and the implementation of sustainable environmental policies.

title: Wind Power Forecasting Using Deep LSTM and Hybrid Quantum Neural Networks
authors: Insaf Ghouaoua, Mohamed Tarek Khadir
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Wind power has become a crucial source of renewable energy, yet its variability poses significant challenges to maintaining a stable balance between electricity generation and demand. Accurate forecasting is therefore essential to support efficient grid management and enable the integration of renewable resources. This study evaluates two advanced approaches for wind power forecasting: a deep Long Short-Term Memory model and a deep hybrid Quantum Neural Network. Both models were trained and tested on wind power data to assess their predictive performance. Results show that the Long Short-Term Memory model successfully captured temporal patterns and produced accurate forecasts, while the deep hybrid Quantum Neural Network achieved higher overall performance with fewer computational resources. The originality of this work lies in the design and evaluation of a hybrid quantum-classical forecasting model applied to wind power prediction, demonstrating its potential to improve accuracy and efficiency compared to conventional methods. These findings suggest that quantum machine learning could play a strategic role in future energy systems by enhancing reliability and reducing the cost of renewable energy integration.

title: Benchmarking GAN-Based Synthetic Data Augmentation for Task-Driven Handwritten Digit Recognition
authors: Jannathul Maowa Hasi
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: This study evaluates the practical utility of synthetic handwritten digits generated by four Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) architectures: DCGAN, CGAN, Conv-CGAN, and WGAN-GP. The models are assessed using both established image-quality metrics—Fréchet Inception Distance (FID), Inception Score (IS), and Structural Similarity Index (SSIM). Moreover, a task-oriented evaluation is employed, in which a lightweight Optical Character Recognition network (OCRNet) is trained on generated synthetic data. Conv-CGAN consistently produces the most realistic and semantically coherent samples across all metrics. When these samples are combined with the MNIST training set(real data), OCRNet's accuracy improves from an intentionally constrained baseline of 89.5 % (trained only on real data) to 94.1 % (trained on real and synthetic data together). This demonstrates that the generated data provides meaningful new information beyond the original dataset. Our findings show that conditional generative models with enhanced capacity can deliver both visually high-quality images and functionally useful training signals for downstream recognition. This work highlights the importance of evaluating generative models by their real-world impact and points to the broader potential of synthetic data in resource-limited machine learning settings.

title: EfficientNet with Channel-Spatial Attention for Accurate Brain Tumor Detection
authors: Nagifa Nujhat Easha, Mohiuddin Ahmad
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Accurate and reliable classification of brain tumors from MRI scans is essential for timely clinical intervention, yet the heterogeneous appearance of tumors and the presence of visually similar anatomical structures make this task highly challenging. To address these limitations, we propose a lightweight attention-guided framework that integrates EfficientNet-B0 with a Convolutional Block Attention Module to enhance both channel-wise and spatial feature discrimination. The incorporation of attention refinement enables the model to focus on tumor-relevant regions while reducing background interference, improving robustness across diverse MRI patterns. Evaluated on a benchmark brain tumor dataset, the proposed approach achieves superior performance compared to standard CNN baselines, attaining a test accuracy of 97.1 % and a macro-averaged AUC-ROC of 0.987. These results highlight the effectiveness of combining principled model scaling with attention mechanisms for advancing automated brain tumor analysis and demonstrate the potential of the proposed framework as a reliable and scalable clinical decision-support tool.

title: AI-Driven RF-DETR Decision System for Real-Time PPE Monitoring in Yemen’s Development Projects
authors: Ezzaldeen Al-Tayar, Mogeeb A. Saeed, Amin Saif
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Occupational safety monitoring remains a critical challenge in fragile development contexts such as Yemen, where construction activities are often implemented under limited oversight and scarce digital resources. This paper presents the development and implementation of a decision-support system for real-time monitoring of personal protective equipment (PPE) compliance, utilizing a transformer-based computer vision model. Building upon prior conceptual studies on trustworthy and explainable detection frameworks, the research advances toward a fully operational application through the deployment of the Roboflow Detection Transformer (RF-DETR Small). A localized dataset representing Yemeni worksites was created, comprising over two thousand annotated images across multiple PPE and non-compliance classes. The model was trained and evaluated under resource-constrained conditions, achieving high accuracy and robustness in detecting helmets, vests, and safety hazards, with precision and recall exceeding ninety percent. The resulting system demonstrates the feasibility of integrating AI-enabled safety analytics into low-infrastructure environments to support timely decision-making, reduce occupational risks, and strengthen safety accountability in humanitarian and development projects. The outcomes underline the strategic value of transformer architectures for sustainable safety management and contribute practical evidence toward data-driven decision-aid systems in engineering project environments.

title: Artificial Intelligence in Business: A Systematic Review of Applications in Leadership, Sales, and Management
authors: Sakher M.A Alnajdawi, Saed Adnan Mustafa, Mahmoud Alghizzawi, Hazem Alkasassbeh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) is increasingly recognized as a transformative force in business, reshaping how organizations lead, sell, and manage in an era of rapid digital change. This paper presents a systematic review of 132 studies published between 2015 and 2025, examining AI’s applications in leadership, sales, and management through the PRISMA framework. The findings reveal that in leadership, AI supports predictive talent analytics, adaptive digital leadership, and inclusive decision-making. In sales, AI enhances demand forecasting, customer engagement, personalization, and dynamic pricing models. In management, AI strengthens human resource practices, improves supply chain resilience, enables advanced financial risk analysis, and supports strategic planning under uncertainty. Across domains, three key themes emerge: AI improves decision-making accuracy, facilitates personalization, and introduces new ethical and transparency challenges. While AI consistently augments efficiency and competitiveness, its effectiveness depends on being implemented as a complement to human judgment rather than a replacement. The review contributes to theory by extending leadership, marketing, and management frameworks into AI-driven contexts and provides practical guidance for organizations seeking to adopt AI responsibly. It concludes by highlighting future research directions, particularly in generative AI, explainable AI, and human–AI collaboration.

title: HPSO-GO: A Hybrid Meta-Heuristic Approach for Multi-Objective Real-Time Cloud Resource Scheduling
authors: D.P.B.S. Lakshitha, H.M.K.T. Gunawardane, K.P.N. Jayasena
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Resource Scheduling in Cloud Computing has become an important NP-Hard problem due to the increasing heterogeneity of workloads and the strict requirements imposed by Service Level Agreements (SLAs). Traditional algorithms are computationally simple, however, they often fail to adapt to the dynamic nature of cloud environments. On the other hand, traditional meta-heuristic algorithms such as Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) can produce good initial solutions, but usually experience “premature convergence,” whereby the swarm becomes “stuck” within a local optimum. To resolve these issues, this work proposes a Hybrid Particle Swarm Optimization with Genetic Operators (HPSO-GO) algorithm for efficient cloud resource scheduling. In HPSO-GO framework, the population diversity is continuously monitored, and when stagnation occurs, genetic operators such as crossover and mutation are triggered to introduce diversification and enable the scheduler to escape local optima. The effectiveness of HPSO-GO was evaluated through extensive simulations using the CloudSim simulation toolkit and real workload traces from the Alibaba Cluster dataset. The performance of HPSO-GO was evaluated against six different baseline algorithms: First-Come-First-Served (FCFS), Round Robin, Min-Min, Min-Max and Ant Colony Optimization along with the standard PSO. The results show that HPSO-GO achieves statistically significant performance improvements over standard PSO, and that it exceeds the static Min-Min heuristic algorithm. Additionally, in terms of total energy consumed, HPSO-GO is by far the highest performer of all the high-throughput scheduling algorithms, consuming less than 156.26 kWh of energy to complete all of its tasks. Through HPSO-GO's use of an HPSO along with a genetic algorithm, the average CPU utilisation for HPSO-GO was also higher than for standard PSO <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(31.64 \%$</tex> vs 22.24%). The findings provide strong support that integrating swarm intelligence with evolutionary operators provides a viable solution for efficiently scheduling cloud computing resources in real-time for multi-objective optimization in dynamic cloud environments.

title: Detecting Fresh & Rotten Fruits: A Comprehensive Study Using Deep & Machine Learning Techniques
authors: Fahmida Tahmin, Md. Musfiqur Rahman Mridha, Sakib Imtiaz, Fatin Nibbrash Nakib, Md. Shahid Ahammed
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The global production of fresh fruits and the spoiled percentage pose significant challenges to public health, the economy, the environment, and food security. Accurate and efficient recognition of fruit quality, especially at the margin between fresh and rotten, is therefore essential. This study explores the application of advanced Deep Learning (DL) and Machine Learning (ML) techniques for the automated classification of fresh and rotten fruits. Several pretrained DL models as feature extractors, including members of the DenseNet family, Xception, and ResNet50V2, are evaluated alongside ML classifiers such as Support Vector Machine (SVM), XGBoost, Random Forest (RF), and a custom DL-based classifier. Experiments are conducted on a publicly available Kaggle dataset comprising two classes: fresh and rotten. The primary contribution of this work is the demonstration of superior performance by the DenseNet169SVM model in binary fruit classification, which achieves the highest accuracy of 99.76 % with the lowest error rate. The findings highlight the potential of DL-ML models for reliable fruit quality assessment, contributing to enhanced food safety, waste reduction, and supply chain optimization. The proposed approach can be extended to other perishable food categories for broader food quality monitoring. Future work may involve real-time deployment and integration with smart agricultural or retail systems.

title: High-Performance Brain Tumor Classification from MRI Using Transfer Learning with Feature Fusion and Attention
authors: Md. Johir Uddin Bhuiyan, Habiba Akter Rimi, Md Asaduzzaman, Hashibul Ahsan Shoaib, Sheikh Razia Sultana, Anika Sayeema
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Brain tumor classification from Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) plays a critical role in clinical diagnosis and treatment planning, yet manual interpretation is often time-consuming and prone to variability. While recent deep learning methods have improved classification accuracy, many approaches depend solely on final-layer features, which may neglect subtle tumor characteristics. To address this challenge, we propose a novel framework that integrates transfer learning with feature fusion and attention mechanisms for high-performance brain tumor classification. The model combines intermediate and deep feature representations to capture both local and global tumor patterns, while channel-wise attention enhances focus on diagnostically relevant features. Experiments on a publicly available brain tumor MRI dataset demonstrate that the proposed model achieves superior results compared to established baselines, reaching an overall accuracy of 96.8 %, F1-score of 96.6 %, and ROC-AUC of 98.8 %. In addition, the framework maintains competitive computational efficiency, with inference times comparable to lightweight architectures. These findings highlight the potential of the proposed approach to provide reliable, accurate, and efficient diagnostic support in clinical settings, offering a practical step toward integrating AI-based systems into neuro-oncology workflows.

title: Big Data-Driven Approach to Customer Churn Prediction in the Telecom Industry
authors: Mishel Rossmaree, Achala Aponso
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Customer churn prediction is a major challenge for the telecommunications industry, where attrition rates often exceed 25 % and directly impact revenue. This study proposes a scalable churn prediction pipeline built on Apache Spark and Hadoop Distributed File System (HDFS), containerized using Docker to ensure reproducibility and deployment portability. Using the widely adopted Telco Customer Churn dataset, three machine learning models: Logistic Regression, Decision Tree, and Random Forest were trained and evaluated. Among them, Random Forest achieved the best overall performance with an accuracy of 75.86 % and recall of 0.76, while Logistic Regression recorded the highest F1 score (0.74). All models trained in under eight seconds, confirming the system's computational efficiency. The pipeline integrates feature engineering, imbalance-aware evaluation using ROC-AUC and precision-recall curves, and model interpretation through feature importance. By emphasizing scalability, automation, and interpretability, this work demonstrates a deployment-ready framework for telecom churn analytics and outlines future directions such as hyperparameter optimization, real-time streaming, and explainable AI integration.

title: Development of a Flip-Chip Bonding Process for Scalable Silicon-Based Quantum Processors
authors: V. K. H. Van, C. Bohémier, S. S. Ramirez, R. Wreyford, A. Dash, A. Dickie, S. A. Yianni, F. E. Hudson, W. H. Lim, A. S. Dzurak, K. W. Chan
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: The scalability of quantum processors relies on the efficient integration of control infrastructure with qubit devices. Flip-chip bonding (FCB) offers a compact and high-density interconnect solution for interconnecting quantum devices with cryogenic control electronics. In this work, we develop and demonstrate an FCB process compatible with silicon metal–oxide–semiconductor (SiMOS) structures. Indium micro-bumps were used to assemble SiMOS daisy chain and quantum dot (QD) devices, and the bonding integrity was evaluated through I—V measurements, electron microscopy, finite element simulations, and quantum transport spectroscopy. The developed FCB recipe provides a path toward scalable quantum processor architectures using standard CMOS-compatible platforms.

title: MOOD-TUNES: A Music & Movie Recommendation System Based on People's Facial Expressions and Hand Gestures
authors: Md. Jisan Ahmed, Sajadul Islam Akash, Isha Das, Md. Shahriar Hussain
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: In this digital era, music or movies are essential part of our day to day life. They are very powerful in expressing emotion, distressing, and communication with society and culture. However, people have problems finding appropriate music and movies that go with their moods. That's why we have prepared a project for you that, while innovative, makes it easy by creating a recommendation system that provides perfect content based on your mood via facial expressions and hand gestures. Our Project reads expressions and gestures in realtime, and we have developed it with deep learning approaches integrating two different models. One is the traditional CNN model, and another is the CNN model with the MediaPipe pipeline. The training accuracy of Model-1(CNN) is 86%, and the validation accuracy is 84%. The training accuracy of Model2 (CNN with MediaPipe) is 98%. We have integrated these two models into a website named “Mood-Tunes.” We also trained an attentional neural networks model but implemented the first two models mentioned above. Our models will help users find their preferred movie or music according to their mood.

title: A Performance Comparison of Machine Learning Techniques for Cholesterol Prediction
authors: Mishaal Ahmed, Muhammad Ajmal Naz, Manzar Ahmed, Mohiuddin Bukhari, Asif Nawaz, Ibrar-ul-Haque
publish: 2026 Global Conference on Wireless and Optical Technologies (GCWOT)
abstract: Hypercholesterolemia is an important in cardiovascular disease and it has high risk, but it remains undetected due to unavailability of modern equipment in laboratory for proper screening tests.
