The proposed algorithm is validated through a numerical example and a mass–spring–damper system. The results and findings show that the proposed algorithm can effectively address the problem caused by asymmetric measurement noise. It can accurately identify the model parameters and suppress the influence of the asymmetric noise.

title: Polarity Effects of Air Discharge at High Altitude Under Steep-Front Voltage Excitation
authors: Bo Zhu, Hao Zhou, Xi-Mu Han, Xiang-Jie Ma
publish: IEEE Transactions on Plasma Science
abstract: To investigate the influence of voltage polarity on air discharge characteristics of electric aircraft operating in low-temperature and subatmospheric environments under steep-front voltage excitation, this study primarily employs numerical simulation, supplemented by experiments to observe key discharge phenomena. A gas discharge model of a needle–plate electrode configuration is established on a multiphysics simulation platform, and three sets of Helmholtz equations are introduced to describe the photoionization process. To validate the simulation results, a streamer-discharge experimental platform capable of low-temperature and subatmospheric operation is constructed, and discharge images under different polarity conditions are recorded using an ICCD high-speed camera. The results show that, for both polarities, the electric-field strength exhibits a temporal evolution characterized by an initial increase, subsequent decrease, and a secondary rise, and the discharge velocity increases with altitude. Electron density decreases as altitude increases, and its spatial distribution differs significantly between the two polarities: under positive polarity, electrons concentrate near the electrode surface, whereas under negative polarity, the high-density region shifts away from the needle tip. The streamer propagation velocity generally follows a pattern in which the mid-stage develops faster than the early stage, and the late stage is slower than the mid-stage; however, at high altitudes, the late-stage velocity may exceed that of the mid-stage, indicating a distinct evolutionary trend.

title: A detailed analysis and design of a 1kV piezo-driver with precise fast-time response for hydraulic system
authors: Nassim Rayane Bennacer, Christian Martin, Loris Pace, Pascal Bevilacqua
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: This paper presents the study of a 1kV DC/AC unipolar converter designed to drive piezoelectric actuators with a precise time response of 1ms, used in a hydraulic system. The selected topology includes a PWM current control to adjust the time response and enables energy recovery from the actuator. This paper presents a detailed analysis, design, and control strategy of the proposed topology. Simulation and experimental results are also included in this work to evaluate time response accuracy, alongside efficiency measurements and loss estimation of the DC/AC converter. The experimental results demonstrate a time response adjustment accuracy of 96.52%(corresponding to 1.036ms), with loss estimation accuracy of 99.04%.

title: SelfBlending: Artificial Intelligence-driven Augmentation with Hand Interactions for Seamless Reality Blending in Virtual Environments
authors: Ahmed Elsharkawy, Bocheon Gim, Aya Ataya, SeungJun Kim
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Accessing real-world objects during immersive virtual reality (VR) experiences remains challenging, as current cross-reality systems often rely on predefined interaction steps, tracking devices/markers, or fixed object setups. They also lack support for personalized object recall, where users can add, remove, or modify real-world items blended into the virtual environment (VE). Many head-mounted displays (HMDs) include passthrough technology to switch between virtual and real worlds, but it often disrupts immersion by requiring a full shift from virtual to real. Thus, maintaining an optimal balance between virtuality and reality is difficult. To address these challenges, we developed SelfBlending, a framework that uses AI-based hand tracking to let users label physical objects through freehand gestures, then blends the selected item into the VE using object recognition, enabling interaction with the relevant real-world object. SelfBlending was evaluated against two common interaction conditions: the default passthrough feature in VR HMDs and the conventional approach of physically removing the HMD to access real-world objects. Results from seated, single-object interactions with tabletop-placed items showed that SelfBlending enhanced user experience by boosting presence, supporting efficient physical interaction, and improving cross-reality continuity. It also enabled selective interaction with real objects while minimizing the disruption of VR experience.

title: Critical End-Effect at Sea-to-Land Transition of Three Core Submarine Cables
authors: W. Poradowski, H. Brakelmann, G.J. Anders, T. Dong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Delivery
abstract: At the sea-to-land transition or offshore interfaces, three-core submarine cables are separated into individual single-core cables arranged in a flat configuration. This separation, combined with protective layers over metallic sheaths that impart semiconductive properties and cause spatial bonding, results in localized increases in circulating sheath currents in the submarine portion of the installation. Despite potential thermal consequences in offshore cable projects, this specific issue has received limited attention in the existing literature; consequently, this paper presents a novel examination of this end-effect phenomenon. An analytical model is developed in the paper and cross-validated with a comprehensive three-dimensional Finite Element Method (FEM) model and PI-section simulations, which represent fundamentally different approaches. The study addresses practical mitigation strategies for end effects in submarine cable installations and addresses a critical gap in existing methods for rating calculations of submarine power cables.

title:
Retention Characteristics in HfO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    -Based FeRAM: Degradation Mechanisms and Lifetime Prediction
authors: Wanmei Zhang, Wenhao Liu, Xingcheng Jin, Chongyong Guo, Hongbo Li, Yutai Man, Fei Yu, Peiyuan Du, Jiayi Zhao, Jiabao Ye, Junru Qu, Keyang Zhu, Hongrui Zhang, Huan Liu, Wei Mao, Jiuren Zhou, Ran Cheng, Yan Liu, Bing Chen, Xiao Yu, Genquan Han
publish: 2026 IEEE International Reliability Physics Symposium (IRPS)
abstract: This study conducts a systematic investigation of the retention characteristics of HfO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>-based ferroelectric random-access memory (FeRAM) in both planar and 3D-trench structures. The retention characteristics are evaluated under various voltages and temperatures in different states, including initial wake-up and fatigue. Two dominant degradation mechanisms are identified: reversible degradation caused by thermally assisted polarization switching and long-term degradation due to charge injection. In addition, a retention lifetime prediction is conducted. For the first time, it is confirmed that Arrhenius-based modeling is applicable to FeRAM retention prediction at the wafer scale.

title: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics Information for Authors
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics

title: Graph Neural Network-based Detection of Man-in-the-Middle Attacks
authors: Awatef Khoury, Avi Mendelson, Ori Shacham-Barr
publish: 2026 IEEE International Systems Conference (SysCon)
abstract: Man-in-the-Middle (MitM) attacks occur when an adversary intercepts and manipulates communication between legitimate entities, posing a serious threat to network confidentiality and integrity. These attacks remain challenging to detect and mitigate, particularly in scenarios involving multiple MitM nodes. This paper introduces a detection and localization method that leverages the Address Resolution Protocol (ARP) and a Graph Neural Network (GNN)-based model to accurately identify MitM nodes. The proposed method represents network communication as a graph comprising IP-MAC address relationships and intermediate connection nodes, enabling the GNN to learn both traffic patterns and structural anomalies indicative of MitM activity. Experimental evaluations on real and simulated datasets demonstrate that the system achieves an F1 score exceeding 99 percent, outperforming existing methods in detection accuracy and introducing a previously unexplored capability-precise attacker attribution.

title: Design and Integration of an Intelligent Battery Management System for Low Voltage Redox Flow Battery with Solar Interface
authors: S Hemavathi, P. Sanjeevikumar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Systems Conference (SysCon)
abstract: Redox flow batteries (RFBs) are receiving increasing attention as reliable solutions for long-duration energy storage, particularly integrated with renewable energy sources. Unlike conventional battery systems, low-voltage RFB stacks operate over narrow voltage ranges and exhibit strong electrothermal hydraulic coupling, which poses unique challenges for battery management system (BMS) design and accurate state estimation. The paper presents the design and experimental validation of an intelligent BMS specifically developed for lowvoltage RFB stacks operating over an extended voltage range of $8-28 \mathrm{~V}$, supporting both 12 V and 24 V configurations. The proposed system integrates a bidirectional buck-boost DC power converter with a solar photovoltaic (PV) maximum power point tracking (MPPT) unit, a multi-domain sensing architecture, and a 32-bit embedded controller capable of executing hybrid state-of-charge (SOC) and state-of-health (SOH) estimation algorithms. SOC for validation was established using controlled current integration with voltage correction, enabling robust monitoring under dynamic operating conditions. Experimental results demonstrate consistent MPPT convergence, stable charge-discharge behavior, SOC estimation errors within $\pm \mathbf{5} \boldsymbol{\%}$, and round-trip energy efficiency exceeding 90%. These results indicate that the proposed BMS provides a compact, scalable, and effective solution for solar-coupled low-voltage RFB systems.

title: Automated Mode Locking in an All-Normal-Dispersion Fiber Laser Using Polarization Feedback.
authors: Ebin Joseph, Simon Tremblay, Hanan Anis
publish: IEEE Photonics Technology Letters
abstract: We experimentally demonstrate real-time automation of nonlinear polarization evolution (NPE)-based mode locking (ML) in an all-normal-dispersion (ANDi) fiber laser cavity using polarization feedback alone. The system not only achieves stable mode locking but also distinguishes between fundamental (FML), harmonic (HML), and Q-switching (QS) regimes. The automation setup combines an electronic polarization controller (EPC) with a Raspberry Pi 4 (RPi). Output polarization measurements are used to calculate the first Stokes parameter (S1); its response to small bias perturbations provides both fitness and a regime classifier. FML is identified by a dominant polarization switch followed by a quiet plateau, HML by a polarization switch preceded by strong pre-switch variation, while QS is flagged by a low S1 baseline with isolated spikes. This technique is reliable, easy to implement, and low in cost, making it well suited for moving NPE-based mode-locking from research laboratories toward practical and commercial applications. In practice, the controller converges within a few closed-loop refinement stages: FML is typically achieved within two to three genetic algorithm (GA) iterations, HML within a few additional refinements, while QS requires longer stabilization using adaptive dithering.

title: Improving phase difference estimation by optimizing the condition number
authors: Yuwei Qiao, Datong Liu, Liansheng Liu, Yu Peng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Phase difference estimation is a core component of measurement and calibration in multi-channel systems. For two channels with the same nominal frequency, a joint sinusoidal model with seven parameters is employed to estimate the phase difference, thereby mitigating estimation bias from minor frequency mismatches. The parameters are obtained by solving the least-squares (LS) problem via singular value decomposition (SVD). Analysis shows that when the system matrix has a high condition number (CN), matrix computations are prone to illconditioning, leading to numerical instability in the iterative process. To alleviate this problem, this paper derives a closed-form scaling factor that minimizes the system matrix CN while preserving the LS solution, thereby ensuring numerical stability. Based on different amplitude relationships between channels, the CN under coherent sampling can be optimized to the range of (14, 110). Simulation results show that the ill-conditioned risk of the original SVD-LS algorithm increases significantly as the CN enters the medium-to-high range. After introducing the scaling factor derived in this paper, the CN of the system matrix decreases significantly, the iterative stability and convergence rate are systematically improved, and the LS optimal solution remains unchanged.

title: Design and Analysis of a Novel Permanent-Magnet Vernier Machine as an Electromagnetic Damper
authors: Congqi Lu, Fengbin Cai, Qifang Lin, Yurong Huang, Yuhao Wang, Xinhua Guo, Shuangxia Niu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: This article addresses the limitations of conventional electromagnetic dampers, such as low torque density and nonlinear damping characteristics, by proposing a novel dual-excitation permanent magnet Vernier machine (DEPMVM) integrated with a resonant circuit. The rotor of the DEPMVM combines spoke-type magnets and a central magnetic ring to enhance torque density (38.27 kNm/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>) and power factor (0.88) under motor operating mode. The resonant circuit compensates for the synchronous reactance of armature winding in the generator mode, improving the linearity of the damping force versus rotational speed. The flux barrier effect is further mitigated through split-teeth stator design. In addition, with the parallel flux paths, the sum of the dominant harmonic torques in the DEPMVM is increased by 26.99% compared with the conventional model. Experimental results indicate that under the optimal resonant parameters (R = 2 Ω, C = 0.9 mF), the fitting degree (FD) of the T-n curve reaches 0.953. Prototype testing reveals a back-EMF deviation of 3.9%.

title: MSFSNet: Multi-Source Few-Shot Adaptation Network for Cross-Subject Depression Recognition from EEG Signals
authors: Kang Wang, Yanan Zhang, Yingwei Zhang, Fa Zhang, Jian Shen, Bin Hu
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Depression is a prevalent mental disorder with severe socio-economic implications, and its early identification and intervention are crucial for mitigating disease progression. However, existing machine learning and deep learning-based approaches for depression recognition exhibit limited generalization across individuals, making them less adaptable to new subjects and restricting their practical applications. To address this issue, we propose a cross-subject depression recognition method based on Multi-Source Few-Shot Adaptation (MSFSA) using electroencephalography (EEG). The proposed method integrates multi-source domain adaptation and ensemble learning strategies. Specifically, the multi-source domain adaptation module employs an alternating training mechanism combining unsupervised domain adaptation and few-shot adaptation, reducing the model's dependency on specific subjects. Meanwhile, ensemble learning improves model robustness and stability by aggregating multiple model predictions, reducing the impact of individual model biases and enhancing classification reliability. Experiments were conducted on the public MODMA EEG dataset, comprising 53 subjects (24 patients with major depressive disorder and 29 healthy controls). With a theoretical chance level of 50% for the cross-subject classification setting, the results demonstrate that, compared with traditional machine learning methods, existing EEG-based depression recognition models, and advanced domain adaptation algorithms, leveraging the Alpha and low-Gamma band features as the key contributing factors, the proposed method achieves a significant improvement in accuracy, reaching 87.12%, which outperforms the state-of-the-art HEMAsNet (80.67%) and WDANet (70.94%) on the same dataset under the 10-fold cross-subject validation protocol. These findings indicate that the proposed approach effectively reduces subject dependency in EEG-based depression recognition and provides a promising solution for improving cross-subject adaptability.

title: Cost effective MFL NDT with Hall sensors for small ferromagnetic samples: penetrating defects vs thickness detection
authors: Vlad Marsic, Petar Igic, Iain Thayne, Soroush Faramehr
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Magnetic scanning non-destructive testing (NDT) methods are a real interest for many research and industrial areas. However, the already well-established popularized instruments and techniques for this area require complex, heavy and expensive equipment supported by specialist software and expertise. Consequently, building a system to test a hypothesis or enhance a current toolset may be discouraging for any adjacent research and industry areas with interest in magnetic NDT. This work demonstrates that a cost effective NDT reduced size magnetic scanner is possible to be created and exploited with a basic knowledge of electronics, programming and signal processing. The system is using the magnetic flux leakage (MFL) technique by means of Hall effect sensors and permanent magnets, at room temperature. The approx. 50 × 50 mm steel sheet scanning exemplifies two usage cases for the magnetic system: determining the sheet’s thickness variation from 0.7 mm to 3.5 mm and, the possibility of detecting holes as defects on a separate 3 mm steel sheet (i.e., from 2 mm to 5 mm radius). The system is non-motorized for simplicity and it is interfaced with MATLAB to illustrate data acquisition, visualization and analysis, all on the same software platform. Since the magnetic field analysis methodology used for both show-cases differs significantly, the parallel for the two investigations reveals the common and unique points of each. This approach is set to unfold the work set for a straightforward magnetic field investigation and set the expectations on the right path when using off-the-shelf inexpensive components.

title: Performance of an IRS-Assisted Underwater Optical Communication System With Energy Harvesting
authors: Himani Mehta, Rohit Kumar, Shankar Prakriya
publish: IEEE Transactions on Green Communications and Networking
abstract: Underwater Internet of Things (IoT) sensor nodes face critical energy limitations, as battery replacement or recharging is often impractical. Underwater optical wireless communication (UOWC) offers high-speed data transmission but suffers from severe impairments such as path loss, turbulence, and pointing errors. Intelligent reflecting surfaces (IRS) have recently emerged as a promising solution to enhance signal quality and extend coverage in challenging environments. This paper presents the performance analysis of an UOWC system featuring downlink energy harvesting (EH) at an IoT sensor node and uplink data transmission assisted by an IRS. A novel composite channel model, incorporating both direct and IRS-reflected links, is proposed and characterized for the UOWC environment. Closed-form expressions are derived for key performance metrics including energy outage probability (EOP), outage probability (OP), and ergodic rate (ER). Extensive analysis is conducted for various water types, demonstrating that the proposed IRS-assisted system significantly outperforms conventional setups without IRS, enabling reliable operation of IoT sensor nodes at greater depths.

title: Expanded Deep Embedding Clustering With Adversarial Learning and Adaptive Graph Constraint
authors: Xiaoyu Zheng, Shiyuan Han, Jin Zhou, C. L. Philip Chen, Yingxu Wang, Yuehui Chen, Lin Wang, Tao Du, Cheng Yang, Bowen Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems
abstract: The autoencoder (AE) is an efficient feature extraction tool that learns latent representations from raw data by minimizing the reconstruction loss. Building upon the AE architecture, deep clustering models are designed to jointly optimize the deep neural network and perform unsupervised clustering. However, existing methods directly impose the clustering objective on the latent features produced by the AE network, thereby neglecting the potential conflict between data clustering and data representation. Specifically, data clustering aims to enhance data aggregation, whereas data representation focuses on ensuring that latent features faithfully reflect the manifold structure of the raw data. To address this issue, this article proposes an innovative expanded deep embedding clustering (E-DEC) model, in which the AE network is employed to seek better latent representations, and a novel residual expansion module (REM) is integrated to construct an expanded feature space that better serves clustering tasks. Furthermore, adversarial learning between the soft cluster assignments and a prior one-hot distribution is adopted in lieu of the conventional Kullback–Leibler (KL) divergence, so as to enhance the discrimination of different clusters and avoid the degeneracy problem. Finally, an entropy regularization technique is incorporated to adaptively refine the affinity graph throughout the clustering process, thereby reducing the sensitivity of clustering performance to the initial affinity graph. Extensive experiments on real-world benchmark datasets demonstrate the superiority of the proposed model over state-of-the-art deep clustering methods.

title: LELN: A Large Language Model-Dynamically Enhanced Learning Network for Patient Similarity Calculation
authors: Zhichao Zhu, Bo Bai, Jianqiang Li, Han Wang, Rui Li, Lan Lan
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: The rapid expansion of Electronic Medical Record (EMR) data has advanced AI-driven patient similarity computation, a key technology for intelligent healthcare. However, the handling of heterogeneous EMR formats and the integration of domain knowledge constrain existing methods. While graph-based approaches show promise, they still struggle with these issues. To address this, we propose a Large Language Model-Dynamically Enhanced Learning Network (LELN), leveraging LLMs' commonsense knowledge and reasoning to dynamically structure EMR data and enhance medical knowledge integration. LELN in tegrates two LLM-basedmodules:DS-EE(DeepSeek-Event Extraction) extracts medical events to construct structured EMR event graphs, and DS-KB (DeepSeek-Knowledge Base) infers disease-relevant knowledge to augment feature representations. The model employs a dual-stage spatial-temporal feature aggregation strategy: a Graph Attention Network captures intra- and inter-event dependencies, followed by a Bidirectional Long-Short Term Memory (BiLSTM) with attention to model temporal disease progression. Additionally, a clinical prior-guided attention mechanism emphasizes discriminative diagnostic features, improving clinical relevance. Extensive experiments on heterogeneous datasets—a real-world Chinese dataset and public MIMIC-III—show LELN outperforms baselines, achieving F1 scores of 87.66% and 85.95%, demonstrating robustness and accuracy.

title:
Miniaturized PtSe
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    -based piezoresistive pressure sensors with enhanced sensitivity
authors: Ragini Gupta, Sebastian Lukas, Nico Rademacher, Michael Gross, Nikolas Dominik, Georg S. Duesberg, Max C. Lemme
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Device miniaturization has emerged as a key driver in the advancement of micro- and nano-electromechanical systems (MEMS/NEMS), enabling enhanced performance, reduced power consumption, and the integration of multiple functionalities into compact platforms. Platinum diselenide (PtSe<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>), a noble metal dichalcogenide (NMDC), has unique characteristics that are particularly advantageous for piezoresistive sensing. Membrane-based PtSe<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> piezoresistive pressure sensors have demonstrated excellent sensitivity in previous studies, although typically using large arrays of membranes designed to counteract low device yield. In this work, we push the boundaries of device miniaturization and demonstrate PtSe<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> pressure sensors with single cavities and circular channels. Our sensors exhibit sensitivities of up to 1 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-3</sup> kPa<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup> for a 42 μm cavity device and achieve high area-normalized sensitivity values of more than 2 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-6</sup> kPa<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup> μm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-2</sup> for 20 μm cavity devices without compromising sensor performance. These results highlight the potential of PtSe<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> for highly sensitive, miniaturized pressure sensing applications.

title: Decoupling Axial Strain and Torsional Twist in Polarimetric Optical Fiber Sensors through Stokes Parameter Analysis
authors: Ana Teixeira, Vinícius Piaia, Paulo Robalinho, Susana Silva, Orlando Frazão
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Polarimetric optical fiber sensors offer a promising approach for simultaneous measurement of multiple physical parameters, such as strain and twist, due to their sensitivity to changes in birefringence. However, their practical application is often restricted by cross-sensitivity between different parameters. In this work, we experimentally investigate the cross-sensitivity of a polarimetric sensor to axial strain and torsional twist. Two independent setups were used to apply controlled strain and twist to a 0.20-meter fiber section, while monitoring the output state of polarization via Stokes parameters. The response of the Stokes parameters S2 and S3, as well as the orientation angle Ψ and ellipticity angle α, were analyzed to construct sensitivity matrices correlating optical changes to mechanical inputs. A cross-sensitivity analysis was performed using the inverse of these matrices, enabling the discrimination of strain and twist contributions in combined loading scenarios. The results demonstrate a clear linear response and provide a quantitative basis for decoupling strain and twist, advancing the development of robust multi-parameter sensing systems. Therefore, this work shows the feasibility of decoupling strain and twist effects using polarimetric interrogation, supporting the potential of such sensors for multi-parameter sensing in practical environments.

title: Green Transitions and Financial Development: A Study of Emerging Asian Economies
authors: Ala Fathi Assi, Hayder Abdel Moniem Omer
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This study aims to identify and analyze the complex links between green transformation and financial growth in emerging Asian economies between 1995 and 2020. MMQR model were used to explore the role of green growth, sustainable energy, natural resources, and green growth in the financial development process. Based on the results, the use of sustainable energy, natural resources, and green growth appears to have negative effects on financial development in emerging Asian economies (β1 = -0.086, β3 = -0.010, β4 = -0.221). On the other hand, green technology has a positive effect on financial development (β2 = 0.000) because it recognizes the important role that environmentally sustainable economic processes play in achieving financial stability and progress. This finding highlights the challenges associated with the transition to greener methods in economies that rely on traditional energy- and resource-intensive industries.

title: The Influence of the Marketing Mix (4Ps) and Alumni Experience on Student Recruitment Decision: Evidence from Bahrain
authors: Eman Mahmood Ahmad, Meryem Masmoudi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: The focus of this study is to determine how marketing mix; product, price, place, and promotion, affect student recruitment in a university in Bahrain, and in this case, the mediating role of the alumni experience will be focused on. By applying a quantitative design and the data gathered on 323 students and alumni, the findings prove that product and the promotion are the most effective in predicting the student recruitment, price is moderately effective, and place has the least effect. Mediation analysis proves that the effect of the marketing mix is considerably greater when alumni are satisfied and engaged, which means that the credibility of the work of an institutional marketing is increased by satisfied and active alumni. The research will be of value to the academic literature in the marketing of higher education in two ways: it will empirically prove the role of alumni experience as a mediating factor, and it will indicate context-specific dynamics in the Bahraini sector. In practice, the results highlight the significance of incorporating alumni engagement in the marketing programs to reinforce the recruitment and institutional competitiveness.

title: Smart Detection of Lemon Leaf Diseases Using InceptionV3 for Better Crop Care
authors: Neha Batra, Deepti Thakral, Pinki Sagar, Tanishpreet Kaur, Ratneshwar Kumar Ratnesh, Manoj Kumar Yadav
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Leaf diseases on lemons threaten agricultural production; therefore, early detection is essential for effective management. The study examines the use of the InceptionV3 model in the classification of diseases in lemon leaves, i.e., Citrus Canker, Deficiency Leaf, Healthy Leaf, and Spider Mites. The data comprised 795 images, and a test set was reserved to test at a ratio of 20% of the total. Preprocessing was done by converting images to grayscale, resizing them, and applying Gaussian filtering, which was used to improve the quality of images to be extracted as features. The model had an overall accuracy level of 88% and performed well in predicting Citrus Canker and Healthy Leaf. There was, however, a number that were misidentified, including Spider Mites and Anthracnose, which need further refinement. A confusion matrix was applied to show the capacity of the model to identify diseases correctly, and a classification report showed that there was a recall and precision balance among classes. This paper has shown that InceptionV3 is effectively applicable in the classification of plant diseases and provides an effective platform in enhancing plant disease management and agricultural activities.

title: CNN-RSA Federated Learning Framework: Secure Deployment for Robotic Engineering Applications
authors: Rajvir Saini, Ravinder Sharma
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Secure and collaborative deployment in robotics requires advanced mechanisms that ensure both efficient In addition to a robust sense of safety. Deep learning and cryptography are two technologies that, when combined, can result in decreased system vulnerabilities and increased system resilience. Existing systems in federated robotics have a number of drawbacks, such as the fact that they are susceptible to assaults from adversaries, that they have a high communication overhead, and that they do not have a robust encryption system for model updates. Within the scope of this investigation, a Secure Federated Deployment Framework (SFDF) is suggested. RSA cryptography is utilized in this framework to ensure that model transfer and aggregation are carried out in a secure manner. Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) are incorporated into this framework to facilitate effective feature learning. The framework was developed with the intention of addressing the difficulties that were highlighted earlier and aiming to provide satisfactory answers for those challenges. The approach that has been proposed guarantees that federated training in robotic systems will continue to be resistant to tampering, scalable, and capable of protecting user privacy. The findings of the experiments reveal that SFDF significantly improves accuracy, reduces the chance of data leakage, and strengthens the overall security of the system when applied to situations that involve remote robotic settings.

title: From Prediction to Causation: An Interpretable Diagnosis of Procrastination Using XAI with ML
authors: Maria Afrin Khan, Anwar Hossain Efat, Mariam Sarker, Jubair Ahmed Nabin
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Procrastination is a pervasive behavioral issue, particularly among students, leading to reduced academic performance and increased stress. Traditional analysis methods often fail to capture its complexity, necessitating advanced machine learning approaches. This study introduces a Feature-optimized Explainable Support Vector Machine (FoX-SVM) framework to predict and interpret procrastination behaviors. Using a dataset of 674 responses from diverse individuals, we applied extensive hyperparameter tuning and synthetic oversampling (SMOTE) to handle class imbalance. Our dual-layer explainability approach integrates seven Explainable AI techniques—including SHAP, LIME, Anchors, and Integrated Gradients—to identify key features and enhance model interpretability. Experimental results demonstrate that FoX-SVM achieves superior performance (97.18% accuracy after feature elimination) compared to six other classifiers. The study highlights critical procrastination indicators, such as task delay initiation, and provides actionable insights for targeted interventions, contributing to both ML and educational psychology fields.

title: Highly Sensitive Novel HC-PCF Sensor for Detecting Harmful Food Additives in Terahertz Domain
authors: Md. Mahbub Hasan, Rabiul Islam, Md. Hasan Marouf Limon, Mahbubul Hasan Mehedi, Shak Mahmudul Hasan, Md. Motiur Rahman Tareq
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: This research proposes a novel hollow-core photonic crystal fiber sensor featuring a central circular core and bi-symmetric semi-circular cladding, specifically engineered for sensitive detection of the food additives saccharin, sorbitol, and butyl acetate. In the terahertz (THz) range, the sensor's core and cladding air-region parameters are optimized to ensure stronger analyte-field interaction and effective suppression of confinement loss (CL). The sensor design is modeled using the finite element method in COMSOL Multiphysics software (version 5.6). Key optical parameters, including relative sensitivity (RS), CL, and effective material loss are evaluated to assess the fiber's suitability for accurate and efficient detection. To exemplify the values of the optimized design, the sensor achieves the values of RS of 99.70 % for saccharin, 95.25 % for sorbitol, and 96.26% for butyl acetate, with corresponding values of CL of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.36 \times 10^{-13} ~\text{dB} / \mathrm{m}$</tex>, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5.58 \times 10^{-15} ~\text{dB} / \mathrm{m}$</tex>, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.01 \times 10^{-14} ~\text{dB} / \mathrm{m}$</tex>, respectively at 2.5 THz. The proposed geometry combines high RS, low CL, simple design and straightforward fabrication, making it promising for real-world deployment in food safety monitoring, offering a pathway toward safer consumption practices while supporting sustainable economic growth and improved food security in developing regions.

title: Ray-Tracing Simulation and Calibration for Indoor Hall Scenarios at 330–500 GHz
authors: Ben Chen, Ke Guan, Danping He, Muhammad Qamar, Akram Alomainy, Lawrence Carslake, Tian Hong Loh
publish: 2026 IEEE International Workshop on Antenna Technology (iWAT)
abstract: Terahertz (THz) communication systems are considered a key technology for achieving ultra-high data rates and low-latency wireless links. To support their deployment, the reliable characterization of THz propagation in realistic indoor environments is required. This paper presents calibrated ray-tracing (RT) simulations of an indoor hall scenario at 330–500 GHz using the CloudRT simulation platform. Material parameters are optimized through calibration against measured data, resulting in close agreement between simulated and experimental power delay profiles. The calibrated RT can accurately reproduce line-of-sight and first-order reflected components, demonstrating its capability to predict multipath behavior in sparse THz channels. The results provide insights into indoor THz propagation characteristics and establish a foundation for developing precise and standardized indoor THz channel models in future research.

title: Design and Development of a Multi-Modal Deepfake Detection System Integrated into the UnMask Mobile App
authors: Anonno Singha Ray, Md. Raihanul Haque, F. M. Rezwan Parvez, Md. Manirul Islam
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The rapid advancement of deep learning has made it possible for the generation of hyper-realistic synthetic media, commonly known as deepfakes, which pose significant threats to privacy, security, and digital trust. Most existing detection approaches available are computationally expensive and unsuitable for real-time use on mobile platforms. This paper presents UnMask, which is a lightweight and scalable multi-modal real-time deepfake detection application capable of processing image, audio, and video inputs with high accuracy. The proposed framework integrates optimized deep learning models such as DenseNet121 for image classification, a CNNbased architecture for audio analysis, and an EfficientNet-B0 with BiLSTM and attention mechanism for video detection into a FastAPI-powered backend. A corresponding lightweight mobile application, “UnMask,” was developed using dart programming language and Flutter, which enables both prerecorded and live media verification using those models. Companion website was also build for the users to get to know about the app's features and download the app on mobile devices. Experimental results demonstrate strong performance, achieving accuracy scores of 99% for images and audio and 73% for video detection on benchmark datasets. These findings highlight the effectiveness of the proposed approach and its potential for real-time deployment on resource-constrained mobile devices to enhance digital media integrity.

title: Underwater Image Reconstruction Using a Swin Transformer-Based Generator and PatchGAN Discriminator
authors: Md. Mahbub Hasan Akash, Aria Tasnim Mridula, Sheekar Banerjee, Ishtiak Al Mamoon
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Underwater imaging is essential for marine exploration, environmental monitoring, and infrastructure inspection. However, water causes severe image degradation through wavelength-dependent absorption and scattering, resulting in color distortion, low contrast, and haze effects. Traditional reconstruction methods and convolutional neural network-based approaches often fail to adequately address these challenges due to limited receptive fields and inability to model global dependencies. This paper presented a novel deep learning framework that integrated a Swin Transformer architecture within a generative adversarial network (GAN) for underwater image reconstruction. Our generator employed a U-Net structure with Swin Transformer blocks to capture both local features and long-range dependencies crucial for color correction across entire images. A PatchGAN discriminator provided adversarial training to ensure high-frequency detail preservation. We trained and evaluated our model on the EUVP dataset, which contains paired underwater images of varying quality. Quantitative results demonstrate state-of-the-art performance with PSNR of 24.76 dB and SSIM of 0.89, representing significant improvements over existing methods. Visual results showed effective color balance restoration, contrast improvement, and haze reduction. An ablation study confirms the superiority of our Swin Transformer designed over convolutional alternatives. The proposed method offers robust underwater image reconstruction suitable for various marine applications.

title: Ensemble Deep Learning Framework for Accurate Prediction of Gestational Delivery Week Using Electronic Health Records
authors: Anika Bintee Aftab, Md. Ashab Mohiuddin, Subah Nawar, Tasnuva Binte Rahman
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Accurate prediction of gestational delivery week is vital for prenatal care and clinical decision-making but remains challenging due to data imbalance, limited features, and reliance on invasive methods in conventional approaches. While numerous studies have focused on classification tasks—such as distinguishing preterm, term, and post-term births—regressionbased prediction of the exact delivery week has received little to no significant attention in the existing literature, and the few available attempts often yield suboptimal performance. To address this gap, this study proposes an ensemble learning framework that integrates deep learning models—including Feedforward Neural Networks (FNN), Recurrent Neural Networks (RNN), Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), and Wide & Deep Learning—with traditional machine learning algorithms to predict gestational delivery week from non-invasive electronic health record (EHR) data. Dataset imbalance was handled using SMOTER and controlled synthetic oversampling, followed by polynomial feature transformation and Bayesian hyperparameter optimization. Individual deep learning models achieved <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex> scores of 0.445-0.15, while ensemble approaches consistently outperformed them. Notably, Ensemble Model 2 achieved an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex> of 0.6454 with an RMSE of 1.6987 and MAE of 1.2764. These results demonstrate that combining diverse models with tailored preprocessing substantially improves prediction accuracy. The proposed framework is scalable, non-invasive, and particularly suitable for low- and middle-income settings, enabling personalized prenatal care and timely interventions for high-risk pregnancies.

title: A Time-Domain Differentiator For Time-Mode Signal Processing
authors: Angeliki Bertsatou, Alexios Tsetsonis, Aikaterini I. Chronopoulou, Konstantinos P. Pagkalos, Spyridon Vlassis
publish: 2026 Panhellenic Conference on Electronics &amp; Telecommunications (PACET)
abstract: In this work, a time-domain differentiator topology suitable for synchronous time-mode signal processing is proposed. The operation principle is based on the time-mode approach and the information is represented as the pulse width of rectangular pulses. The proposed topology uses a charge-based time register as a core circuit in order to implement unit delay tap and adder. Thanks to its modular, clock-synchronous structure, the differentiator can serve as building block in more complex time-mode systems. It is designed in TSMC 65 nm technology node, with 5 MS/s sampling rate. The power consumption is 137 μW under 1.2 V voltage supply. Magnitude and phase frequency responses match the corresponding response of an ideal delayed discrete-time differentiator.

title: Ground-Based Beam Position Estimation Methods for Low Earth Orbit Satellite
authors: Xin Jiang, Lei Sun, Wei Deng
publish: 2025 International Conference on Satellite Internet (SAT-NET)
abstract: Beam position estimation is crucial for the coverage and service quality of low-orbit satellites. Traditional beam position estimation is applied in the radar field and are specifically for estimating the spatial beam shape. However, low-orbit satellites focus on estimating the beam shape on the terrestrial surface. This paper proposes a method to estimate the shape and area of beam projected on to the terrestrial surface. Based on the principle of beam consistency in sine space and leveraging the straight-line propagation characteristics of electromagnetic waves, this method combines the half-power ellipse equation with the spherical equation of the Earth to solve for the coordinates of the edge curve of the projected beam footprint, thereby enabling parametric estimation of the terrestrial surface beam coverage. This approach precisely describes the projected shape and area of satellite beam, which offers new insights for addressing challenges in beam position planning, beam management, and optimization of satellite networks.

title: A Lightweight Federated–Ensemble Deep Learning Approach for Early Detection of Alzheimer's Disease Using MRI Imaging
authors: Sagufta Sabah Nakshi, Wahia Tasnim, Rabea Khatun
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The inevitable neurological disorder known as Alzheimer's disease (AD) demands an early and precise diagnosis to enhance patient outcomes and facilitate prompt interventions. Though centralized training raises questions about data privacy, heterogeneity, and availability across institutions, recent developments in deep learning (DL) have shown powerful abilities in extracting prejudicial features from clinical, neuroimaging, and genetic data. This study proposes a federated learning (FL)-based framework that incorporates deep learning models for early detection of Alzheimer's disease while maintaining patient privacy. The framework ensures adherence to data governance as well as ethical standards by facilitating cooperative model training across dispersed datasets without involving the sharing of raw data. Our proposed lightweight hybrid model is generated by combining transfer learning and feature fusion techniques, and the training of the MRI image datasets has been done in dual stages, along with fine-tuning. The hybrid model combining CNN and MobileNet achieves an accuracy of 99.98% on the training dataset, 99.96% on the validation dataset, and 99.92% on the test dataset. After integrating with FL, the proposed system has achieved 99.93% accuracy on the test dataset, which outperforms all the recent studies and also established a scalable, clinically applicable, and privacy-preserving solution by combining deep learning along with federated learning.

title: From Blueprint to Genome: A Multi-Criteria Decision Analysis Framework for Ethical and Risk-Aware Decision-Making in Genetic Engineering
authors: Hagar M. Mohamed, Mahmoud Khalifa, Amina Toumi, Shama Musabah
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: The rapid advancement of genetic engineering, from recombinant DNA to CRISPR-Cas9, has consistently outpaced the development of robust regulatory and ethical frameworks. This lag creates a critical challenge for policymakers, engineers, and scientists who must navigate complex trade-offs between innovation, safety, equity, and public trust. Traditional decision-making processes, often reliant on expert committees alone, can lack transparency, struggle to incorporate diverse stakeholder values, and fail to quantitatively integrate ethical principles. To address this gap, this paper proposes a structured Multi-Criteria Decision Analysis (MCDA) framework tailored for the evaluation of genetic engineering projects. Our framework operationalizes abstract ethical principles—Beneficence, Non-Maleficence, Justice, and Autonomy—into a actionable hierarchy of weighted criteria. We illustrate the application of this framework through a case study on a gene drive for malaria control, demonstrating how it can transparently evaluate alternatives and clarify trade-offs. By providing a systematic, transparent, and adaptable tool, this research aims to bridge the gap between ethical discourse and practical engineering governance, fostering responsible innovation in genetic technologies.

title: GlucoNova: A Hybrid Model for Non-Invasive Blood Glucose Monitoring Using Near-Infrared Photoplethysmography
authors: Tanim Ahmmod, Rajat Sarkar, Mohammod Abdul Motin
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Diabetes mellitus is a growing global health crisis requiring frequent and accurate blood glucose (BG) monitoring. Traditional invasive methods, while effective, present challenges of pain, high cost, and poor patient compliance. This study presents a novel non-invasive BG monitoring system leveraging a low-cost photoplethysmogram (PPG) sensor. The core innovation of this work is a hybrid prediction framework that combines a global polynomial regression model with an optional personalized model. This approach effectively addresses the issue of intersubject physiological variability, which is a major limitation of many non-invasive techniques. The system's comprehensive pipeline includes a MATLAB-based signal processing stage and an automated data synchronization architecture that transmits real-time patient metrics to a secure, cloud-based platform for remote monitoring. Performance evaluation demonstrated the superior accuracy of the hybrid model, yielding an outstanding RMSE of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.602 \text{mmol} / \mathrm{L}$</tex>. This marked improvement confirms that our innovative hybrid methodology offers a precise and reliable solution for non-invasive BG monitoring, representing a significant step toward creating an accurate, affordable, and user-friendly system for personal diabetes management.

title: An Efficient DRAM RowHammer Defense Circuit Based on a Novel In-Situ Monitoring Method
authors: Lingyun Hu, Yulong Tan, Yuansheng Zhao, Zixuan Shen, Yanqing Wu, Chao Wang
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: This paper proposes an efficient DRAM RowHammer defense circuit based on a novel in-situ monitoring method. A defense method based on dummy cell is proposed, which employs dummy cells with faster voltage decay to monitor storage-node voltage and trigger refreshes when they cross preset dual thresholds, effectively reducing redundant operations and bandwidth overhead. An in-situ monitoring circuit with adjustable threshold is designed, where a threshold adjustment circuit tunes the inverter’s equivalent switching voltage under PVT variations to enhance robustness. An 8 × 8 DRAM macro integrating the proposed monitoring circuit is implemented in a 28 nm CMOS @ 0.9 V, 200 MHz, achieving 83% bandwidth overhead reduction and 41% normalized area overhead reduction compared with state-of-the-art design.

title: A Tissue-Integrated Rectenna for Efficient and Safe Wireless Power Harvesting
authors: Raisul Ismail Apon, S.K. Tanchin Rahman, Md Azad Hossain
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The integration of radio frequency (RF)-based wireless power transfer (WPT) systems into implantable medical devices (IMDs) represents a transformative step toward realizing fully autonomous, battery-free biomedical implants. This study presents the design, simulation, and optimization of a miniaturized implantable rectenna operating at the 2.4 GHz ISM band, aimed at addressing the critical challenges of power delivery, biocompatibility, and electromagnetic safety. A multi-layered human tissue phantom model incorporating realistic dielectric properties is developed to assess in-situ antenna performance and ensure compliance with specific absorption rate (SAR) regulations. The antenna is geometrically optimized for low return loss, minimal volume, and efficient power harvesting under physiological constraints. Concurrently, the rectifier circuit is designed using harmonic balance analysis in ADS, with various diode models evaluated for maximizing RF-to-DC conversion efficiency. The proposed system achieves a peak efficiency of 88.7%, with SAR values maintained well below regulatory limits across multiple implant positions. These findings underscore the potential of the proposed rectenna architecture to enable safe and efficient wireless powering of next-generation IMDs, thereby advancing the frontier of biomedical telemetry and implantable electronics.

title: Multi-Instrument Investigation of Lightning-Induced Ionospheric Disturbances Over Bangladesh
authors: M.H. Rafi, M.G. Mostafa, M.N. Nahar
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: This study investigates the influence of both Cloud-to-Ground(CG) and Intra-Cloud (IC) lightning on ionospheric irregularities over Bangladesh during 2023. Lightning observations were obtained from the World Wide Lightning Location Network (WWLLN), Earth Network Total Lightning Network (ENTLN), and International Space Station Lightning Imaging Sensor (ISSLIS). Ionospheric parameters were derived from six Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) stations across Bangladesh. The geomagnetically quiet days of 2023 are calculated having a Kpindex <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<\mathbf{4}$</tex>, Dst-Index <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<-\mathbf{2 5}$</tex> nanotesla (nT), and also considering solar flares and earthquake events. The data from GNSS is processed through the GNSS TEC algorithm to derive the Vertical Total Electron Content (VTEC) data, which is used to compute the rate of the TEC index (ROTI). During the geomagnetically quiet days, the lightning was simultaneously detected by WWLLN, ENTLN, and ISS-LIS, and significant ionospheric disturbances were observed in ROTI values exceeding 0.15 TECU/min. Three representative case studies illustrate the typical response: On 6 March 2023, lightning peaks at 09:00 UT and 13:00 UT coincided with enhanced ROTI values reaching 0.6 TECU/min and VTEC around 120 TECU. On 3 June 2023, the highest lightning count of the year was recorded, with stroke peaks at 09:00 UT and 12:00 UT, during which ROTI reached TECU/min and VTEC rose to 80 TECU. On 1 September 2023, lightning peaked at 09:00 UT, with concurrent ionospheric disturbances where ROTI exceeded 0.55 TECU/min and VTEC was <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 80 \text{TECU}$</tex>. These results confirm that lightning activity is strongly correlated with ionospheric irregularities and plays a significant role in perturbing ionospheric conditions.

title: Senseoftense: A Machine Learning Framework for Identifying Tense Errors in Bangla
authors: Pavel Ferdous, K. M. Azharul Hasan
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Tense error detection in Bangla language is a significant task in Natural Language Processing (NLP) that aims to identify whether the tense used in a sentence is appropriate and grammatically correct. Bangla language has a rich and complex verb morphology that changes based on tense, aspect, person, number, and honorifics, making tense error detection challenging and crucial for many applications such as grammar correction, automated essay writing, and intelligent tutoring systems. In this paper, we propose a machine learning-based framework to detect tense errors for Bangla. We create a tense dataset that contains 20,030 correct Bangla sentences having possible tense forms of Bangla text labeled according to their tense, that is, past, present, and future. The dataset is created considering the syntactic variability, morphological richness, and ambiguity that may occur. The sequence-aware LSTM autoencoder and a Dense autoencoder are used to train the dataset for tense error detection. The LSTM model outperforms the Dense model and shows the benefits of sequence modeling. This work provides a useful resource and a baseline for detecting tense errors, especially for error detection in Bangla grammar.

title: HiddenGuard: Real-Time Detection and Mitigation of Steganographic Prompt Injection Attacks in Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Vamshidhar Reddy Parupally, Monisha Gottam
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) enable powerful cross-modal reasoning but expose new security risks. One such threat is steganographic prompt injection, where malicious instructions are hidden in images. Existing defenses either incur high latency or fail to detect sophisticated attacks. We propose HiddenGuard, a real-time detection framework integrating (1) cross-modal consistency analysis, (2) wavelet-based steganographic detection, and (3) context-aware threat assessment. HiddenGuard achieves 94.7% accuracy with under 50 ms latency, outperforming prior methods while reducing overhead by 67%.

title: Low-Power and Robust SRAM Design: Comparative Evaluation of 6T, ST-9T, and SB-9T Architectures
authors: Md Faiyarul Islam, Md. Rakibul Hasan, Tahmina Akter, Shihab Ahmed, Md. Hasan Maruf, ASM Shihavuddin
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: High-Performance Computing (HPC) depends on multi-core processors and optimized memory architectures to speed up AI, large data, and other powerful applications. These applications require memory systems that combine high speed, energy efficiency, and robustness under aggressive scaling. To address this demand, this work compares three Static Random-Access Memory (SRAM) architectures conventional 6T, Schmitt-Trigger 9T (ST-9T), and Single-Bit line 9T (SB-9T) and analyzes read/write delays, dynamic and static power, Static Noise Margins (SNM), layout area, and Figure of Merit (FOM) to identify the most suitable cell. Simulation results show that the ST-9T cell consumes the lowest dynamic power of 58.1 nW with lowest static power, which is 2.66 nW lower than the other cells, while achieving the highest Read SNM (RSNM) of 261.1 mV. It also delivers a 72.87% faster write operation than the traditional 6T cell and a 38.29% faster write than the SB-9T cell, resulting in the best FOM. Overall, the ST-9T cell is the best option for obtaining low power consumption with robust stability and noise immunity. The simulations and detailed data analysis were executed using EDA design tools with 32 nm CMOS technology.

title: Securing TLS in the Quantum Era: A Robust Hybrid Protocol with Kyber, X25519, and Dilithium
authors: Abu Sayem, Tahmid Enam Shrestha, Farzana Rahman, Md Asif Khan, Md. Asraf Ali
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The rise of quantum computers threatens the security of current TLS protocols, which rely on elliptic curve and RSA-based primitives that quantum algorithms like Shor's can break. This creates a “store now, decrypt later” risk, where attackers may collect encrypted data today to decrypt it in the future once quantum capabilities advance. This study introduces a novel hybrid TLS protocol that seamlessly integrates classical (ECDSA and X25519) and post-quantum (CRYSTALS-Dilithium5 and CRYSTALS-Kyber1024) primitives. The proposed method generates a composite X. 509 certificate dual-signed with ECDSA and Dilithium5. It conducts a key exchange that concatenates an X25519 Diffie-Hellman shared secret with a Kyber KEM shared secret, followed by HKDF-SHA256 key derivation. The entire protocol is implemented in Go, leveraging the OQS liboqs-go library alongside Go's standard crypto packages. Experimental validation via a proof-of-concept demonstrates successful hybrid certificate validation and secure session establishment with both classical and post-quantum peers. These results confirm that hybrid cryptographic integration can provide quantum-resilient security while preserving compatibility with existing TLS deployments.

title: A Lightweight Ensemble Network for Brain Tumor Detection from MRI Images
authors: Rizoan Toufiq, Zahirul Islam, Shovon Sarker
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Brain tumor detection from MRI images is a critical task in medical imaging, requiring a balance between model performance and computational efficiency. In this study, we propose a lightweight ensemble network that combines the outputs of a custom Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and MobileNet through two dense layers and a final classification layer. We evaluate the performance and parameter efficiency of several state-of-the-art (SOTA) deep learning models, including EfficientNetB0, DenseNet-121, MobileNet, InceptionV3, Xception, SqueezeNet 1.1, and a customized CNN.Experimental results showed that DenseNet-121 delivered the best performance among all evaluated models, achieving an accuracy of 97.54 %. However, this high performance came with a significant computational cost, as DenseNet-121 required <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7. 1 8}$</tex> million parameters (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7. 1 8 ~ M}$</tex>). In contrast, the proposed ensemble method offered a better balance between performance and efficiency, reflecting a significant improvement in the performance-parameter tradeoff. The ensemble network achieved a very similar, or comparable, accuracy of 97.42 %. The ensemble method outperforms lightweight models such as SqueezeNet <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(90.05 \%)$</tex> and the customized CNN <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(94.6 \%)$</tex>. Its performance is also competitive with larger models like InceptionV3 and Xception. These findings highlight the effectiveness of the proposed ensemble method for resource-constrained applications that require high diagnostic accuracy.

title: Digital Transformation in GCC Higher Education: A Decision Aid Framework for Institutional Success
authors: Abdulla Alkhaja, Colin Turner, Laura Galloway, Mohamed Salama
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This paper presents the Successful Digital Transformation Framework for Higher Education (SDTF-HE), a conceptual and empirically grounded model designed to guide higher education institutions (HEIs) across the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) in achieving effective digital transformation (DT). The study addresses persistent fragmentation in DT initiatives and the absence of coherent institutional strategies, governance mechanisms, and adoption pathways. Drawing on multiple case studies from seven HEIs across Bahrain, Saudi Arabia, Kuwait, Oman, and the United Arab Emirates, the research integrates organizational change, technology adoption, and resource-based perspectives. Findings identify six key capability enablers: DT Strategy, Governance and Leadership, Communication and Culture, Training and Development, DT-Specific Investments, and Ecosystem Partnerships, that influence DT outcomes through the mediating mechanism of Digital Adoption. The research demonstrates how these enablers collectively enhance academic quality, operational efficiency, and decision transparency. It provides actionable guidance for HEI leaders seeking to progress from isolated digital projects toward comprehensive, strategically aligned transformation initiatives.

title: Investigation of Post-Programming Characteristics for Charge-Trap Transistors in Standard 28nm CMOS
authors: Ye Lin, Jun Ma, Xiaoyong Song, Anying Jiang, Yuan Du, Li Du
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: In this paper, the Charge-trap transistor (CTT) family is extended to include Ultra-Low Voltage Threshold (ULVT), Standard Voltage Threshold (SVT), and High Voltage Threshold (HVT) transistors in standard 28nm bulk-Si CMOS technology. Measurements across 45 transistor types and channel widths (W) under various bias stresses indicate that W and transistor type have a minimal effect on the maximum threshold voltage shift (∆V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th,max</inf>). After the programming (PRG) operation, CTTs exhibit reduced low-frequency noise (LFN) possibly due to charge filling of interface traps, stable on-resistance (Ron) under identical V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">GS</inf> − V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</inf> bias conditions, and enhanced anti-aging characteristics. These results highlight the potential of the CTT family as fundamental components for nonvolatile storage and analog circuits.

title: Assessing the Relationship Between Service Quality Dimensions and Customer Trust in Digital Banking Systems
authors: Florensia Irenna, Rio Andy Bong, Welly Gusnaidi, Stefanus Rumangkit, Christopher Joshua Leksana
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This study aims to investigate the impact of digital banking service quality on customer satisfaction and its subsequent influence on customer trust in the context of intelligent technologies. Specifically, the research examines the relationship between various service quality dimensions and customer satisfaction, and how satisfaction, in turn, affects trust. The study employs a quantitative research approach, utilizing primary data collected through an online questionnaire distributed via Google Forms. A total of 312 samples were analyzed, with the sample size determined using SPSS Sample Power. The purposive sampling method was used to select participants. Data analysis was performed using Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM) with the SmartPLS software. The findings reveal that reliability not significant on customer satisfaction, while other factors such as service efficiency, service availability, responsiveness, security, privacy, and mobile application systems do not significantly affect customer satisfaction. Additionally, customer satisfaction was found to have a significant positive effect on customer trust.

title: Spatio-Temporal Hybrid Neural Network for Automated Classification of Batting and Bowling Actions in Cricket Videos
authors: Arnab Singha, Abir Hassan, Saadman Sakib, Kaushik Deb
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Automated cricket action classification is crucial for sports analytics, coaching, and performance evaluation. Existing methods often rely on handcrafted features or shallow models, limiting their ability to capture complex motion dynamics across diverse actions. In this work, we propose a deep learning framework that effectively classifies both batting and bowling actions by integrating spatial and temporal feature extraction. We extend the widely used CKT dataset to eight action classes by incorporating three additional bowling actions: Legspin, Offspin, and Fast Bowling, enhancing the dataset's diversity and representativeness. EfficientNetB0 is employed as a pre-trained backbone to extract robust spatial features from key frames, while a Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (Bi-LSTM) network captures temporal dependencies across sequences of frames. Evaluated on the extended dataset, the proposed system achieves a high classification accuracy of 95.89%, outperforming traditional approaches and demonstrating reliable classification of both batting and bowling action classes. The framework's efficiency and accuracy make it suitable for real-time applications in automated sports analysis, coaching assistance, and player performance monitoring.

title: Design and Analysis of Microstrip Patch Antenna with High Gain at 14 GHz for Satellite Communication
authors: Md Tushar Ahammed, Tofayel Karim, Mohammad Abdul Alim
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: This study represents an investigation and performance evaluation of a stacked microstrip patch antenna operating at 14 GHz within the broad Ku-band frequency range, designed and analyzed using CST Studio Suite 2019. The proposed antenna is designed on a Rogers RT/Duroid 5880 substrate with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\epsilon_{r}=2.2$</tex> and substrate thickness of 1.6 mm. An inclusion of quarter-wave transformer (QWT) and defected ground slot are incorporated to enhance the impedance matching, radiation performance and other related antenna parameters. Simulation results responses with a return loss (S11) of −37.12 dB, bandwidth of 1.014 GHz, a high gain of 7.403 dBi, directivity of 8.066 dBi, and radiation efficiency of 85.85%. These characteristics make the proposed antenna suitable for VSAT, CubeSat mission and Satellite communication.

title: Predicting High Fatigue Risk in Workers Using Machine Learning: A Comparative Study of Logistic Regression and Random Forest
authors: MD. Zubaer Rahman Sajib
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Worker fatigue is a major contributor to workplace incidents and reduced productivity. This study develops and evaluates machine learning models to predict high fatigue risk using a compact set of work- and lifestyle-related features. A synthetic dataset of 3,000 samples was generated to simulate realistic distributions of work hours, sleep hours, task load, break frequency, caffeine intake, and stress level. We compare Logistic Regression (LR) and Random Forest (RF) trained on both full and minimal feature sets; hyperparameter tuning was applied using GridSearchCV with F1 as the optimization metric. On the minimal feature set, the tuned Logistic Regression achieved an accuracy of 0.895, a cross-validated F1 of 0.739, and ROC-AUC 0.95; the tuned Random Forest achieved an accuracy of 0.878, a cross-validated F1 of 0.738, and ROC-AUC 0.94. Baseline ROCAUC values for the untuned models were 0.95 (LR) and 0.93 (RF). Feature-importance analysis indicates that sleep_hours, stress_level, and work_hours are the most predictive features. Results show that minimal-feature models, when tuned appropriately, give strong predictive performance with better interpretability—suitable for practical fatigue-risk monitoring in industrial settings.

title: Explainable Hybrid Ensemble Models for Agricultural Soil Erosion Mitigation
authors: Afzalul Abid Nazir, Jahir Uddin Mohammad Babar, Islam Saiful, Md. Faruk Abdullah Al Sohan
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Soil erosion is a major environmental and agricultural challenge, leading to land degradation, reduced crop productivity, and long-term ecological imbalance. Traditional assessment techniques often rely on empirical or rule-based models, which are limited in scalability and adaptability to diverse environmental conditions. This study introduces a hybrid stacking ensemble framework for soil erosion risk prediction, formulated as a binary classification task (HighErosion vs. LowErosion). Eight baseline machine learning models such as Logistic Regression, Naïve Bayes, Decision Tree, Random Forest, K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), AdaBoost, Gradient Boosting, and XGBoost were systematically evaluated against the proposed hybrid ensemble. The methodology integrates preprocessing, hyperparameter tuning, and interpretability techniques such as feature importance and SHAP analysis. Experimental results demonstrate that the hybrid stacking ensemble outperformed all baselines, achieving 98.07% accuracy, an F1-score of 0.981, and an AUC of 0.997. Key predictors included rainfall, slope, and soil type, while vegetation cover contributed secondary but significant effects. The findings highlight the potential of ensemble-based machine learning for developing decision-support tools that enable policymakers and farmers to implement proactive erosion control strategies, ensuring sustainable land management.

title: Development and Evaluation of Machine Learning Models for Diabetes Risk Prediction Using BRFSS 2023 Data
authors: Sarah Mokhtar, Jad Abbass
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Diabetes remains a major global health burden, with its rising prevalence demanding scalable approaches to early risk detection. Traditional clinical screening is resource-intensive, whereas survey-based modelling offers a complementary tool for population-level surveillance. In this study, we develop and evaluate machine learning classifiers on the 2023 U.S. Behavioural Risk Factor Surveillance System (BRFSS) to predict self-reported diabetes and prediabetes. Following a comprehensive computational comparison, three models—elastic net logistic regression, gradient boosting, and XGBoost—were selected for hyperparameter optimisation, probability calibration, and recall-oriented threshold tuning. Given that diabetes risk prediction prioritises minimising false negatives (recall) over false positives (precision), we emphasised PR-AUC as the primary metric, with AUROC as a secondary measure. XGBoost and gradient boosting achieved the strongest overall performance (PR-AUC ≈ 0.48, AUROC ≈ 0.83), and emerged as reliable models, balancing discrimination, calibration, and sensitivity for practical screening applications while elastic net maximised sensitivity at the cost of precision.

title: Deep Learning Method for Schizophrenia Detection from Hilbert-Huang Transformed EEG Signals
authors: F. M. Mahmudul Haque, M. Morshed, M. S. Rana, M. A. Quaium, M. J. Rahimi, S. A. Fattah
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Schizophrenia is a debilitating neuropsychiatric disorder that profoundly affects cognition, perception, and executive function. Traditional clinical diagnosis primarily relies on subjective symptom evaluation, underscoring the urgent need for objective neurophysiological biomarkers. To detect schizophrenia, a Hilbert-Huang transform (HHT)-based deep learning framework has been addressed in this research work that investigates the non-stationary nature and transient nonlinear dynamics of electroencephalogram (EEG) signals. The proposed approach employs an empirical mode decomposition (EMD) to adaptively decompose EEG segments into intrinsic mode functions (IMFs) and applies HHT to extract instantaneous amplitude and frequency components. These components are then aggregated into Hilbert-Huang scalograms, representing a finegrained time-frequency matrix. Next, a 2D convolutional neural network (CNN) is developed in PyTorch to extract discriminative spectral-spatial representations from the scalograms. Moreover a channel and spatial attention module is provided to enhance the spatial-spectral features to improve the overall performance. A publicly available EEG dataset is employed to evaluate the proposed method, achieving a classification accuracy of 86.90%, thereby demonstrating its robustness and clinical applicability in schizophrenia detection.

title: Process Voltage Temperature Performance Comparison of CMOS and GDI Based Arithmetic Circuits in 45 nm and 180 nm Technologies
authors: Sadia Tasnim Shara, Hamim Ibrahim, Shahriyar Masud Rizvi, Md. Mortuza Ahmmed
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Nowadays, maintaining dependable and energy efficient digital performance is getting harder under Process, Voltage, and Temperature (PVT) variations as technology nodes get smaller and circuits get more crowded. Three popular arithmetic circuits a 2-to-1 multiplexer, a 2-bit comparator, and a full adder are designed with both CMOS and Gate Diffusion Input (GDI) logic styles across 45 nm and 180 nm nodes and they are compared in this paper. Cadence Virtuoso post layout simulations were performed under realistic PVT corners <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(-40^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex> to 125° C), examining average power, area, transistor count, propagation delay, and leakage power. The findings demonstrated that 180 nm GDI circuits offered comparatively lower power consumption, leakage, and improved thermal stability which made them more appropriate for low power, reliable embedded systems, even though 45 nm CMOS circuits produced faster speeds. Without sacrificing functional reliability, these findings offer engineers useful design insights for energy conscious VLSI applications.

title: Transformer-Based Manipulative Span Identification with SHAP Explainability in Multilingual Telegram Data
authors: Arupa Barua, Ifath Jeba Chowdhury, Ashim Dey
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The proliferation of manipulative content on social media poses significant challenges to information integrity, particularly in sensitive geopolitical contexts. During the Rus-sia-Ukraine war, such narratives have the potential to distort public perception and shape international opinion.
