To address this issue, a shared task UNLP 2025 was formulated to detect such manipulative spans in Ukrainian and Russian 9,500 Telegram posts. This study leverages the dataset provided by the task organizers and explores a range of Machine Learning (ML), Deep Learning (DL) and Transformer-based approaches to investigate the detection of manipulative spans in text. SHapley Additive ex-Planations (SHAP) are further employed to interpret token-level contributions towards manipulativeness, providing transparency into model predictions. Our experimental results reveal that the RemBERT transformer model achieves the highest F1 score of 0.5841, outperforming other architectures. These findings also underline the potential and current limitations of computational approaches for detecting manipulative spans in multilingual and geopolitically sensitive discourse.

title: Advanced Stair-Climbing Delivery Robot with Multimedia Transmission and Client Verification
authors: Md. Al Tas Did Ul, Md. Muddassir Fuad, Dip Kumar Saha, Sarafat Hussain Abhi
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: This paper presents a stair-climbing delivery robot designed to facilitate secure package delivery in multi-story buildings without elevator access. Current stair-climbing robots excel in mobility however, they don't have integrated client verification system which is important for secured handover of items. Beside this, existing delivery systems often miss terrain adaptability. Proposed stair-climbing robot addresses this gap by providing reliable stair navigation with secured and contactless delivery. These are achieved through a dual-tracked locomotion system with onboard face recognition and live video streaming to provide reliable stair navigation and secure, contactless delivery. Implemented on a cost-effective Raspberry Pi 4B and ESP32-CAM platform, the system leverages teleoperation supported by simulation-based mapping and localization. Experimental results demonstrate an 89% success rate in physical stair-climbing trials under payload, with secure face verification accuracy of up to 88% in typical indoor lighting. The streaming module delivers real-time video with low latency, ensuring transparency and user confidence. This integrated approach offers a practical solution for constrained indoor last-mile delivery, bridging the gap between mobility and security in robotic delivery.

title: A Computational and Empirical Study of ML and DL Models for Scalable DDoS Detection
authors: Md Saidur Rahman Kohinoor, Mehzabeen Azad Chowdhury, Suhrath Tahra
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks pose a persistent challenge to modern network security, requiring detection models that are both accurate and computationally efficient. This paper presents a comparative analysis of six machine learning and deep learning models-Logistic Regression, K-Nearest Neighbors, Random Forest, XGBoost, Long Short-Term Memory, and a feed-forward Neural Network-evaluated on two benchmark datasets: the DDoS Attack Detection Classification dataset and the DDoSdata-Botnet dataset. Models were assessed using accuracy, F1-score, training and prediction time, and memory usage to capture both empirical performance and computational trade-offs. Results demonstrate that XGBoost consistently achieved the highest accuracy (up to 98%) with balanced efficiency, while Neural Networks performed strongly (94%) but required significant resources. Logistic Regression offered reliable performance (91%) with minimal overhead, whereas KNN struggled with high-dimensional data <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(78 \%)$</tex>. The findings highlight the suitability of deep learning for complex attack patterns and the importance of model selection based on resource constraints.

title: Improved Ensemble Machine Learning Framework for Superior Smart Grid Stability Prediction
authors: Md. Shovon Rahman, Md. Tanjil Islam Aronno, Md. Ahsan Kabir, Shaikh Anowarul Fattah
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: As the population is increasing, energy regulatory bodies are facing difficulties in managing consumer demand alongside the production rate. Therefore, incorporation of more sources which are renewable in nature, along with intelligent generation-demand control, is required. Smart grid technology addresses this problem by the inclusion of advanced metering infrastructures (AMIs) as well as IoT devices. As the smart grid offers many functionalities, its stability is a major concern. Stability can be analyzed from different aspects, such as the security of the grid from various cyber-physical attacks or maintaining a balance between demand and prediction. Machine learning and artificial intelligence techniques are successfully implemented on smart grids to achieve perfection in customer demand prediction. In this paper, we propose a improved stacking ensemble model that combines multiple classifiers with optimized hyperparameters. This approach achieved an accuracy of around 99%, showing its effectiveness in ensuring smart grid stability. The experiments were performed on a publicly available dataset from the UC Irvine (UCI) machine learning repository. Robustness of the model is further demonstrated through the analysis of performance metrics, including F1-score, recall, precision, and accuracy, along with ROC-AUC analysis.

title: Advancing Security in Android Devices for malware detection using Machine Learning and Deep Learning Techniques
authors: Monika Sharma, Ajay Kaul, Naveen Kumar Gondhi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: Intelligent and adaptable security measures have become necessary due to the swift increase in the frequency and complexity of cyber-attacks. Among these, machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) have shown promise in improving cybersecurity frameworks. Due to their dominance in the mobile ecosystem, Android smartphones are increasingly being targeted by malicious applications for attacks. The complexity of the Android ecosystem and the dynamic nature of attack routes make it difficult to detect such malware. This study examines the effectiveness of machine learning methods in categorizing apps using extracted feature patterns in order to detect Android malware. The study makes use of two benchmark datasets, Android MalGenome and Drebin, from which three subsets are obtained: (i) the API-permission dataset, (ii) the only API dataset, and (iii) the only permission dataset. These subsets are used to evaluate and compare the classification performance of six supervised machine learning algorithms: Support Vector Machine (SVM), Random Forest (RF), Artificial Neural Network (ANN), AdaBoost, and XGBoost. The comparison analysis offers empirical information about the performance of various algorithms across feature subsets. The findings show that ensemble-based models, like Random Forest and XGBoost, are more efficient at identifying new malware variants, which helps make Android security systems smarter and more effective.

title: Design and simulation of an electronic dice
authors: Kathleen C. Capalaran, Ma. Isabela D. Capalaran, Hazel Roi D. Coronel, Eunice Jane D. Landicho, Christine Raine B. Santos, Jobert C. Sapaden, Johnel Ian M. Songco, Eureka Neesha M. Lapuebla, Angelica D. De Sagun, Teofilo D. De Sagun, Selverino A. Magon
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This paper presents the design and simulation of an electronic dice, offering a modern alternative to traditional dice. The electronic dice utilize integrated circuits and digital components to simulate the randomness inherent in conventional dice rolls. Key features include a decade counter for generating outcomes, a function generator for pulse generation, and a 7-segment display for visual representation of numbers. The study explores the circuit design, operational functionalities, potential enhancements, and various applications of electronic dice. The findings suggest significant implications for board games, educational tools, and recreational activities, providing a reliable, interactive, and versatile solution for generating random outcomes. Additionally, the paper discusses improvements such as multi-sided dice simulation and power source optimization to enhance user experience and sustainability. Overall, electronic dice offer a novel and effective approach to simulate randomness, catering to diverse user needs in gaming and educational contexts.

title: Detecting Depression in Social Media Posts Using SBERT-CNN: A Transformer-Based Semantic Neural Network Approach
authors: Shaikh Ibna Sazzad, Sumaiya Rahman, Shyla Afroge
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Depression, a silent and pervasive mental health crisis, continues to be one of the foremost causes of emotional suffering, disability, and loss of life across the world. In today's digital era, individuals often disclose their emotional struggles through social media, turning platforms like Reddit into unintended yet valuable sources for early psychological insight. Leveraging this phenomenon, our study proposes a novel deep learning architecture, SBERT-CNN, that fuses the semantic encoding strength of Sentence-BERT with the hierarchical feature extraction of Convolutional Neural Networks. In contrast to prior studies that utilize lighter SBERT models such as all-MiniLM-L6-v2, we employ the more semantically expressive all-mpnet-base-v2 model, which significantly enhances the contextual understanding of user-generated posts. Utilizing a balanced dataset collected from Reddit, the proposed model obtains an accuracy of 94.5%, demonstrating its strong capability in capturing intricate linguistic indicators associated with depressive behavior. This study presents a scalable framework for real-time mental health screening through natural language cues in online environments, while also contributing significant advancements to the current leading approaches in depression detection.

title: MEMS Load Cell Based on the Nanomechanical Membrane-Flexure (NMF) Sensor for Sub-Micronewton Thrust Measurement
authors: Haneen Shameem, Amba Agnes. R. P, Seena. V
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: This paper reports the developement of a highly sensitive MEMS load cell for measuring sub-micronewton level thrust from micro propulsion systems. The device is based on a novel Nanomechanical Membrane-Flexure (NMF) structure, which mechanically transduces the membrane deflection into an amplified uniaxial stress on the sensing flexures. To achieve high sensitivity, the electromechanical transduction is performed by integrating a high gauge factor (-430) Indium Tin Oxide (ITO) thin film as the piezoresistive sensing element. This approach simplifies the fabrication process by obviating the need for silicon doping. Finite element analysis (FEA) was performed on two scaled designs: (A) fabricated NMF version with 250 μm radius membrane for high-resolution point-force sensing and (B) 2.5 mm radius membrane optimized for capturing plumes from various micro-thrusters. Simulations for both point and boundary load conditions show significant stress concentration in the flexures, resulting in a promising high sensitivity suitable for characterizing next-generation micro-thrusters.

title: Analysis of Atomic Structures in Ultrathin Crystalline/Amorphous Alumina Films Using STEM-EELS and Virtual Reference Spectra
authors: Yusuke Yuzawa, Takanori Asano, Kenji Nakamura, Yuta Aiba, Daisuke Hagishima, Masumi Saitoh, Hiroki Tanaka
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: We investigated the atomic structures of ultrathin alumina films using scanning transmission electron microscopy combined with electron energy-loss spectroscopy (STEM-EELS). To analyze the effects of electron-beam damage causing crystalline-to-amorphous transitions, we used multiple virtual reference spectra calculated from amorphous atomic structure models via first-principles molecular dynamics simulations. We found that the crystalline-to-amorphous transition occurs during the repeated STEM-EELS measurement, which is consistent with nano-beam diffraction results. This approach is effective for the assessment of the atomic structures before damage in ultra-thin films in semiconductor devices.

title: Comparing Deep Learning Frameworks for Air Quality Index (AQI) Prediction in Bangladesh: Static vs. Year-Wise Forecasting Evaluation
authors: Fariha Zaman Nishat, Saiful Islam, Jasiya Fairiz Raisa, Mosabber Uddin Ahmed
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Air quality has significant impacts on human health, ecosystems, and wildlife, making its monitoring and forecasting a critical concern. Bangladesh frequently experiences high levels of air pollution that exceed safe thresholds. This paper presents a harmonized Air Quality Index (AQI) dataset for Bangladesh (2012-2024), covering eight cities and ten monitoring stations, and a systematic comparison of static and forecasting deep learning (DL) models for AQI prediction. Building on this dataset, two complementary modeling frameworks were developed: (1) a forecasting pipeline employing residual learning with temporal lags, and (2) a static pipeline utilizing concurrent pollutant and station data. DL architectures including Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU), Temporal Convolutional Network (TCN), Recurrent Neural Network (RNN), Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), and Multilayer Perceptron (MLP) were systematically evaluated under static and forecasting scenarios, with ensemble methods applied to enhance prediction accuracy. Stacking ensembles consistently outperformed individual models, achieving mean absolute error (MAE) of approximately 2.33, mean squared error (MSE) of 40.56, and coefficient of determination (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex>) of 0.98 in static evaluation, and improving from MAE of 4.1 to 1.99 in forecasting with extended temporal windows. CNN and TCN demonstrated strong performance for longer horizons, whereas recurrent models and MLP were less effective. These findings can inform more accurate AQI forecasting and support evidence-based air quality management in Bangladesh.

title: Deep Learning-Based Classification of Pea Leaf Diseases Using EfficientNetB7: A Scalable Approach for Agricultural Diagnostics
authors: Srikanta Kumar Mohapatra, K. K. Manivannan, Dipannita Ray, A. Parvathi Priya, Kochumol Abraham, Disha Garg
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Pea leaf diseases affect the yield of crops and thus require a proper and efficient identification system for management. This research leverages the EfficientNetB7 architecture to classify pea leaf samples into five categories: FRESH_LEAF, DOWNY_MILDEW_LEAF, LEAFMINER_LEAF, POWDER_MILDEW_LEAF, and OTHER. The data used in this work encompasses 1,432 images from the ghetto-d dataset; the images were pre-processed by resizing, normalizing, and applying augmentation. The dataset was split into training, validation, and test subsets in an 80:10:10 ratio to ensure balanced evaluation. The proposed model achieved 85% accuracy and a high level of precision, recall, and F1 score for all classes, thus confirming the model’s good generalization capabilities. Some methods, such as dropout layers, early stopping, and learning rate scheduling, were used to reduce overfitting to improve the performance. The confusion matrix and classification report provided the model’s effectiveness, and four minor classification errors identified further optimization opportunities. This work shows that EfficientNetB7 is capable of automating disease classification and offers a scalable approach for agricultural applications. Possible further studies could enhance the present dataset by including wider datasets or applying more effective feature learning systems, such as Vision Transformers. Such developments may also enhance the automatic control of sustainable agriculture to manage crops.

title: Covert Communication for UAV-Assisted Systems with an Active Warden
authors: Mingjie Yang, Linying Tian, Chan Gao, Bingbing Xiao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Satellite Internet (SAT-NET)
abstract: To address the inherent openness of wireless communication environments, covert communication has emerged as a promising approach to protect legitimate transmissions from unauthorized detection. This paper investigates a UAVassisted wireless covert communication system, where channel uncertainty is exploited to enable covert information transmission in the presence of active wardens acting simultaneously as jamming sources. We first model the indirect communication strategy between legitimate parties and characterize the detection behavior of an active warden. To achieve both covertness and communication efficiency, the transmit power of the devices and the hovering positions of UAVs are jointly optimized, subject to the dual constraints of maintaining a sufficient covert communication rate and ensuring a high detection error probability at the warden. Closed-form expressions for the wardens detection error probability and the achievable covert communication rate are derived. Furthermore, numerical optimization is employed to maximize the achievable rate under covertness constraints. Simulation results corroborate the theoretical analysis and demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed scheme in enabling covert communication in jamming-assisted active warden scenarios.

title: Female Leadership in HR Decision Models for Employee Satisfaction in a Cement Company
authors: Nanda Ayudhea, Delsa Arrani, Vilena Nadianty Gunawan, Rini Kurnia Sari, Aisyah Qanita, Baghas Budi Wicaksono
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Employee satisfaction has long been acknowledged as a key determinant of organizational performance, productivity, and sustainability. Employee satisfaction is affected by multiple factors, such as female leadership, organizational culture, and work discipline by multiple determinants, such as female leadership, organizational culture, and work discipline, which collectively shape employees’ motivation levels, accountability, and engagement among employees. The purpose of this study is to explore how female leadership, organizational culture, and work discipline directly affect employee satisfaction in an engineering-based context, with PT XYZ in the cement industry as the case example. A quantitative research design was applied, utilizing purposive sampling involving 100 participants. The data collected were processed through multiple regression analysis using SPSS version 22. The results of the validity and reliability testing confirmed that all measurement items were both valid and reliable, while classical assumption tests demonstrated that the regression model satisfied all statistical requirements. The results indicate that female leadership, organizational culture, and work discipline each exert a positive and significant effect on employee satisfaction. Among these, female leadership demonstrated the strongest effect, underscoring the importance of inclusive and transformational leadership in motivating employees. These results highlight the strategic importance of leadership, culture, and discipline in fostering a motivated, productive, and competitive workforce within industrial organizations.

title: Collaborative Semiconductor Production Economic: Mobile Demand Fulfillment via Leakage Predictive Model and Device Technology Optimization
authors: Rami Salem, Songtao Wo, Ting Chen, Ahmed Mohamed, Huy Do, Anand Subramaniam, Tsung-Han Lee
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: This paper addresses critical challenges in meeting mobile unit demand while minimizing production costs through an advanced thermal design power predictive model and concurrent device process optimization. The integrated approach increased mobile unit allocation from 15% to 70%, thereby making the mass production more cost effective while meeting customer specifications, demonstrating effective collaboration in semiconductor yield enhancement.

title: Toward Safer Workplaces: A Review of Digital Twin-Enabled Safety Training
authors: Shahed Chowdhury Omi, Musfiq Alam Chowdhury, Sabiha Alam Chowdhury, Mehedi Hasan
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Rapid technological advancements in Industry 4.0 are reshaping industrial environments, introducing new operational risks alongside productivity gains. Traditional safety training methods often fail to adequately prepare workers for the complex, dynamic challenges of modern workplaces. This paper presents a comprehensive review of Digital Twin (DT) technology as a transformative solution for industrial safety training. By creating dynamic, high-fidelity virtual replicas of physical systems that are updated with real-time data, DTs offer immersive, risk-free and data-rich training environments. This review explores the limitations of conventional training, examines the role of enabling technologies such as the Internet of Things (IoT), Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Extended Reality (XR) and outlines the significant benefits of DT-enabled training, including enhanced realism, scenario repeatability and datadriven performance feedback. It also tackles the main obstacles to adoption, including infrastructure expenses, data standardization and legacy system integration. This paper makes the case for DT-based methods' potential to greatly increase workforce competency and lower workplace incidents by contrasting them with other digital training solutions and highlighting their distinct advantages in developing flexible and contextually relevant safety protocols.

title: Polarization Insensitive Metasurfaces Absorber for Visible Wavelengths
authors: Hayatul Islam Navin, Abu Ismail Siddique, Makshudur Rahman, Sharaf Tasnim
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Metasurface-based plasmonic absorbers leverage engineered metallic surfaces to enhance light absorption at specific wavelengths, which have opened new applications in nanophotonics. However, many existing plasmonic absorbers suffer from narrow bandwidths or reduced efficiency in the visible regime, which limits their applicability in practical photonic devices. This work presents a plasmonic light absorber (PLA) using metasurfaces to achieve a high absorption in the visible spectrum of light. The metasurface consists of an infinite array of unit cell with a cylinder surrounded by a ring, where the cylinder and the ring are made of aluminum oxide (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Al}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}$</tex>) and titanium (Ti), respectively. Simulation results reveal an average absorption <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>97 \%$</tex> covering the visible wavelengths of 380 to 700 nm and a maximum absorption around unity from 380 to 500 nm. Additionally, the metasurface is polarization-insensitive due to its structural symmetry, hence independent of incident light polarization and giving same absorption efficiency. The proposed metasurface absorber can be used for applications requiring broadband, polarization-independent absorption in the visible spectrum.

title: SBEX: Noise-Robust Speech Bandwidth Expansion Through Mamba-Inspired Residual Time-Frequency Learning
authors: Sayeda Rahman Ananna, Taieba Athay, Nursadul Mamun
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Robust speech super-resolution aims to reconstruct high-frequency cues from low-bandwidth speech while preserving intelligibility in real-world acoustic conditions. Although state-of-the-art methods perform well in clean scenarios, they often fail to recover high-frequency components under noisy environments. To address this challenge, SBEX is proposed as a noise-robust framework for speech super-resolution. Unlike conventional approaches that degrade under acoustic distortions, SBEX leverages a state-space-inspired core to capture both fine spectral details and long-range temporal dependencies. In addition, a noise-aware encoder is integrated to ensure stable and accurate reconstruction of high-frequency harmonics. Experimental results demonstrate that SBEX produces more natural and intelligible speech across diverse noisy conditions, highlighting its potential for deployment in telecommunications, speech recognition, and assistive hearing technologies.

title: CPS-Guard: Defensive Architectures for Securing Large Language Models in Cyber-Physical Systems
authors: Md. Mazharul Islam, Mubasshir Ahmed, Niaz Ashraf Khan
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Cyber-physical systems (CPS) are increasingly adopting large language models (LLMs) for log analysis, operator support, and anomaly triage. While LLMs enable powerful reasoning and flexible natural-language interaction, their probabilistic and opaque behavior introduces new attack surfaces, including prompt injection, adversarial manipulation, and privacy leakage. Existing CPS defenses largely assume deterministic models and do not account for LLM-specific threats, whereas current LLM safety work rarely targets real-time, safety-critical control environments. To bridge this gap, we propose CPS-Guard, a defense-in-depth architecture for LLM-enabled CPS that operates under advisory-only semantics. CPS-Guard combines secure prompt handling, explicit threat modeling, a deterministic safety validator with policy and bounds checking, and anomaly-based veto over plant telemetry. We implement CPS-Guard as middleware between CPS controllers and an aligned LLM, and evaluate it on 300 synthetically generated adversarial inputs spanning prompt injection, adversarial perturbations, and poisoning-style triggers. Compared to an unprotected LLM advisory baseline, CPS-Guard reduces failure rates by more than 85 % while incurring only 10 18 % additional latency across small to large CPS workloads. These results indicate that system-level hardening can make LLM-assisted CPS substantially more robust without violating real-time constraints.

title: Salary Prediction in Thailand’s IT Sector Using Supervised Learning Models
authors: Arocha Saengwirojanapat, Jirachaya Nongbualang, Natthida Theepakornsukkasem, Nuntanut Bhooanusas
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: Salary determination remains a growing concern in global job markets, as many job seekers lack accurate selfevaluation skills and often overestimate their qualifications. Consequently, salary recommendation systems have been increasingly introduced across various platforms. However, most existing models rely on candidate-centric data, which rarely reflect employers’ actual requirements. This paper proposes a salary prediction framework for Thailand’s IT industry using employeroriented job postings collected from real-world portals. As ITrelated positions in Thailand continue to expand due to digital transformation, our study also provides data-driven insights into emerging job trends. A key challenge in data collection is the rarity of explicit salary offers. To address this, reference salaries from the ISM-Tech Salary Report 2024 were adopted, and controlled noise was introduced via an exponential distribution to improve model generalization. The proposed method achieved promising performance, with a median percentage error of 16% overall and only 8% for software development roles using the default XGBoost model.

title: Trajectory-centric Probing of Frozen Chest CT Foundation Embeddings for Multi-endpoint Lung Cancer Phenotyping
authors: Kuan-Ting Wu, Hsu-Ching Huang, Wei-Kai Lee, Wei-An Chen, Chien-Ying Huang, Heng-Sheng Chao, Yung-Hung Luo, Jing-Yao Weng, Yi-Chen Yeh, Yuh-Min Chen, Yu-Te Wu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: Serial chest CT is routinely acquired during the preoperative workup of lung cancer, yet most imaging AI studies treat each scan as an isolated snapshot. We study whether frozen chest CT foundation embeddings can support clinically meaningful two-timepoint reasoning without end-toend fine-tuning [1]. Using a paired preoperative cohort with two CT exams per patient (prior exam $t_{S}$ and the preoperative exam $t_{0}$), we construct three longitudinal feature types from each encoder [2], [3]: the single-scan embedding $e\left(t_{S}\right)$, the change vector $\Delta e=e\left(t_{0}\right)-e\left(t_{S}\right)$, and the annualized velocity $v=\Delta e / \Delta t_{\text {years }}$. To mitigate acquisition/protocol heterogeneity across timepoints, we apply fold-wise CORAL alignment [4] and L2 normalization prior to probing. We evaluate a nonlinear probe (RBF-SVM) [5], [6] under 5-fold stratified crossvalidation with class-balanced training, and quantify uncertainty by bootstrap confidence intervals computed from out-of-fold predictions [7]. Across six binary endpoints spanning reportderived progression and pathology/molecular phenotypes, our framework demonstrates that longitudinal information provides statistically significant additive signal beyond single-timepoint embeddings, particularly for progression phenotypes, even without task-specific fine-tuning.

title: GhostNet-Based Peach Tree Disease Detection with Class-Aware Augmentation
authors: Kalikiri Niranjan, Seeniappan Kaliappan, R. Priyadharshini, Ritika Joshi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This paper evaluates the performance of GhostNet, a lightweight CNN, for four-class peach health prediction using a curated dataset of 4,307 images (Healthy: 3,569; Anarsia lineatella: 370; Grapholita molesta: 304; Dead Trees: 64). Images were rescaled to 224x224 px and normalized using ImageNet statistics. A stratified random split of 70/15/15 was used; augmentation (which was class-aware, and limited to minority classes) and a balanced sampler (targeting B = 360 samples per class per epoch) were applied (using RandomResizedCrop, color jitter, RandomPerspective, CLAHE, MixUp, and CutMix). GhostNet (ImageNet-pretrained) was end-to-end fine-tuned using AdamW and cosine-annealed learning rates. On the held-out test set (n = 644), a top-1 accuracy of 92%, and macro precision/recall/F1 of 0.70/0.90/0.76 was obtained. Recall is good for Anarsia lineatella and Grapholita molesta, and there is residual loss in precision only for the rare class of Dead-Trees. Results suggest that GhostNet provides an effective accuracy vs. efficiency trade-off for field-deployable peach disease monitoring.

title: An Inverse-Time Hybrid Characteristic Design for Communicationless Overcurrent Relay Coordination in Microgrids
authors: Mehran Abdi, Reza Mohammadi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Protection and Automation of Power Systems (IPAPS)
abstract: Microgrids present considerable challenges for conventional protection systems due to the significant variations in fault current levels between grid-connected and islanded modes. To overcome this issue, this paper introduces an innovative inverse-time characteristic that leverages both current and voltage signals. The core of this characteristic lies in the integration of an exponential term, which depends on the fault current, and a logarithmic term, which depends on the voltage. This combination significantly enhances the relay's sensitivity to various fault conditions. It allows for faster and more accurate fault detection without relying on costly communication infrastructure or the need for dynamic setting updates. The optimal parameters for this characteristic are determined using an efficient hybrid approach combining Genetic Algorithm and Linear Programming. This characteristic effectively achieves two opposing objectives: minimizing relay operating time while maintaining full coordination between all primary and backup relays, regardless of the microgrids operating mode (grid-connected or islanded). Simulations conducted on a real 18-bus microgrid show that this method significantly reduces the total relay operating time when compared to the standard IEC characteristic, while ensuring complete coordination between primary and backup relays across all operational scenarios.

title: Enhancing Wind Farm Operational Efficiency Through Power Output Classification Using MLP and Ensemble Models
authors: Md Fujael Ahmed, Rayhan Ahmed Opi, Rashedul Albab, Syed Nadim Mehdi, Abdul Hadee Tahsin, Md. Shahid Iqbal
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Accurately forecasting wind turbine power output is challenging due to the complex and non-linear patterns present in wind farm operational data. Conventional approaches largely adopt regression-based forecasting, which, while effective in predicting continuous values, often lacks the interpretability required for direct operational decision-making. In this work, we reframe the problem as a multi-class classification task, categorizing power output into discrete levels, low, medium, and high, to provide actionable insights for identifying underperforming turbines and optimizing maintenance schedules. A curated Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) dataset was processed through a rigorous pipeline, including the handling of physically impossible negative power values, outlier capping, feature normalization, and label encoding. Five Machine Learning (ML) models, including Logistic Regression (LR), Random Forest (RF), Gradient Boosting (GB), XGBoost, and a Multilayer Perceptron (MLP), were trained and optimized using Grid Search hyperparameter tuning. The MLP model obtained the highest classification accuracy of 93.51%, marginally outperforming the top-performing ensemble models. Feature importance analysis revealed that while tree-based models relied heavily on a limited set of features, the MLP distributed its attention more evenly across all inputs. The proposed classification-based framework offers a robust and interpretable alternative to regression-based approaches, providing direct operational benefits for the renewable energy sector.

title: Slack-Jawed and Breathless
authors: Shawn Powers
publish: 2025 Artificial Intelligence x Humanities, Education, and Art (AIxHEART)
abstract: In this invited position paper, the author explores the interrelationship between aesthetic experience, consciousness, and intelligence in the age of artificial intelligence (AI). Grounded in philosophical traditions as well as personal observation, the author argues that intense aesthetic experiences provoke cognitive engagement and embodied awareness, serving as a foundation for human intelligence. It is noted that language doesn't serve how we think about AI systems creating a default to anthropomorphizing algorithms. The author asserts that human intelligence is not congruent with AI as it is rooted instead in lived experience, cultural contexts, and the capacity for wonder.

title: Bridging Educational Gaps: Predicting Retakes Among Bangladeshi Undergraduates with Machine Learning and Explainable AI
authors: Nusrat Zahan Nila, Md. Ali Mahmud Pritom, Fatema Tuz Johora, Aditya Rajbongshi, Md. Salauddin Sarker, Md. Ashrafuzzaman
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: In higher education, course retakes present a significant challenge, disrupting academic progression, increasing financial burdens and psychological distress among students. This study presents a novel approach for early prediction of students' course retakes in the context of Bangladesh. The dataset was meticulously collected from different university students, consisting of 474 instances. Initially, the dataset was prepared using various preprocessing techniques, including data cleaning, transformation, handling missing values, one-hot encoding, column renaming, as well as feature scaling technique. Experimentation with several Machine Learning (ML) models revealed that the proposed Soft Voting classifier outperformed the baseline classifiers Logistic Regression (LR), Gaussian Naive Bayes (GNB), Random Forest (RF), Support Vector Classifier (SVC), Perceptron, and K-nearest Neighbors (KNN) with an accuracy of 94.74%. To enhance the interpretability of ML model's two eXplainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) algorithms were integrated to identify the key factors influencing the model's predictions. This approach provided a reliable and equitable prediction of course retakes or failures utilizing real datasets, contributing to enhanced student academic success and improved institutional efficiency.

title: CAL-Log: Calibration-Aware Logarithmic Cost Modeling for Active Learning in Low-Resource NLP
authors: Vihanga Supasan Kariyakaranage, Banuka Athuraliya
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Building robust NLP systems for low-resource languages remains constrained by the high cost of expert annotation rather than model architecture availability. This paper proposes CAL-Log, a cost-calibrated active learning strategy that jointly accounts for (i) human annotation time and (ii) model miscalibration in early rounds, extending our prior framework for costaware active learning [23] to genuinely low-resource multilingual settings. CAL-Log introduces a logarithmic annotation-cost model to avoid the “cost-collapse” behavior of linear utility-pertoken heuristics, coupled with a calibration-aware uncertainty signal. We evaluate on <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0}$</tex> low-resource text classification datasets spanning 8 languages (Sinhala, Tamil, Hindi, Telugu, Yoruba, Amharic, Swahili, Xhosa) over 3 independent random seeds. Results show that CAL-Log achieves an Efficiency Score of 0.281 F1 per 1,000 minutes (mean ± std: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.432 \pm 0.217$</tex> F1 at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1,535 ~{\min}$</tex>), maintaining robust performance across all datasets. Multi-seed ablation confirms that removing the cost model raises annotation cost by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 9 \%}(+597 ~{\min})$</tex> and lowers efficiency to 0.180, while removing temperature calibration yields a less stable learning trajectory. Our stability analysis demonstrates that CAL-Log prevents the seed-dependent performance collapse observed in linear cost models (cross-seed std up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 0.296$</tex> on MasakhaNEWS-sw for CAL-Linear) and achieves superior consistency on morphologically complex tasks such as Sinhala Sentiment <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(0.547 \pm 0.033$</tex> vs. <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.422 \pm 0.061)$</tex>. To our knowledge, this is the first active learning framework to jointly formalize annotation cost collapse and integrate model calibration error into the acquisition function under realistic human-time constraints for low-resource multilingual NLP.

title: Reliability Enhancement of 40nm RRAM Chip by Bottom Electrode Engineering
authors: Kaimeng Liu, Chengxiang Ma, Wei Chang, Jinyi Liu, Ruofei Hu, Didi Zhu, Yue Xi, Dong Wu, Bin Gao, He Qian, Jianshi Tang, Huaqiang Wu
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: This work proposes an electrode engineering optimization strategy to enhance the comprehensive performance and reliability of resistive random-access memory (RRAM) chip on a commercial 40nm CMOS platform. By adopting a post-deposition sequential treatment (PDST) with plasma-induced surface activation and thermal oxidation on the TiN bottom electrode (BE), the 40nm RRAM array can achieve a high endurance exceeding 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">5</sup> cycles, long retention over 10 years at 85℃, and excellent uniformity in the initial state within the 16kb array. The physical mechanism underlying the impact of TiN BE on the reliability in terms of TiN layer thickness and interface treatment is clarified through comprehensive electrical tests and material characterizations. These findings provide key insights into the reliability improvement of RRAM and could facilitate the commercialization of RRAM technology.

title: Risk-Aware Hybrid CNN-LSTM for Stock Forecasting with Macro-Technical Integration
authors: Ravi Bandara, Maninda Edirisooriya
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Stock markets exhibit non-linear dynamics influenced by both technical patterns and macroeconomic regimes. While Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks capture temporal dependencies effectively, they often neglect macroeconomic drivers that account for 15 to 20 percent of price variance. This paper proposes a hybrid CNN-LSTM framework integrating technical indicators with Federal Reserve Economic Data (FRED) macroeconomic variables for short-horizon S&P 500 price forecasting. A Principal Component Analysis (PCA) preprocessing step orthogonalizes 45 engineered features into 14 principal components (95 % variance retention), mitigating multicollinearity. Bayesian hyperparameter optimization tunes a dual-output architecture that simultaneously optimizes price regression (primary task) and direction classification (auxiliary regularization task). Evaluated on 10 years of daily data (20152024) via walk-forward validation, the model achieves Rsquared <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.86$</tex>, Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) = 3.98%, and 18% Mean Absolute Error (MAE) reduction versus LSTM baselines. Direction prediction accuracy of 45.44 % reflects inherent unpredictability of short-term movements. Risk-aware outputs including confidence scores and volatility bands support practical decision-making. Results validate that systematic macroeconomic integration substantially enhances forecasting accuracy beyond technical indicators alone.

title: A Novel African Vulture Optimization Algorithm for Maximum Power Extraction from Different PV Array Arrangements
authors: Rabbiul Hasan Ankon, Munshi Jawad Ibne Azad, Md. Al Imran Fahim
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: With the increasing demand for the world's renewable energy, photovoltaic (PV) systems are an emerging and popular choice for producing sustainable electricity. However, they are strongly impacted by environmental factors such as temperature and sunlight. A plurality of local maxima (LM) can exist in the PV characteristic curve during partial shading (PS), and tracking of global maxima (GM) instead of LM is a big challenge. In this regard, the present work utilized a novel African Vulture Optimization Algorithm (AVOA) for GM tracking to overcome these issues. The AVOA-based maximum power point tracking (MPPT) algorithm is evaluated for multiple PV array configurations, and also, a comparison is made with the Horse Herd Optimization Algorithm (HHOA). Simulation results in MATLAB/Simulink are presented to show the effectiveness of the AVOA, and it is found that it is greater than that of the HHOA in tracking time, settling time, and tracking accuracy. Interestingly, AVOA achieves a 99.98% average tracking efficiency, with an average tracking time 70 ms faster and an average convergence time 60 ms faster. Along with that, AVOA consistently outperforms HHOA in error metrics, ensuring more dependable MPPT performance. These findings demonstrate the effectiveness of the AVOA-based MPPT approach to increasing the performance of PV systems under different PV array arrangements.

title: BanglaFeel: Enhanced Bangla Back-Transliteration
authors: Masfiq Mahmud, Sumaiya Akter Iqra, Muhammad Anas, Mohammed Ashikur Rahman
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Banglish, a Romanized form of Bangla, poses challenges for NLP models, especially sentiment analysis. The aim of this study is to develop an effective back-transliteration model that converts Banglish into native Bangla script, thereby enhancing the accuracy of downstream NLP tasks. Our model integrates a multi-encoder architecture, combining characterlevel CNN (CharCNN) and word-level LSTM (WordLSTM) encoders with a pre-trained T5 transformer decoder. This design helps capture both fine-grained spelling variations and broader contextual relationships. In the context of noisy Banglish, this multi-encoder design is necessary rather than arbitrary: the CharCNN encoder focuses on local character n-grams to model inconsistent and non-standard spellings, the WordLSTM encoder captures sequential word dependencies in often ungrammatical sentences, and the T5 encoder provides a strong semantic prior through transfer learning from large-scale pretraining, allowing the system to map noisy surface forms onto stable meaning representations. Trained on the BanglaTLit dataset, the model achieves a BLEU score of 68.03%, ROUGE-1 F1 of 83.34%, and a BERTScore F1 of 96.38%, demonstrating its superior ability to handle the variability inherent in Banglish. This approach offers a robust solution to the transliteration problem, improving performance across various Bangla NLP applications.

title: Hybrid Deep Learning for Litchi Leaf Analysis: Health Monitoring and Growth Stage Detection
authors: Bishwa Das, Md. Asiful Islam, Md Assaduzzaman
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Proper evaluation of the health and growth phases of the litchi leaves is crucial to proper management of the crop and prevention of diseases, and optimization of yield. The traditional practices are based on manual inspection, which is time-consuming and error-prone. We address these constraints by suggesting a CNN-ViT hybrid model, which integrates MobileNetV2 with an efficient local feature extractor and a Vision Transformer (ViT) as a global context extractor. The model was trained and tested on a selected set of leaf images of litchi with various health conditions and levels of development, achieving a peak accuracy of 99.74 % with high precision, recall, and F1-scores across all five classes. The hybrid architecture was shown to be better in generalization and robustness as compared to ResNet121, InceptionV3, and MobileNetV2. Ablation experiments also demonstrated that batch size 16 is optimal and provides the highest generalization. The results demonstrate the potential that the proposed framework has to automated monitoring of the leaves of the litchi tree and its relevance in the real-life context of precision agriculture systems.

title: Beam-Reconfigurable Quadrifilar Helix Antenna Based on a Bi-Stable Origami Metamaterial
authors: Dongxu Chen, Min Li, Chenye Xu, Wanchen Yang, Weixu Yang, Mengzhe Song, Qiubo Ye, Dian Wang, Wenquan Che
publish: 2026 IEEE International Workshop on Antenna Technology (iWAT)
abstract: This paper presents a novel beam-reconfigurable quadrifilar helix antenna (QHA) integrated with a bi-stable origami metamaterial structure. The design leverages the mechanical bistability of the origami structure to switch between two distinct radiation states without requiring continuous external power. The antenna radiates with righthand circular polarization (RHCP) in both states. In the unfolded state (State I), the antenna achieves a wide half-power beamwidth (HPBW) of 182° at 1.57 GHz, suitable for broad coverage. In the folded state (State II), it exhibits a narrow HPBW of 109° at 1.22 GHz, ideal for directional tracking. This provides a beamwidth reconfiguration range of 73°. Both simulation and measurement results validate that the design is potential for 6G adaptive communication scenarios.

title:
P-GaN-Gate GaN-on-Si HEMTs with BV>1200 V and Excellent Dynamic Performance Realized Through Low Power NH
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    Plasma Acceptor Re-Passivation
authors: Zhaofeng Wang, Zhihong Liu, Weichuan Xing, Weihang Zhang, Shenglei Zhao, Xiangdong Li, Shuzhen You, Jincheng Zhang, Yue Hao
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: In this letter, we report p-GaN gate enhancement-mode (E-mode) GaN-on-Si high electron mobility transistors (HEMTs) fabricated using an Mg acceptor re-passivation technique (ARP HEMT) via NH<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">₃</inf> plasma treatment. The gate-to-source and gate-to-drain access regions were subjected to hydrogen ion implantation through NH₃ plasma treatment in a plasma-enhanced chemical vapor deposition (PECVD) system, forming a high-resistance cap layer. The fabricated device demonstrates a high threshold voltage (V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">TH</inf>) of 2.1 V, a low gate reverse leakage current (I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">gr</inf>) of 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">⁻¹¹</sup> A/mm, and a high ON/OFF current ratio (I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ON</inf>/I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">OFF</inf>) of up to 3×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">⁹</sup>. The dynamic on-resistance (R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on,d</inf>) measured under a quiescent drain voltage (V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ds,q</inf>) of 600 V with a stress time of 1200 s is only 1.03 times the static on-resistance (R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on,s</inf>) at 25°C. A breakdown voltage (BV<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">OFF</inf>) of 1298 V and a specific on-resistance of 0.85 mΩ•cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> were achieved in a device with Lgd=6 μm after additional SiN passivation. The developed fabrication process shows significant potential for application in GaN-based power transistors.

title: Fake News Detection in Bangla: A Transformer Ensemble Framework for Robust Classification
authors: Shubail Haque Turza, Akif Mahdi, Md. Ibne Shihab Shad, M. Hasnat Kabir, Ariful Islam
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The exponential growth of fake news over digital media has seriously disrupted information and public trust, as well as threatened social stability. These challenges are even more severe for low-resource, morphologically rich languages like Bangla, where the scarcity of NLP resources and a high amount of code-mixed content make the automated detection even more challenging. To address this gap, the research proposes a transformer-based ensemble model for Bangla fake news detection, which combines BanglaBERT, IndicBERT, and XLM-RoBERTa via soft voting, along with a morphology-aware preprocessing pipeline. On the balanced IBFND benchmark, BanglaBERT achieved the best single-model result with 99.03% accuracy, and the ensemble achieved 98.93 % accuracy with more stable performance on evaluation metrics. Cross-dataset validation on the BanglaFakeNews corpus provided additional evidence of the approach's robustness but also demonstrated the effect of imbalance and domain coverage on evaluation. Overall, the results have shown that language-aware preprocessing combined with ensemble learning sets a high and consistent baseline for Bangla fake news detection, and offers a promising direction for under-resourced languages with complex linguistic structures as well.

title: Hybrid Classical-Learned Residual Lossless Image Compression with Affine Translation Augmentation and Entropy-Fidelity Tradeoff Analysis
authors: Sumaya Ishrat Moyeen, Md. Sadiqur Rahman Mabud, Md. Shahid Uz Zaman, Md. Al Mamun
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: This paper presents a hybrid residual compression framework that integrates classical spatial prediction with a convolutional autoencoder to improve lossless image coding. Established predictors—PAETH, MED and a simplified CALIC-GAP—are first applied to generate low-entropy residuals, with integer-pixel translation augmentation used during training to enhance predictor robustness. The residuals are then normalized and encoded by a lightweight autoencoder trained for minimal distortion. Evaluations on MNIST, CIFAR-10 and CLIC datasets under both teacher-forced and predictor-driven decompression demonstrate consistent entropy reduction with high reconstruction fidelity. Comparative analysis highlights that PAETH predictor consistently achieves the best balance between compression efficiency and reconstruction fidelity, yielding the highest SSIM/PSNR and lowest MSE across all the datasets, while also maintaining competitive entropy levels; CALIC minimizes true entropy but inflates reconstructed entropy; and MED yields the lowest reconstructed entropy at the cost of significant quality loss. These results confirm that combining classical prediction with learned residual modeling provides an efficient and practical pathway toward deployable lossless image compression.

title: LoRA-Adapted Vision Transformers for Efficient and Explainable Colorectal Cancer Classification
authors: Nafisa Binte Ghulam Kibria, Hafsa Binte Kibria, Md. Nahiduzzaman
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Colorectal cancer is one of the leading causes of cancer-related deaths, where an early and accurate diagnosis is vital for effective treatment. Conventional histopathological analysis is manual, time-consuming, and subject to inter-observer variability. That is why automated diagnostic tools come in handy. Deep learning has shown great potential to address these challenges by enabling fast and reproducible image-based classification. In this study, Vision Transformers (ViTs) are used for the classification of colorectal cancer tissue using the NCT-CRC-HE100 K dataset. A baseline ViT demonstrates strong performance but requires high computational resources. To enhance efficiency, Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA), a parameter-efficient fine-tuning method, is introduced. By updating only 3.25% of the total parameters, LoRA improves classification performance, achieving 0.9949 accuracy and a 0.9949 weighted F1-score. Gradientweighted Class Activation Mapping (GradCAM) is integrated to provide visual explanations of predictions, supporting clinical interpretability. External validation on the EBHI-Seg dataset further demonstrates generalizability, with over 90% accuracy and F1-score across six tissue classes. Overall, the LoRA-adapted ViT offers an accurate, efficient, and interpretable solution for colorectal cancer diagnosis.

title: An Emotion-Aware Conversational AI with Intensity Scoring, Response Ranking, and Emotional State Visualization
authors: K.M.S.S. Kumarasingha, W.M.C.J.T. Kithulwatta, M.M S.M. Hansana, S.L.C. Hansika, P.A.T.C. Wijerathna
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Various psychological distresses are leading global healthcare challenge. Therefore, this research study proposes a multi-component, emotion-aware solution for detecting and responding sensitively to users' emotional states during conversations. This study includes emotional intensity scoring, emotion classification, & emotional response generation. Firstly, analyzing user input using a BERT-based model fine-tuned with the Go-Emotions dataset to determine emotional intensity via the NRC emotional intensity dictionary was done. The user message is also processed through a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG). The system uses 5 possible responses from the model for ranking their suitability The most suitable response selection collects emotional metadata about intensity and category and stores it for visualization of user-model relationship patterns. The study indicates that the responses yielded a RAG relevance score of above 0.70 in each instance with a mean value of 0.77 and emotional intensity scores of between 0.0168 and 0.1344. It means that there is a controlled emotional expressiveness variation. Centered visualization shows a gradual increase in the emotional valence of the user between -0.4 and about 0.8 in all interactions, which is a positive emotional regulation. The study observed for a positive relationship between user emotional status and system responses. This research projects practical applications for mental health support, emotion recognition and socially intelligent Artificial Intelligence tools, which needed for conversation-based adaptive agents.

title: Accelerating Distributed GNN Training by Attention Based Two-Level Dynamic Feature Caching
authors: Md Golam Morshed, Md. Murad Kabir Nipun, Raton Kumar Nondi, F A Sabbir Ahamed
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The effectiveness of deep learning approaches on datasets such as images and texts has been extended to more complicated graph-structured data by evolving graph neural networks (GNNs). The current frameworks integrate mini-batch and sampling to train models efficiently on big graphs using GPU accelerators. Distributed GNN training has become more common as several concurrent workers learn on separate parts of a big graph, driven by the rapid increase in both the size of graphs and the dimensions of features. However, research has shown that the collection of graph features across servers takes up the majority of each worker's training time, which poses a serious problem for the training of distributed GNN. This paper presents a new and fast memory-aware, two-level attention-based dynamic feature caching method to accelerate GNN training. Our suggested solution effectively minimizes the time required for obtaining features by using the available GPU and CPU resources to store frequently accessed graph data in a cache. Additionally, it incorporates an efficient dynamic caching strategy that considers both the attention score and the structural information of the graph, as well as the data access patterns during the training of a sampling-based GNN. We found that it could achieve a performance speedup of up to 5x over the state-of-the-art baselines, all while eliminating the feature retrieval time from the distributed GNN training pipeline. We used six publicly available large graph datasets to show the efficacy of our method.

title: A Comparative Study of FPGA Debouncing Architectures for Emergency Stop Safety Channels
authors: Md Towhidul Islam, Shanjida Islam, Isha Das, Chaitaly Nandi, Monjurul Islam, Tasnim Jerin Labonno
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: When a human hits an emergency stop, the logic must not chatter. Mechanical contacts exhibit millisecondscale bounce that can produce multiple spurious transitions. For emergency-stop (E-Stop) channels, debouncing must provide explicit guarantees on latency and single-count interpretation. This paper implements and compares two FPGA debouncers on a Cyclone IV device at 5 0 MHz: (i) a window-qualified finite-state machine (FSM) that accepts a change only after a fixed stability interval, and (ii) a cyclelevel FSM that accepts the first stable edge and enforces a lockout to bound subsequent toggles. Both designs employ two- stage synchronization and close timing with large margin. We characterize resource cost, timing, power, latency distributions, and accepted-rate envelopes under shared bounce stimuli. The windowed design offers a simple audit trail tied to an explicit qualification window; the lockout design delivers near-instantaneous response with a clear throughput contract. Results indicate that either approach can supply deterministic, auditable behavior at negligible area and power, enabling straightforward integration into certified E-Stop functions. Parameterselection guidance and diagnostics suitable for proof testing are provided.

title: Real-Time Student Attention Monitoring in Classrooms Using Computer Vision
authors: Md. Abdul Moeid, Md. Mokammel Haque
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Monitoring student attention in classrooms is vital for improving learning outcomes, yet traditional observation methods are often subjective and impractical for large classes. This paper presents a real-time student attention monitoring system that leverages computer vision techniques and existing CCTV infrastructure to provide an objective, scalable, and costeffective solution. The system integrates dlib and Mediapipe libraries to detect and analyze key visual cues-head pose, eye blinks, sleep indicators, and mouth movements-captured from multiple classroom camera feeds. A region-based segmentation approach ensures comprehensive coverage and reduces occlusion issues. Experimental evaluation demonstrated high detection accuracy for head position (99.86%), mouth movement (99.86%), and sleep detection (up to 99.86% at 100 cm), with slightly reduced accuracy at longer distances. A real-classroom trial with six participants confirmed the system's ability to differentiate attentiveness levels and detect disengagement patterns. The proposed framework offers potential applications in personalized learning, early intervention for at-risk students, and enhanced teacher-student interaction, while adhering to privacy and ethical considerations.

title: FedProx-IDS: A Stable and Scalable Federated Learning Framework for Intrusion Detection
authors: Farhan Abid Ahmed, Aurprita Mahmood, Meherin Sultana, Shamim Akhter
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS) are vital for protecting digital infrastructures. However, traditional centralized approaches struggle with data privacy, scalability, and communication overhead, particularly when faced with nonindependent and identically distributed (non-IID) data across networks, which is very common in this domain. Federated Learning (FL) offers a decentralized alternative, enabling collaborative model training without sharing raw data. Yet, existing research remains largely theoretical, lacking practical implementation. Our method involves carefully preparing the data with a privacy-preserving, schema-aligned categorical encoding process, employing a structured data preprocessing pipeline, and utilizing a feed-forward neural network designed for distributed learning. We use FedProx to train local models, which helps to minimize the negative impact of differing data distributions across the network, including an intentionally extreme non-IID configuration used as a stress-test scenario, and addressing these data heterogeneity challenges. Furthermore, we use WebSockets for efficient and reliable model sharing, ensuring smooth communication. The global model achieves exceptional performance, with 96.12% accuracy, 95.28% precision, 94.90% recall, and a 95.09% weighted F1-score, validating the framework's effectiveness. The stability of the training process is further demonstrated through convergence analysis, and a hyperparameter study validates the choice of the FedProx regularization term. By transitioning from theory to practice, this work demonstrates the feasibility of deploying FL-based IDS in real-world environments, offering a scalable, privacy-preserving solution for intrusion detection.

title: Evaluating Agentic AI Systems in Manufacturing: A Review of Taxonomy, Challenges and Future Directions
authors: Nastaran Moradzadeh Farid, Sara Shafiee
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) and agentic Artificial Intelligence (AI) systems are rapidly transforming the landscape of intelligent automation by enabling autonomous decision-making, planning, and interaction with complex environments. As these systems grow in capability and complexity, evaluating their performance becomes increasingly critical, yet remains a substantial challenge. In manufacturing, where agentic AI is increasingly applied to tasks such as predictive maintenance and quality control, standardized evaluation frameworks are still lacking. Prior research has proposed structured categories for this purpose in general scope, such as analyzing submodules like tool use, reasoning, memory, and self-learning, or evaluating overall high-level performance. In this paper, we aim to review the evaluation approaches of agentic AI systems, with a specific focus on applications in manufacturing and studies released between 2024-2025 and proposes a three-dimensional taxonomy spanning scope, methodology, and analysis. Reviewing 31 studies, we find evaluations are mostly small-scale proofs of concept with limited metrics, while reliability, safety, and large-scale benchmarking are overlooked. We propose a manufacturing-oriented taxonomy and reporting checklist that synthesize existing evaluation practices into a standardized framework for reproducibility and transparency. Our synthesis highlights gaps and offers guidelines for future industrial pilots.

title: Hybrid Data Preprocessing of Office Actions in Patent Prosecution History
authors: Jieh-Sheng Lee
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: This study explores data preprocessing techniques for extracting relevant information from office actions in patent prosecution history. It utilizes resources available through the USPTO’s Open Data Portal, such as the Office Action Text Retrieval API and the Enriched Citation API. By combining traditional rule-based Natural Language Processing approaches with statistical language models, this research addresses challenges in parsing complex patent texts. Specifically, a hybrid approach is implemented to integrate language models for named entity recognition and deterministic rule-based techniques for identifying claim numbers and reference locations. This combination enhances the accuracy and efficiency of data extraction processes. This study also highlights both practical applications and areas requiring further development, particularly concerning document structure and segmentation challenges within office actions. In summary, this research offers valuable insights into improving data preprocessing techniques for patent prosecution history, emphasizing the potential of hybrid approaches to effectively handle diverse and complex patent texts.

title: Multi-Agent AI System for Personalized Medication Adherence in Retail Pharmacies
authors: Roopam Dhanraj Choudhari
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: Medication non-adherence remains a critical public health challenge, contributing to preventable hospitalizations and healthcare costs exceeding ${\$}100$ billion annually in the United States. Traditional adherence interventions rely on static reminder systems and single-model prediction approaches that fail to address the complex behavioral patterns underlying nonadherence. This paper proposes a novel agentic artificial intelligence framework designed to transform medication adherence support through a multi-agent pharmacy ecosystem. The system integrates six specialized AI agents: Risk Prediction, Reasoning, Intervention Planning, Personal Health Companion, Pharmacist Assist, and Digital Twin Simulation. Combining gradient boosted decision trees with large language models, the framework provides explainable risk prediction and autonomous intervention generation using reinforcement learning. We present the system architecture, agent specifications, and validate the approach through MEPS dataset with augmented behavioral features and three detailed case studies demonstrating practical applicability across diverse patient populations. The proposed framework addresses fundamental limitations of existing systems by providing explainable predictions, contextual reasoning about adherence barriers, and adaptive intervention strategies tailored to individual patient needs.

title: A Blockchain-Oriented Software Engineering Architecture for Carbon Credit Certification Systems
authors: Matteo Vaccargiu, Azmat Ullah, Pierluigi Gallo
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Software Analysis, Evolution and Reengineering - Companion (SANER-C)
abstract: Carbon credit systems have emerged as a policy tool to incentivize emission reductions and support the transition to clean energy. Reliable carbon-credit certification depends on mechanisms that connect actual, measured renewable-energy production to verifiable emission-reduction records. Although blockchain and IoT technologies have been applied to emission monitoring and trading, existing work offers limited support for certification processes, particularly for small and medium-scale renewable installations. This paper introduces a blockchain-based carbon-credit certification architecture, demonstrated through a 100 kWp photovoltaic case study, that integrates real-time IoT data collection, edge-level aggregation, and secure on-chain storage on a permissioned blockchain with smart contracts. Unlike approaches focused on trading mechanisms, the proposed system aligns with European legislation and voluntary carbon-market standards, clarifying the practical requirements and constraints that apply to photovoltaic operators. The resulting architecture provides a structured pathway for generating verifiable carboncredit records and supporting third-party verification.

title: MobileNetV4-Small for Seven-Class Soil Classification on CycleGAN-Augmented Images
authors: V.Rekha, Uma Shankari Srinivasan, Neeraja Veeramachineni, Manikandaprabu P, P.Arthi Devarani, Divyanshu Sharma
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This study introduces a lightweight and deployable pipeline for soil-type recognition based on MobileNetV4-Small and a composite dataset comprised of 5,097 images consisting of an original set and a CycleGAN-augmented CyAUG set. Seven soil categories (Alluvial, Arid, Black, Laterite, Mountain, Red, Yellow) were taken with the stratified ratio of 70/15/15 for training, validation, and testing. Images were resized to 224×224 pixels and normalized, with no heavy preprocessing needed. The model was trained for 50 epochs using Adam, with categorical cross-entropy, achieving 97.12% test accuracy, macro precision/recall/F1 ≈ 0.964/0.967/0.966. The confusion matrix exhibits strong diagonal dominance, with limited misclassifications mainly between the visually similar Red and Laterite soils. Results indicate that the MobileNetV4-Small successfully captures discriminative chromatic and textural cues while still being suitable for edge deployment in precision agriculture and environmental monitoring. The curated dataset, careful stratification, and well-balanced metrics contribute to a robust baseline on soil classification with compact CNNs.

title: Skill Factors Influencing Low-Code/No-Code (LCNC) Readiness Among Sri Lankan IT Undergraduate
authors: Pasindu Jayasekara, Chalani Oruthotaarachchi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: This study examines the skill factors influencing Low-Code/No-Code (LCNC) readiness among 144 Sri Lankan IT undergraduates using Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM). The analysis evaluates the effects of Business Process Skills (BPS), Collaboration Skills (CS), Governance and Compliance Awareness (GCA), and Technical and Integration Skills (TIS) on LCNC readiness, with Self-Efficacy (SE) as a mediating construct. Results reveal that CS, GCA, and TIS significantly enhance LCNC readiness (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p}<\mathbf{0. 0 5}$</tex>), while BPS demonstrates a comparatively weaker impact. Furthermore, SE partially mediates several relationships, highlighting the critical role of confidence in LCNC skill acquisition. These findings provide empirical evidence to guide the integration of LCNC-oriented learning into IT curricula, thereby fostering digital transformation initiatives in developing economies.

title: Volleyball Lineup Recommendation using GraphSAGE with Knowledge Distillation and Feature-Aware Modeling
authors: Wei-Chi Lin, Jenn-Jier James Lien, Ya-Ning Chang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: Effective lineup selection is crucial in competitive volleyball, where team synergy often outweighs individual talent. However, traditional lineup decisions largely rely on coaches’ intuition and lack objective, data-driven support. In this paper, we propose a graph-based lineup recommendation system that models player synergy through pairwise interaction learning. Leveraging five seasons of data from the Taiwan Volleyball League, we train separate GraphSAGE models for each team to predict collaboration scores. To capture role-specific dynamics, we apply ElasticNet regression to derive position-aware feature weights. To enable scalable, cross-team generalization, these teacher models are distilled into a single student model (FinalGraph). This knowledge distillation framework achieves an $83.4 \%$ reduction in parameters, making the system efficient and interpretable. Our final system achieves an average lineup overlap of $86.2 \%$ (with the highest accuracy in the women’s division reaching $91 \%$ and the men’s division achieving up to $\mathbf{9 8 \%}$) against actual starting rosters, demonstrating a strong alignment with coaches’ real-world decisions.

title: Meta-Learning for GPU-Accelerated Quantum Many-Body Problems
authors: Yun-Hsuan Chen, Jen-Yu Chang, Tsung-Wei Huang, En-Jui Kuo
publish: 2026 International Conference on Quantum Communications, Networking, and Computing (QCNC)
abstract: We explore the industrial and scientific applicability of the VQE-LSTM framework by integrating meta-learning with GPU accelerated quantum simulation using NVIDIA's CUDA-Q (CUDAQ) platform. This work demonstrates how an LSTM-FC meta-initialization module can extend the practical reach of the Variational Quantum Eigensolver (VQE) in both chemistry and physics domains. In the chemical regime, the framework predicts ground-state energies of molecular Hamiltonians derived from PySCF, achieving near FCI accuracy while maintaining favorable <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$O\left(N^{2}\right)$</tex> scaling with molecular size. In the physical counterpart, we applied the same model to quantized Simple Harmonic Motion systems (SHM), successfully reproducing its ground and excited states through VQE and Variational Quantum Deflation (VQD) methods. Benchmark results on NVIDIA GPUs reveal significant speedups over CPU-based implementations, validating CUDAQ's capability to handle large-scale variational workloads efficiently. Overall, this study establishes VQE-LSTM as a viable and scalable approach for GPU accelerated quantum simulation, bridging quantum chemistry and condensed-matter physics through a unified, meta-learned initialization strategy.

title: Research on Multi-Mode Optimal Scheduling of Microgrids Based on Improved Multi-Objective Particle Swarm Optimization Algorithm
authors: Jinguo Li, Shihong Gan
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Electrical Technology and Automation Engineering (ETAE)
abstract: Aiming at the collaborative optimization problem of economy, environmental friendliness, and power supply reliability of microgrids under grid-connected/off-grid operation modes, an improved multi-objective particle swarm optimization algorithm with an adaptive inertia weight is proposed. An optimization model with operation cost, environmental protection cost, and power shortage rate as the objectives is established. The adaptive inertia weight is used to improve the optimization performance of the algorithm, thus achieving the multi-objective collaborative optimization of microgrids. Simulation results show that the proposed algorithm can obtain a uniformly distributed Pareto optimal solution set under both operation modes, which can provide effective support for microgrid operation decision-making.

title: A Knowledge-enhanced Hierarchical Graph Transfer Learning Framework for Large-scale Mobile Traffic Prediction
authors: Xing Wang, Zhiyan Song, Zhendong Wang, Kexin Yang, Chao Deng, Junlan Feng
publish: 2025 IEEE/ACM 33rd International Symposium on Quality of Service (IWQoS)
abstract: Large-scale mobile traffic prediction is critical for intelligent telecommunication networks, but remains challenging due to complex spatial-temporal patterns and network heterogeneity. Existing transfer learning-based prediction methods, tailored for grid data, inadequately handle graph-based mobile traffic data and ignore network heterogeneity. In this paper, we propose a Knowledge-enhanced Hierarchical Graph Transfer Learning (KHGTL) framework integrating graph-level and node-level knowledge transfer, for large-scale mobile traffic prediction.
