Conventional metasurface illumination systems suffer from several interrelated limitations—including geometric mismatch between the illumination spot and the active sensing area, poor irradiance uniformity, insufficient control over incident angle distribution, wavefront aberrations, and low optical integration density. To address these challenges, this work introduces a purpose-built, high-uniformity illumination optical system tailored specifically for efficient excitation of Fano resonant metasurfaces. Grounded in the principle of optical étendue conservation, the system performs secondary light redistribution of a halogen source using an ellipsoidal reflector. A square-shaped light integrator rod ensures highly uniform spatial intensity distribution, while a custom-designed relay lens group simultaneously accomplishes beam compression, wavefront correction, and strict confinement of the marginal ray exit angles. Experimental validation confirms that the system achieves >95.65% illumination uniformity across the target area, maintains marginal ray angles within ±11°, yields a root-mean-square (RMS) wavefront error of <λ/6, and produces a precisely sized 1.2 mm² square illumination spot aligned to the chip footprint. All output parameters fully comply with the physical excitation requirements of Fano resonant metasurfaces. By combining miniaturization, cost-effectiveness, and high optical performance, this illumination strategy provides a practical and scalable foundation for next-generation metasurface-based biochemical detection platforms.

title: Relative Range-Doppler Map for Multipath Ghost Suppression in FMCW Harmonic Radar
authors: Feng Zhao, Yuanzhe Li, Tao Yuan, Zhenhua Liang, Weidong Hu, Hongqi Fan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Radar Systems
abstract: Harmonic radar is particularly valuable in clutter-rich environments owing to its excellent clutter rejection capability. However, in frequency-modulated continuous wave harmonic radar systems, multipath propagation caused by strong linear scatterers generates ghosts on the range-Doppler map (RDM), which significantly increases the false alarm rate. To address this issue, we first establish a signal propagation model that explicitly incorporates the intermodulation effect between multi-path signals. This model reveals that the true targets and ghosts can be categorized into four distinct types, exhibiting midpoint-symmetric relationships (MSRs) on the RDM. To leverage this property for ghost suppression, we propose a new range-Doppler domain termed the relative range-Doppler map, defined with reference to the specific range and velocity. The proposed signal model and ghost suppression algorithm are validated by simulation and experimental results. Compared with the comparison methods, the proposed algorithm demonstrates a significant advantage in ghost suppression effectiveness. Furthermore, this study can provide a feasible solution for multipath interference mitigation in other radar systems where targets show MSRs.

title: Conferences: What's in Scope for Interactive Health?
authors: Daniel A. Epstein, Aneesha Singh, Pin Sym Foong
publish: Voices of SIGCHI
abstract: None

title: Evaluating User Preferences for Gender and Embodiment Scope in Human-Agent Interaction Systems
authors: Kaicheng Yan, Tianshi Bu, Jingyu Ye, Xichun Xu, Liu Yang
publish: Proceedings of the 2025 International Conference on Human-Engaged Computing
abstract: None

title: A Systematic Evaluation Framework for Business Process Reengineering Tools in Local Government: A Literature-Based Analysis
authors: Muhamad Sayuti, Bambang Syairudin, I Ketut Gunarta
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: To meet the growing demand for efficient, accountable, and sustainable public services, local governments are increasingly adopting Business Process Reengineering (BPR) to redesign their administrative workflows. The success of BPR initiatives largely depends on selecting appropriate evaluation tools that align with institutional needs across different implementation phases. This study uses a Systematic Literature Review (SLR) of Scopus-indexed articles published between 2020 and 2025 to identify and classify evaluation tools relevant to BPR in local government settings. Four key tools are examined: Structural Equation Modeling (SEM), the Technology–Organization–Environment–People–Process (TOEPP) framework, Lean Six Sigma (LSS), and the Analytic Hierarchy Process (AHP). Based on this review, we propose a phased decision-support framework: TOEPP is used for assessing organizational readiness before implementation; SEM is applied during implementation to validate causal relationships and predict success; and LSS and AHP are used after implementation for performance improvement and strategic prioritization. This integrated framework supports data-driven decision-making and promotes continuous improvement in local government reform initiatives.

title: Skin Cancer Classification: Explainable Deep Learning Framework Using Cbam-Integrated Densenet
authors: Rozy Sultana, Maimuna Akter Shawon, Rehena Sultana, Md. Khaliluzzaman
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Among the most common and deadly cancers in the world Skin cancer ranks as one of the most crucial one. It is very important to find it early for improving survival rates. Traditional diagnostic methods often demonstrate subjectivity, require significant time investment, and necessitate extensive clinical expertise, thereby necessitating the integration of deep learning and machine learning techniques into automated diagnosis. We offer a thorough method for sorting skin lesions into benign and malignant groups in this study. The images were first enhanced and then preprocessed by augmentation, normalization, and resizing. Several state-of-the-art backbones were fine-tuned, and their calibrated outputs were combined using validationdriven fusion. On held-out data, the CBAM model achieved 94% precision with 92% accuracy and consistently outperformed its individual components in our ablation analyses, indicating improved robustness over single-network baselines. These findings suggest that probability-level of proposed model offers a practical, interpretable, and deployment-friendly route to reliable skin cancer classification.

title: Performance-Maximizing Trajectory and Resource Optimization for an Energy Harvesting Intelligent Reflecting Surface-Aided UAV Relay
authors: Monzur Morshed, Apu Kumer Mohonto
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) equipped with Intelligent Reflecting Surfaces (IRS) are poised to become a key part of future wireless networks. This technology offers significant power gain which is proportional to the square of the number of the reflecting elements (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$M^{2}$</tex>). However, getting this full gain depends on having a very good and clear wireless channel. This paper looks at a UAV-IRS relay system that is designed to find the best possible channel. The UAV uses energy harvesting from radio frequency (RF) signals transmitted by a terrestrial transmitter to overcome its low battery life. We set up a new optimization problem to get the highest possible communication rate. We do this by jointly finding the best 3D flight path for the UAV and the best policy for splitting power between communication and energy harvesting. The most important part of our work is a realistic, height-dependent channel model. This model helps the UAV to dynamically find and use strong line-of-sight (LoS) channel conditions by adjusting its altitude. The problem we set up is complex and non-convex, so we solve it with an efficient method called Block Coordinate Descent (BCD). Our simulation results show that our smart, channelaware system works much better than a benchmark system that uses a simpler, fixed channel model. Crucially, we demonstrate that by optimizing its altitude, the UAV can not only improve the average channel gain but can also actively eliminate the fading effects, transforming the link into a more stable and predictable quasi-deterministic channel.

title: ORAMA: A Stereo Depth-Aware Architecture for Real-Time Exercise Coaching and Functional Mobility Assessment
authors: Orestis N. Zestas, Konstantinos I. Roumeliotis, Dimitrios N. Soumis, Kyriakos D. Kyriakou, Sofia H. Kapellaki, Kyriaki Seklou, Nikolaos D. Tselikas
publish: 2026 Panhellenic Conference on Electronics &amp; Telecommunications (PACET)
abstract: We present ORAMA, a modular virtual coaching system for home and lab-based use that combines stereo depth sensing with markerless body tracking to deliver exercise guidance and functional mobility assessment. The platform integrates a ZED 2i stereo camera and the ZED SDK (CUDA 13 / TensorRT-accelerated pipeline) within a Python software stack, using OpenCV for frame-level processing and a Qt user interface for real-time visual feedback. Depth-aware kinematic descriptors are computed from tracked 3D joints to assess posture quality and task completion, supporting both exercise sessions and standardized tests such as the Timed Up and Go and the 30s Sit-to-Stand test. The implementation emphasizes repeatability via per-session calibration and deterministic session resets, enabling iterative trials under consistent state. ORAMA provides a reproducible reference architecture for virtual coaching systems that must operate at low latency while remaining extensible to additional assessments.

title: A Comparative Study of Custom CNNs, Attention Modules, and Machine Learning Classifiers for Satellite Image Classification
authors: Mahdi Hasan, Nurunnahar Puspa, Takia Akter, Nurjahan Akhter Lipi, Al Hossain, M. Saddam Hossain Khan, Rashedul Amin Tuhin, Mohammad Rifat Ahmmad Rashid, Raihan Ul Islam, Mohammad Shahadat Hossain
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Classifying satellite data to infer land use and land cover (LULC) properties is essential for environmental monitoring and resource management. In this work, a comparative study of custom CNNs, attention-enhanced custom CNNs, pre-trained deep networks, and hybrid custom CNN + Machine Learning classifiers on the EuroSAT dataset is presented. The results show that Custom CNN + SEBlock + Random Forest achieved the best accuracy of 99.26 % and Custom CNN + SEBlock achieved accuracy of 98.3 %, outperforming pre-trained architectures. The study also incorporates a GradCAM ++ analysis to visualize the model's interpretability. Cross-dataset validation on RSI-CB256 confirmed robustness. The findings highlight the effectiveness of combining lightweight CNNs, attention modules, and ML classifiers for remote sensing classification with limited computational resources.

title: Energy Harvesting in Grid-Connected Residential PV Systems Under Partial Shading Through Advanced MPPT Techniques
authors: Md Sharif Robbani, Bappaditta Saha Chowdhury, Md Khayruzzaman Abhi, Md Mobarok Hosen Omi, Nahim Amin, Md. Minhajul Islam
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The paper presents the experiment of the work conducted on the performance of residential on-grid photovoltaic (PV) system under partial shading conditions, focusing specifically on the effectiveness of Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) algorithms and the system stability in general. To deal with irradiance variations and the related uncertainties, a fuzzy logic-based MPPT controller was utilized which allowed us to track the Global Maximum Power Point (GMPP) accurately and reduce the power losses caused by the presence of shading. According to the simulation results, the PV system under standard test conditions <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(1000 \mathrm{W} / \mathrm{m}^{2}\right)$</tex> achieves an efficiency of about 96% and a maximum electrical output of 2400 W which corresponds to a loss of 61.7% of the original. By using the fuzzy-logic MPPT strategy, the system restores the power levels up to 1364 W, and the system achieves a 48.3 percent efficiency enhancement of the local maximum. These results prove the flexibility of fuzzy-logic control in the optimization of energy production and the increase in stability of residential PV systems under changing shading conditions.

title: Lightweight Kalman Filtering for Battery Pack SOC Estimation: A Bar-Delta Approach
authors: Kazi Abdullah Al Imon, Md Sharif Robbani, Bappaditta Saha Chowdhury, Antony Ghosh Arthey, Nahiyan Ahmed, Nahim Amin
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Accurate estimation of battery state-of-charge (SOC) is a fundamental requirement for reliable and efficient battery management systems (BMS), particularly in applications such as electric vehicles and renewable energy storage. Conventional estimation techniques, including extended Kalman filtering, often face scalability and computational challenges when applied to large packs of series-connected cells. This work presents a bar-delta filtering approach for SOC estimation that combines a single full Kalman filter for pack-average SOC estimation with lightweight single-state Kalman filters to capture individual cell deviations. The proposed method was validated on a five-cell series-connected pack with initial SOC variations between 0.45 and 0.65 over a six-hour charge-discharge cycle. Results demonstrate that the estimator converges to the true SOC within ten minutes and maintains low estimation error for all cells throughout the experiment The bar-delta filtering framework achieves substantial computational savings compared to conventional multi-cell Kalman filtering approaches while preserving accuracy, making it a promising solution for scalable, real-time SOC estimation in advanced BMS implementations.

title: Contact Engineering in Novel Gr/h-BAs Heterostructure: For Semiconductor Devices
authors: Kunal, Amit Kumar Singh Chauhan, Vishvendra Singh Poonia, Sanjeev Kumar Manhas
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: Low contact resistivity is essential for high-performance semiconductor devices. In this work, we have investigated a novel heterostructure of a Graphene (Gr) and hexagonal Boron Arsenide (h-BAs) monolayers for contact engineering. We have used Density Functional Theory (DFT) combined with Non-Equilibrium Green Function (NEGF), performed by using Quantum ATK software. The electronic properties (bandgap, DOS, electron density, and effective potential) of monolayers and current–voltage characteristics of their two-terminal devices with different contact configurations were analyzed. The Gr/h-BAs heterostructure exhibits enhanced orbital overlap, leading to 65.98% and 51.01% increase in the density of states compared to graphene and h-BAs, respectively. At the interface, a potential drop of 0.060 H (1.65 eV) and an electron density increase of 0.016 Å<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-3</sup> promote efficient charge transfer. Importantly, the top-contact device exhibits a 79.03% and 71.13% reduction in contact resistivity compared to the pristine and side-contact configurations, respectively, leading to an increase in the "ON" current by 83.27% and 68.27%. These results highlight Gr/h-BAs heterostructures as promising candidates for semiconductor devices with low contact resistivity.

title:
Monolithically 3D-Stacked CFETs Based on WSe
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    /MoS
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Heterostructures: High-Gain Inverters and Logic Gates
authors: Jichao Li, Yan Hu, Weijie Sun, Shengduo Fan, Sen Wang, Yuchen Tian, Junlong Shi, Yuexi Wang, Yue Zhang, Xinliu He, Qicheng Sun, Yin Wang, Saifei Gou, Yin Xia, Zihan Xu, Yuxiang Li, Mengyang Liu, Jiawei Zhang, Wenzhong Bao
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: The semiconductor industry faces challenges in three-dimensional (3D) integration: traditional 3D integrated circuits (ICs) suffer from through-silicon via (TSV)-induced area overhead and performance limitations, monolithic 3D (M3D) integration on silicon is constrained by thermal budget issues, and conventional complementary field-effect transistors (CFETs) exhibit performance degradation with scaling. In contrast, two-dimensional transition metal dichalcogenide (TMD) materials retain their mobility even at atomic-layer thickness. This study demonstrates a chemical vapor deposition (CVD)-based monolithic 3D-stacked CFET consisting of WSe2/MoS2 heterostructures. The fabrication process is detailed, including the use of a sapphire substrate, CVD growth of monolayer films, Au electrode formation, and atomic layer deposition (ALD) of dielectric layers. Measurements show that the WSe2 pFETs achieve an average hole mobility of 4.1 cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> V<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup> s<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup>, while the MoS2 nFETs exhibit an average electron mobility of 15.7 cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> V<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup> s<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup>. The fabricated inverters demonstrate a high voltage gain of 187 V/V at VDD=4 V and the NAND and NOR gates function correctly, validating their potential for future low-power digital ICs.

title: Hybrid Ensemble Approach for Film Revenue Forecasting with Sentiment Features
authors: Warunya Udomkhwamsuk, Areenta Lertyongphati, Nawasawan Yenrompho, Nuntanut Bhooanusas
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: In the aftermath of the COVID-19 pandemic, the global film industry has faced severe financial challenges as audiences increasingly shift to media-streaming platforms. Accurate box-office forecasting is therefore crucial for studios and investors seeking to optimize production and marketing strategies. However, maintaining prediction accuracy is challenging due to numerous external factors such as social media engagement and audience sentiment. This paper presents a hybrid ensemble-based approach to film revenue forecasting using sentiment features. The proposed framework consists of two stages: (1) movie rating classification using features such as genre, cast popularity, and sentiment scores extracted from YouTube comments via a pretrained DistilBERT model; and (2) revenue prediction based on refined features from the first stage. The effectiveness of the proposed hybrid ensemble method is evaluated against the top three classification models and top four regression models in each stage. Experimental results show that the proposed approach outperforms baseline models, achieving an F1-score of 0.876 for classification and an RMSE of $\mathbf{1. 5 6 5}$ for revenue prediction.

title: Bridging the Trust Gap: An Explainable AI (XAI) Framework for Integrated Maintenance and Energy Optimization in Industrial Compressed Air Systems
authors: Radhwan Hazza’a Mohammed
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Compressed air systems are among the most energy-intensive industrial utilities, responsible for a significant share of electricity consumption and frequent costly downtime. Traditional maintenance strategies and isolated energy-saving measures often fail to address the dual challenge of reliability and efficiency in an integrated manner. While artificial intelligence has achieved high accuracy in predicting failures and optimizing energy, the opaque nature of advanced models creates a lack of trust that limits their adoption in critical industrial settings. This paper introduces a novel decision support framework that uniquely integrates predictive maintenance and energy optimization within a transparent, human-centered design. The system employs dual prediction engines for equipment health and energy performance, combined with an explanation layer that generates clear, human-interpretable justifications for every recommendation. An illustrative scenario shows how the framework identifies a high probability of bearing failure alongside rising energy consumption, and then advises timely replacement, supported by quantified benefits such as cost avoidance and energy savings. The conclusions highlight that integrating predictive maintenance with energy optimization, supported by explainable outputs, enhances trust, operational resilience, and sustainability in industrial decision-making. The broader implication is that by embedding explainability at the core of decision support, artificial intelligence can evolve from a black box into a trusted partner, enabling more sustainable and accountable engineering practices.

title: Deep Learning Based Frequency Selective Fading Channel Estimation Using SRCNN and DnCNN
authors: Kazi Subha Ibnath Obony, Al Amin Islam Ridoy, Farzana Akter, Md. Rakib Hossain
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: In wireless communication, channel estimation of a fading channel caused by multipath propagation requires a stable method to ensure proper, lossless communication. Deep learning based methods offer promising solutions by learning nonlinear relationships of the channel and adapting to all the varying channel conditions. This research introduces an image processing technique to solve the issue of unknown pilot positioning of the channel matrix by considering the timefrequency channel response as a two-dimensional image. The image processing techniques treat the channel estimation as image restoration, filling in the missing coefficients at certain points. The image generated using pilot positioning has low resolution, improved by a general pipeline using image superresolution (SR) and image restoration (IR). The proposed SR model is a super-resolution convolutional neural network (SRCNN), and the IR model is a denoising convolutional neural network (DnCNN). The restored image pixel is equivalent to the estimated channel coefficient, split into real and imaginary values. For the output calculation of the estimation, the enhanced output is mapped back to the channel domain. This approach reduces Mean Squared Error up to 84.52 % with the increased Signal to Noise Ratio. Use of deep learning for fading channel estimation solves the issue of varied channel coefficients, signifying an improvement compared to traditional methods. It also provides practical insights for not only optimizing different types of channel estimation but also opens up the possibility of designing deep learning based wireless communication receivers.

title: The Silence of the Comments: Patterns and Pitfalls in Smart Contract Code Documentation
authors: Ermanno Francesco Sannini, Lucia Simeone, Corrado Aaron Visaggio, Andrea Di Sorbo
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Software Analysis, Evolution and Reengineering - Companion (SANER-C)
abstract: In Ethereum's immutable environment, high-quality documentation is essential for users and auditors to fully understand smart contract behavior and build trust. However, an empirical, manually conducted, and comprehensive investigation that analyzes and identifies undocumented implementation details, implicit assumptions, and comment-code inconsistencies in operational smart contracts is still missing. To address this gap, this paper examines the commenting practices occurring in the source code of smart contracts through a systematic manual review of 100 up-to-date Solidity smart contract projects mined from Etherscan, divided into high-usage and low-usage groups based on the number of transactions they received. By combining quantitative analysis, validated with Fisher's exact test, and a multidimensional qualitative checklist, we identify a systemic deficiency in documentation, especially concerning smart contract-specific facets, such as critical security patterns and gas optimization strategies. Our findings show that documentation quality is generally insufficient regardless of a contract's popularity. Smart contract developers tend to prioritize functionality over verifiability, highlighting an urgent need for domain-specific documentation standards and best practices that better support the entire development lifecycle of blockchain applications.

title: Miniaturized Perovskite/a-IGZO Phototransistors with 10 μm Channels for Visible Light Detection and Imaging
authors: Maojun Sun, Rundong Hu, Jiaxin Yang, Xinxin Xu, Yufeng Jin, Hang Zhou
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: Perovskite/a-IGZO hybrid phototransistors, which integrate perovskite photoabsorbers with a-IGZO backplanes, provide a promising route towards highly sensitive and scalable light detection. However, most reported devices feature channel lengths exceeding 100 μm, thereby hindering dense integration and limiting their suitability for high-resolution imaging. Here, we presented BA<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>MA<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>Pb<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</inf>I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">13</inf> quasi-two-dimensional perovskite/a-IGZO phototransistors with miniaturized 10 μm×10 μm channels. The devices enable dual-mode light detection under both off-state and on-state operation, underscoring their potential for next-generation imaging applications.

title: Creative Self-Efficacy as a Mediator Between Work Engagement and Creativity: Evidence from Petrochemical Industry
authors: Adel Mahmoud Al Samman, Fatema Abdujalil Mohamed Alabbasi, Ibrahim Hatamleh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This study examines how creative self-efficacy (CSE) mediates the relationship between work engagement—captured through vigor, dedication, and absorption—and individual creativity among employees of the Gulf Petrochemical Industries Company (GPIC) in the Kingdom of Bahrain. Researchers employed a quantitative, descriptive, and analytical design using a structured questionnaire. Out of 250 distributed questionnaires, 220 valid responses were retrieved for analysis (response rate = 88%). Data were analyzed with SPSS v24 and SmartPLS 3.2 (PLS-SEM), including reliability and validity checks (Cronbach’s alpha and HTMT), descriptive statistics, correlations, and structural modeling. Findings indicate a statistically significant positive effect of work engagement on individual creativity; at the facet level, vigor (β = 0.322, p < .01) shows the strongest association, followed by absorption (β = 0.308, p < .01) and dedication (β = 0.268, p = .001). Work engagement also strongly predicts CSE (β = 0.873, R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = .761, p < .01), and CSE, in turn, predicts individual creativity; the indirect path confirms partial mediation (β = 0.181, R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = .732, p < .01). The study recommends capacity-building programs (such as problem analysis and thinking outside the box), reducing distractions, and encouraging job crafting to enhance engagement and creativity.

title: ML-Driven Clustering with Multi-Metric Cluster Head Selection for Prolonged Lifetime of WSNs
authors: Saima Bushra, Sk. Shariful Alam
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The major challenges in large-scale wireless sensor networks are energy efficiency and network longevity, as the nodes are powered with limited batteries and need to relay data over multi-hop networks. Conventional methods for cluster formation, cluster head selection, and multi-hop routing mostly don't consider node heterogeneity and multiple metrics for CH selection, which results in imbalanced energy consumption, early network failure, and degraded performance. To address this, we propose an integrated, data-driven framework that incorporates ML-based clustering on a PCA-reduced feature set, multi-metric cluster head selection, and an energy-efficient routing protocol. This workflow begins with collecting the sensor data and preprocessing it before performing the subsequent steps. Then we apply the PCA-enhanced Mini-Batch K-Means clustering to divide nodes into optimal clusters. Next, we implement a zone-quota balanced method to select eligible cluster heads (CHs) based on six TOPSIS criteria. Finally, we use a dynamic multi-zone multi-hop routing protocol to forward aggregated data from the selected cluster heads to the sink using next-hop selection based on a multi-criteria cost function. We simulate our proposed approach on a network of around 9,000 nodes to determine the performance matrices. Results demonstrate a longer time to the first node failure, an extended network lifespan, and lower end-to-end latency compared to the baseline protocols, EEPBC and MTE-LEACH, and prove the efficiency of our proposed approach for sustainable and reliable WSN operations.

title: A Label-Efficient Boundary-Aware Framework for SEM Segmentation of Metal Halide Perovskites
authors: Xunyong Yang, Hao Xu, Lijuan Huang, Ying Meng, Yuanyuan Shi, Qin Hu
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: Metal halide perovskites (MHPs) are promising optoelectronic semiconductors due to excellent optoelectronic properties and low-cost processing technologies. Microstructural features in MHP films are informative of device performance. Specially, the MHP film morphology analysis usually relies on scanning electron microscopy (SEM) observation. However, current deep learning methods for SEM image segmentation contend not only with the confounding effects of complex morphology but also with severe class imbalance between MHP crystal grains and grain boundaries (GBs). In this work, a coarse-to-fine, two-stage framework is introduced, coupling transfer learning with architectural refinements and a boundary-aware loss to enhance the performance of SEM image segmentation. The results demonstrate superior separation of adjacent grains, together with a 10.5% improvement in mean Intersection over Union (MIOU) over the classic U-Net baseline. This study paves an effective path toward accelerating intelligent analysis of MHP film morphology and associated optoelectronic devices.

title:
High-Quality PVD-WS
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Thin Films via H
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    S Annealing Enabling Suppression of S-S Bonds
authors: Soma Ito, Naoki Matsunaga, Kaede Teraoka, Jaehyo Jang, Taiga Fuse, Shunsuke Nozawa, Iriya Muneta, Takuya Hoshii, Kuniyuki Kakushima, Hitoshi Wakabayashi
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: Ultrathin tungsten disulfide (WS<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>) films grown by physical vapor deposition (PVD) are a promising channel material for future transistors. However, these films often suffer from sulfur vacancies and poor crystallinity. To resolve these issues, post-deposition annealing in a hydrogen sulfide (H2S) atmosphere was investigated. The X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS) confirmed that annealing above 250°C restores the ideal S/W stoichiometric ratio of 2.0. Furthermore, the Raman and XRD analyses indicated that a 550°C anneal improves crystallinity. Crucially, the S 2p XPS spectrum revealed that H2 S annealing effectively compensates for sulfur vacancies without forming the detrimental S-S bonds observed after conventional sulfur vapor annealing. These findings demonstrate that H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> S annealing is a robust method for producing high-quality and S-S bond-free ultrathin WS<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> films, enabling their applications in advanced electronic devices.

title: Voting-Based Feature Selection on Hybrid Feature Representations for Bangla Sentiment Analysis
authors: Anjan Debnath, Al-Mahmud
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Understanding public opinion on social media and other digital platforms has made sentiment analysis of Bangla text increasingly crucial. However, challenges, including highdimensional feature spaces, data sparsity, and language complexity, continue to restrict performance in this field. This study presents a hybrid approach that captures the semantic and statistical properties of Bangla text by combining BanglaBERT embeddings and TF-IDF features. To overcome dimensionality and redundancy, high-rank features are identified using a votingbased feature selection method that integrates mutual information, ANOVA F-test, and XGBoost feature importance. Principal component analysis (PCA) is also assessed for comparison. Then, this study applies several classifiers, including KNN, DT, RF, and LR, LightGBM, and MLP, to evaluate performance. Among them, the MLP classifier achieved the maximum accuracy of 83.16% and F1-score of 0.83. According to experimental findings on a benchmark dataset of Bangla drama and TV series reviews, the proposed method outperforms a recent existing study. To this end, this study demonstrates that constructing high-rank features from hybrid representations under the direction of voting-based selection offers a solid foundation for sentiment analysis in Bangla.

title: Simple Controller Designs for Bio-Medical Applications
authors: Petros Tolis, Julia Nako, Costas Psychalinos
publish: 2026 Panhellenic Conference on Electronics &amp; Telecommunications (PACET)
abstract: Simple designs of controllers which are suitable for biomedical applications of glucose level and dose of irinotecan control are presented in this work. Both integer and non-integer-order proportional-integral controllers are considered with the last ones approximated by an appropriate curve-fitting based method. The resulting rational integer-order impedance function is realized using a Foster type-I RC network. The behavior of the resulting schemes is evaluated through the employment of the OrCAD PSpice software.

title: Hybrid ML/DL Framework for Arthritis Risk and Severity Prediction
authors: Sanjeewa Sandeepani, Shehan Amarasooriya
publish: 2026 IEEE International Research Conference on Smart Computing and Systems Engineering (SCSE)
abstract: Arthritis is a common chronic disease with significant consequences for the quality of life among older populations. Early diagnosis of arthritis can help in preserving positive health outcomes through mitigating the progression of the disease. In this study, a hybrid predictive framework was introduced that takes individual lifestyle parameters along with the radiographic imaging of the patient as parameters that have not been studied in the Sri Lankan context before. Data were collected from Gampaha Wickramarachchi Ayurveda Hospital, and the data were split into two main data sets: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 2 0 0}$</tex> records of lifestyle factors and clinical manifestations, and 598 diagnostic X-radiography images of knee joints. Supervised Machine Learning algorithms have been applied to the textual dataset, and the best validation accuracy of 96 % for the prediction of the likelihood of Arthritis by employing the Random Forest (RF) classifier. Patients at high risk were graded into three groups using deep learning architectures (ResNet-50 and a convolutional neural network). CNN validation accuracy was 95 % and 97 % for training accuracy. The outcomes of this research have been operationalized through a web application that integrates the two models, enabling arthritis risk prediction based on lifestyle and symptom data, followed by severity classification through X-ray analysis when risk is detected.

title: Hybrid Open-Switch Fault Diagnosis in 3-Phase T-Type Inverter
authors: Jeevan N D, Niraj Kumar Dewangan, Krishna Kumar Gupta, Sunil K Singla, Pallavee Bhatnagar, Karthik B M
publish: 2026 IEEE Bangalore Humanitarian Technology Conference (B-HTC)
abstract: Multilevel inverters (MLIs) are extensively used in renewable energy systems, grid-connected converters, and industrial drives due to their superior power quality and reduced harmonic distortion. However, as the number of semiconductor switches increases, the probability of switch failures increases drastically. This may lead to a reduction in the reliability of the system. This paper presents a hybrid fault diagnosis approach for open circuit switch faults in a three-phase five-level T-type MLI based on ensemble empirical mode decomposition. The normalized output voltage signals are decomposed into intrinsic mode functions, from which kurtosis and energy entropy features are extracted to capture fault-related characteristics. For fault classification, four machine learning techniques: Decision Tree, Random Forest (RF), Extreme Gradient Boosting, and Gaussian Naive Bayes are used and evaluated. Among them, the RF classifier achieves the highest accuracy of 99.61% and is employed for the online localization of individual switch OCFs. MATLAB/Simulink simulation results validate the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed method for practical power conversion applications.

title: Performance Evaluation of an Erlang Loss System with Server Failures and a Single Repair Facility
authors: Konstantinos Lolis, Marinos Vlasakis, Ioannis Moscholios, Irene Keramidi, Dimitris Uzunidis, Georgios Bouloukakis, Nikolaos Tselikas, Michael Logothetis
publish: 2026 Panhellenic Conference on Electronics &amp; Telecommunications (PACET)
abstract: Loss models of fixed capacity constitute a fundamental tool in teletraffic theory, with the classical Erlang loss model being widely used for dimensioning purposes. However, practical communication systems often experience time-varying capacity due to server failures and repairs, which significantly affects Call Blocking Probabilities (CBP). This paper studies an Erlang loss system where busy servers may fail. Failed servers are repaired by a single shared repair facility, while in-service calls are lost upon server failure. Three different approaches for determining CBP are examined. The first one provides exact results through the solution of a 2D Markov chain but becomes computationally demanding for large systems. The second one, known as the performability method, offers a simple approximation but allows failures of idle servers. The third approach employs state aggregation and restricts failures to busy servers. Through analytical comparisons, we evaluate the accuracy of the two approximate methods against the exact solution. Results show that the third approach consistently outperforms the performability method.

title: Real-Time Performance Prediction Using Tiny Machine Learning in Advanced Catalytic Systems
authors: Hakan Karasu, Veysi Adın, Rashid Ali, Fredrik Sikström
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: Electrochemical CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> reduction requires real-time monitoring to assess catalyst efficiency and detect degradation patterns, yet current approaches rely on resource-intensive computing infrastructure. Here we present a lightweight machine learning model deployed on resource-constrained microcontrollers for real-time performance prediction. We developed a one-dimensional convolutional neural network with $\mathbf{1 1, 9 2 3}$ parameters that simultaneously predicts total current, CO Faradaic efficiency and $\mathbf{H}_{\mathbf{2}}$ Faradaic efficiency from linear sweep voltammetry data, achieving mean absolute errors of $0.038 \mathrm{~A}, 1.97 \%$ and 1.97%, respectively. Systematic comparison with a traditional convolutional neural network architecture and an attentionenhanced variant demonstrates that the proposed model achieves comparable prediction accuracy with significantly reduced model complexity. Post-training quantization compressed the model to 22.31 kB, enabling deployment on ESP32-S3 and Arduino Nano 33 BLE microcontrollers. Our work establishes the feasibility of accurate, real-time electrochemical performance prediction for autonomous experimental monitoring in resource-limited environments.

title:
Direct Deposition of High-k van der Waals Dielectric for Top-Gated MoS
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    FETs Enabling Reconfigurable Logic and 2T0C DRAM toward BEOL-Compatible 3D Integration
authors: You-Jia Huang, Hui-Chun Huang, Bor-Wei Liang, Hung-Wei Yen, Min-Hung Lee, Yann-Wen Lan
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: Two-dimensional material-based FETs hold great promise but face significant challenges as a strong candidate for integration with Si-based devices in BEOL 3DICs. The fabrication process of top-gate dielectrics and the presence of dangling bonds are major factors that degrade channel transport and hinder VLSI. In this work, we successfully demonstrate top-gate MoS<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> MOSFET using PVD high-k vdW dielectric, Sb<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>. With EOT of 5.2 nm, the device achieves on/off ratio 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</sup> and D<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">it</inf> of 2×10¹¹ cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-2</sup>eV<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup>. By tuning the voltage ranges of the top gate and back gate, the transistor can operate under different logic gate configurations within each single device. Furthermore, two FETs are interconnected to form a 2T0C DRAM structure that exhibits memory retention under a 10 ms pulse operation. The use of inorganic oxide as high-k dielectric and the proposed 2T0C layout demonstrate strong potential for advancing 2D material applications in future manufacturing technologies.

title: Unifying Adult and Pediatric Domains: Multi-Dataset Deep Learning for Fracture Classification, Detection, and Segmentation
authors: Md Saib Hossain, Md Maynul Hossen, Iffat Nur, Md Ashraful Islam, Shahariar Sarkar, Tasmin Sanjida, Azizur Rahman Anik
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Fracture detection from radiographs is critical across both adult and pediatric populations, yet existing public datasets are isolated by age group and anatomical site. This study systematically evaluates deep learning (DL) models (ResNet, YOLOv8, YOLOv11, Faster R-CNN, and DeepLabV3) for fracture classification, detection, and segmentation using (i) FracAtlas, an adult multi-organ fracture dataset, and (ii) GRAZPEDWRI-DX, a pediatric wrist dataset. Training models independently on FracAtlas, followed by a unified training pipeline that harmonizes label spaces across both datasets, enabling models to consume adult and pediatric radiographs as a single input stream. Using unified training improves crossdomain generalization, reduces overfitting, and yields consistent gains in classification AUC (+3.8%), detection mAP@0.5 (+4.2%), and segmentation Dice (+2.7%) on held-out test sets. Beyond performance, multi-dataset training narrows the gap between pediatric and adult domains, providing more reliable predictions in clinically challenging cases. Moreover, the study also suggest that integrating heterogeneous datasets offers a scalable pathway toward foundation models for musculoskeletal imaging.

title: 3D Simulations for Fin Geometry Effects on the Linearity and On-Resistance of AlGaN/GaN Multi-Channel Fin-HEMTs
authors: Hui Jiao, Peiran Wang, Xiaowei Ma, Wenchuan Tao, Qing Wang, Hongyu Yu
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: In this work, the effects of fin geometry on the linearity and on-resistance (R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on</inf>) of AlGaN/GaN multi-channel Fin-HEMTs are systematically investigated using three-dimensional technology computer aided design (3D TCAD) simulations. Compared with baseline planar HEMTs (BSL HEMTs), the multichannel Fin-HEMTs exhibit significantly improved linearity and conduction performance. The simulation results further reveal distinct trends in multi-channel Fin-HEMTs characteristics associated with variable fin geometry. Specifically, a larger fin length increases the carrier path leading to increased R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on</inf> and degraded linearity. In contrast, narrower fins leads to higher R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on</inf> and better linearity, which is attributed to the stronger channel confinement. Alternatively, increasing fin height reduces R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on</inf> and better linearity. Finally, the duty ratio (α) strongly influences inter-fin coupling. These findings provide valuable physical insights and practical design guidelines for optimizing the design of GaN-based multi-channel Fin-HEMTs.

title: A SPICE-Compatible Compact Model for Ferroelectric-Gated GaN-HEMTs
authors: Abhishek Kumar, Dariskhem Pyngrope, Avirup Dasgupta
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: We present a compact model for ferroelectric-gated GaN high-electron-mobility transistors (HEMTs). The model consists of a ferroelectric capacitor model combined with an industry-standard advanced SPICE model for HEMTs to capture the behavior of ferroelectric-gated HEMTs. The proposed model is based on the nucleation-limited switching approach, which captures the statistical multidomain dynamics of ferroelectric materials. We have validated the model with measured data from ferroelectric-gated HEMT devices. The model accurately captures the device behavior.

title: EndoVision: A Hybrid Deep Learning Framework for Endoscopic Image Classification
authors: Prottoy Kundu, Arnob Das Shacha, Sabbir Hossain Durjoy, S.M. Abdullah Al Muhib, Rejowan Arifin Nayeem, Md. Emon Shikder, M. Humayet Islam
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The gastrointestinal (GI) system is an important part of the human body that breaks down food into necessary nutrients and removes the unused portion from the body as feces. This system includes the mouth, esophagus, stomach, small intestine, large intestine, liver, gallbladder, and pancreas. If any organ malfunctions, it negatively affects the entire body. Gastrointestinal (GI) diseases are diagnosed correctly at an early stage, and the chances of treatment are greatly increased. These diseases are usually diagnosed using endoscopy technology, but this process is dependent on the experience of the specialist. As a result, small lesions or symptoms are often not detected due to differences in observation, which delays early diagnosis and complicates treatment. Gastrointestinal (GI) diseases such as infections, ulcers, and tumors can be lifethreatening. Although endoscopy is effective in detecting these diseases, it is time-consuming and experience-dependent, as only a few of the thousands of frames contain disease symptoms. Deep learning-based artificial intelligence plays a special role in addressing this challenge. The hybrid stacking ensemble model proposed in this study has shown high accuracy and sensitivity in detecting GI diseases, which helps in unbiased and effective diagnosis. The best performance in our experiments came from the proposed Hybrid model of CNN, LSTM and ViT, which attained 97.67% accuracy. These outputs are considerably higher than those of single models, including ViT (accuracy of 93.97%), CNN (90.37%), and LSTM (80.07%), and other networks, including DenseNet121, GAT, GCN, InceptionV3, and MobileNet. This high performance indicates that our method is very reliable for detecting gastrointestinal diseases based on endoscopic imaging, facilitating less demand for manual detection and higher possibility of earliest diagnosis.

title: Continuous Delivery Platform Development in the Cloud-Native Era
authors: Zhen Guang Yang, Alvin Sarraga Alon, Leonard L. Alejandro, Jesus Paguigan, Carmelita H. Benito
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: With the rapid development of cloud computing technologies and the widespread adoption of microservices architecture, traditional software development and deployment models can no longer meet the strict requirements of modern enterprises for rapid delivery, high availability, and security. This paper proposes and implements an enterprise-level cloud-native continuous delivery platform that adopts a Flask server-side rendering architecture and integrates core technological innovations including service-level fine-grained permission isolation, unified management of massive Kubernetes service configurations, and production environment stability enhancement. Through innovative production environment stability control mechanisms, the platform implements key functions such as core service mandatory waiting mechanisms, monitoring screenshot upload verification, release process pause/resume control, rapid rollback mechanisms, release time window control, automated security scanning, and release notification closed-loop management. Deeply integrated with mainstream CI/CD toolchains such as Jenkins, it supports end-to-end automated delivery from code commit to production, covering advanced deployment strategies such as canary releases and blue-green deployments. According to the evaluation strategy indicators, compared to the traditional "cloud-based virtual machines + Jenkins" model, the new platform has improved deployment efficiency, rollback time, resource utilization, and fault recovery capability by 85.7%, 85.5%, 52.1%, and 69.6% respectively, providing strong technical support for enterprise digital transformation and meeting ISO 25010 software quality standards and security compliance requirements for enterprise applications.

title: Comparative CFD Study of HTF Connection Configurations for Optimized Energy Management in Latent Thermal Storage of CSP Systems
authors: Ahmad W. Omari, Basil Ibrahim, Hamdy M. Mohamed, Hend ElZefzafy
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This study presents a computational investigation of multi-stage latent heat thermal energy storage (LHTES) systems integrated with organic phase change materials (PCMs) for concentrated solar power (CSP) applications. The novelty of this work lies in evaluating the influence of heat transfer fluid (HTF) connection strategies on melting dynamics and system efficiency, with a particular emphasis on the trade-off between localized melting enhancement and overall thermal stability. The cascaded PCM configuration, comprising Octadecanoic, RT58, and Dodecanoic, was modeled within two geometrical layouts: Model 1 with extended HTF connections and Model 2 with direct PCM coupling. Using computational fluid dynamics (CFD) in ANSYS Fluent with the solidification/melting model, simulations were performed under controlled inlet temperature (100 °C).The results reveal that eliminating HTF connections accelerates localized melting rates by reducing flow resistance and promoting direct PCM–fluid interaction. However, extended HTF pathways improve staged melting and layering effects, mitigating thermal shock and ensuring uniform temperature distribution. Model 1 achieved more controlled thermal progression, while Model 2 favored faster early-stage melting but required longer to reach full PCM liquefaction. These findings underscore the critical role of HTF connection design in balancing charging time, heat transfer uniformity, and operational reliability. The study provides valuable insights into optimizing LHTES integration for CSP plants, demonstrating that careful geometric tuning of HTF pathways enhances both energy efficiency and long-term system stability.

title: Ethical and Legal Decision-Making Frameworks for Data Governance in Smart Cities: Towards a Digital Sovereignty Model
authors: Mohamed Abdelsalam, Amal Mohamed Abedel Aty, Alaa Abouahmed, Mahmoud Sabry Abdelaziz Ebrahim
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: Smart cities increasingly depend on large-scale data collection, AI-enabled analytics, and interconnected infrastructure to manage urban services. However, this technological expansion raises critical legal and ethical challenges related to privacy, digital rights, and state accountability. This paper examines data governance in smart cities through a legal-ethical decision-making framework, focusing on how regulatory systems can balance technological innovation with the protection of individual rights. The study analyzes international regulatory models, such as the GDPR in the European Union [1], and contrasts them with emerging data sovereignty approaches in the GCC and MENA region [2]. By conceptualizing data regulation as a structured decision process rather than a post-hoc compliance mechanism, the paper proposes a digital sovereignty model that embeds ethical safeguards into engineering governance. This approach demonstrates that the legitimacy of smart city transformation is contingent not only on technological capacity but also on legally grounded and ethically coherent decision logic.

title: The Role of Community Adaptive Capacity and Policy Flexibility in Rice-Based Food Security: A Systematic Review
authors: Annisa Indah Pratiwi, Elisa Kusrini, Winda Nurcahyo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: This study systematically reviews the existing body of literature on community adaptive capacity and policy flexibility within the broader framework of rice supply chain resilience. Despite growing scholarly attention to food system sustainability, current studies have yet to propose a comprehensive analytical framework capable of evaluating how government policies respond to dynamic risks and uncertainties across the supply chain. Furthermore, empirical indicators that measure the social endurance and adaptive capacity of institutional agencies in managing challenges such as land-use conversion, climate variability, and supply–demand fluctuations remain limited. To address these gaps, a Systematic Literature Mapping (SLM) coupled with K-diagram analysis was conducted using leading academic databases Scopus, ScienceDirect, and Google Scholar covering publications from 2020 to 2025. This combined approach was designed to identify emerging knowledge trends, thematic gaps, and methodological orientations across relevant peer-reviewed studies. The findings highlight the critical need to formulate adaptive and resilient intervention strategies that incorporate six essential dimensions of food security—availability, access, utilization, stability, agency, and sustainability while accounting for the inherent complexities and uncertainties of contemporary food supply chain systems.

title: Safety-Critical IoMT Security: A Lightweight, Cost-Aware Approach at the Network Edge
authors: Md. Asraful Islam Khan, Md Mahinur Alam, Md Sakibul Islam, Kanita Jerin Tanha, Sadia Salma, Mohammad Mofasserul Islam
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Hospital networks require intrusion detection systems that are both practical and accurate, especially in situations where computational resources are scarce. For the Internet of Medical Things (IoMT), this study suggests an inexpensive and lightweight IDS. The technique uses Random Forest for top-K feature selection, then a compact CNN-LSTM architecture. A contextual-bandit Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) algorithm is used in a final fine-tuning stage. It is guided by a reward matrix that penalizes missed attacks more severely than false alarms. System operators can adjust detection priorities based on clinical risk levels thanks to this design. The suggested method achieved 96.79 % accuracy, a macro-F1 score of 0.9242, a Matthews correlation coefficient (MCC) of 0.9557, and an expected calibration error (ECE) of 0.0336 when tested on the CIC-IoMT 2024 dataset. With an average CPU latency of 0.71 ms per network flow, the model is only 0.147 MB in size. The percentage of attacks incorrectly classified as benign decreased to 1.67 % when the penalty for missed detections was increased. With a latency of 0.74 ms and a model size of 0.146 MB, the system achieved 97.95 % accuracy, 0.9524 macro-F1, 0.9056 MCC, and 0.0140 ECE in binary classification. The false-positive rate was 5.13 %, and the false-negative rate was 1.64 %. These findings show that, without increasing model size or computation time, cost-conscious training techniques can significantly lower missed detections. The system is appropriate for edge deployment and clinical evaluation due to its small size and well-calibrated outputs.

title: Dual Band Polarization Insensitive Metamaterialbased Em Absorber for 5G EMI Shielding
authors: Nishan Das, M. H. Mahmud Sajib, Md. Mirajul Islam, Touhidul Alam, Mohammad Tariqul Islam, Rezaul Azim
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: This article proposes a dual-band metamaterialbased absorber (MMA) for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 G}$</tex> Sub-6 GHz electromagnetic interference (EMI) shielding, demonstrating absorption at 3.5 GHz and 4.6 GHz. Designed on a low-cost FR4 substrate with electrical dimensions of approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.25 \lambda \times 0.25 \lambda \times 0.013 \lambda$</tex> (where <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\lambda$</tex> is calculated at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3. 5 G H z}$</tex>), the MMA achieves maximum absorptions of 90 % and 99.5 % through a resonating patch consisting of two concentric circular copper rings. Simulations were performed using CST Microwave Studio 2025, revealing angular stability of 0 to 30 degrees in both TE and TM modes. The MMA exhibits negative permittivity over the frequency ranges <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3}. \mathbf{4}-\mathbf{3}. \mathbf{6} \text{GHz}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4}. \mathbf{5}-\mathbf{4}. 7 \text{GHz}$</tex>, along with corresponding near-zero permeability, ensuring its single-negative (SN) characteristics. This MMA can be integrated into various electronic devices or systems for effective EMI shielding against 3.5 GHz and 4.6 GHz signals.

title: Federated Contrastive Learning for Privacy-Preserving ECG Arrhythmia Classification
authors: Md. Labib, Md Foysal Rahaman, Ahammed Ekrak Utsha, Shadman Sakib Faruki, Sharfuddin Mahmood
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Accurate detection of ECG arrhythmias plays a critical role in early diagnosis of cardiovascular diseases, yet traditional methods that rely on centralized data aggregation raise concerns about patient privacy and data sharing regulations. This study proposes a novel framework that integrates federated learning and contrastive learning to address these challenges in a decentralized setting. The proposed approach enables multiple healthcare clients to collaboratively train deep models without sharing sensitive data, while simultaneously leveraging contrastive learning to enhance the quality of ECG feature representations. Experiments are conducted on a public ECG arrhythmia classification dataset using five non-IID data partitions reflecting various class imbalance scenarios. The results demonstrate that the federated contrastive learning framework achieves competitive classification performance, with an F1-score of 0.9789 in the fully balanced setting and improved recall in imbalanced scenarios, highlighting its robustness in detecting minority classes. Key techniques such as client-specific augmentations, mixup regularization, and federated batch normalization contribute to improved generalization under data heterogeneity. This work provides empirical insights into the convergence behavior, representational quality, and privacy-preserving benefits of contrastive objectives in federated environments. The proposed solution shows strong potential for scalable and secure deployment in realworld clinical settings where data diversity and privacy are paramount.

title: Exploring Compute-in-Memory Architecture with Various Peripheral Circuits and Neural Networks
authors: Jonghyun Ko, Jiseong Im, Jong-Ho Lee
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: Compute-in-memory (CIM) stores weights in nonvolatile memory to perform vector–matrix multiplications (VMMs) via Ohm’s and Kirchhoff’s laws, reducing energy and latency. We present a comprehensive and comparative study of CIM architectures employing different peripheral circuits and neural network schemes. Fixing the nonvolatile memory array (AND-type flash) and technology node (45 nm), we evaluate four CIM architectures on a 9-layer conventional DNN (CIFAR-10) and a spiking DNN. By replacing ADCs with PWMs in only the first two layers, we reduce dynamic energy by 18.5 % and area by 19.8 % with no throughput loss. Substituting LIF neurons yields a 14.9 % energy saving at the cost of a small area overhead. ADC-based baseline achieves 87.05 % accuracy on the DNN and 81.85 % on the spiking DNN, while hybrid schemes reduce quantization loss.

title: Integrating Country Clustering with Hybrid Graph-Spectral Forecasting of Agri-Food $\text{CO}_{2}$ Emissions: A Bangladesh Case Study
authors: Md. Mamun Hossain, Nusrat Kaniz Khan
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: We propose a unified framework that integrates advanced hybrid forecasting with country-level clustering to model and interpret global agri-food <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CO}_{2}$</tex> emissions. At its core is a novel Graph Laplacian-Short-Time Fourier Transform-Conformal Prediction (GL-STFT-CP) model, which leverages graph-based signal processing to capture cross-country interdependencies and STFT-derived spectral embeddings to represent complex temporal patterns. Compared to ARIMA and LSTM baselines, GL-STFT-CP achieves superior predictive accuracy and delivers calibrated multi-step uncertainty intervals, addressing key challenges of nonlinearity, spatial heterogeneity, and forecast reliability. As an illustrative case, we present 10-year forecasts for Bangladesh, where GL-STFT-CP provides tighter, calibrated prediction intervals and more accurate trends than ARIMA. Exploratory analysis also contrasts Bangladesh with the South Asia region, contextualizing national trajectories within broader dynamics. Building on the global forecasts, we apply hierarchical clustering to multi-decade emission trajectories, revealing two coherent groups: a high-emission, rapidly growing cluster dominated by emerging economies, and a larger group of moderate- or low-growth countries, including many OECD members. Silhouette analysis <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(s \approx 0.91)$</tex> and bootstrap stability tests (ARI <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 1.0)$</tex> confirm the robustness of this partitioning. Finally, we derive differentiated policy implications: high-growth countries need efficiency and low-carbon practices, while mature economies can focus on technology diffusion and refining existing practices. By linking advanced forecasting with clustering-based diagnostics, we demonstrate how an integrated pipeline yields data-driven, cluster-specific insights for climate policy and sustainable agriculture planning.

title: IoT-Integrated Pharmaceutical Packaging: Bridging Safeguarding Quality & Patient Safety
authors: Rakibul Hasan, Ratin Barua, Anup kamal Dipta, Tonny Chowdhury, Aditi Bhattacharjee, Trina Palit, Md Imam Uddin Bhuiyan, Muhammad Tazrian Taharin
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) has revolutionized healthcare by providing new solutions to address various healthcare challenges. Intelligent pharmaceutical packaging designed using IoT and GSM technologies introduces a smart and integrated approach to medication management. IoTintegrated pharmaceutical packaging bridges safeguarding quality and patient safety, ensuring a more secure and efficient healthcare system. This paper presents an overview of the design, implementation approach, and benefits of smart pharmaceutical packaging utilizing IoT and GSM. The system is equipped with various sensors and actuators to monitor drug adherence and ensure the secure storage of medications. It is also integrated with GSM technology to provide remote monitoring and notifications for both patients and healthcare providers. Patients can receive medication reminders, maintain drug history records, and get immediate alerts for missed doses through a user-friendly mobile application. This system enhances outcomes for both patients and healthcare professionals-patients benefit from improved adherence and better health, while healthcare providers can remotely monitor compliance, enabling early intervention and reducing complications from non-adherence. This article explores the technology behind smart pharmaceutical packaging, its implications for healthcare, and the challenges and opportunities in its implementation.

title: Deep Learning-Based Classification of Mango Diseases Using InceptionResNetV2: A Robust Approach for Agricultural Diagnostics
authors: Chaitanya Singla, T. Roseline Velankanni, K Chokkanathan, S. Dhivya, Samridhi Sharma, Manik Rakhra
publish: 2025 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications (DASA)
abstract: In this study, the InceptionResNetV2 deep learning model is applied for the classification of mango diseases to improve agricultural practices by accurately identifying diseases. A dataset of 862 images was utilized, encompassing five classes: Black Mould Rot, Alternaria, Anthracnose, Healthy, and Stem End Rot. Results show that the model resulted in an overall accuracy of 94%, successfully identifying healthy and diseased mangoes. A training and validation process showed a large drop in loss, indicating successful learning, and a confusion matrix was used to find which labels were misclassified. High Precision and recall rates were noted in the classification report, especially for the Healthy class. Deep learning techniques are shown to be a promising tool for agricultural diagnostics, with the presented research demonstrating their potential to reliably identify mango diseases for farmers and researchers. This future work can be carried on by integrating other data sources to improve model robustness and also by investigating more advanced architectures for better classification accuracy over the varied datasets.

title: Resilient Load Frequency Control Against DoS Attacks Using Hybrid LSTM-PID Defense Scheme
authors: Md. Faiyaj Ahmed Limon, Ifte Khairul Amin
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: The increasing reliance of modern power systems on cyber-physical infrastructures makes Load Frequency Control (LFC) particularly vulnerable to cyberattacks such as Denial of Service (DoS). This paper presents a novel hybrid defense methodology that synergizes a Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO)-tuned Proportional-Integral-Derivative (PID) controller with a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM)-based anomaly detector to enhance resilience under adversarial conditions. A comprehensive dataset generated from a two-area interconnected power system under sequential load disturbances was used to train the LSTM network for robust ACE signal estimation. The proposed architecture employs an intelligent switch mechanism that replaces compromised ACE signals with LSTM-predicted values during DoS attacks, ensuring continuous and stable control action. Simulation results demonstrate that while DoS attacks cause severe frequency deviations (up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 0.25$</tex> p.u. in Area 1 and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$-0.16$</tex> p.u. in Area 2) and tie-line power oscillations (up to 0.035 p.u.) in conventional systems, the hybrid framework achieves superior performance in mitigating these effects. The LSTM-based detector successfully estimates missing ACE signals, reducing frequency fluctuations and maintaining system stability, confirming the effectiveness of integrating intelligent anomaly detection with traditional control strategies for improving the robustness and security of cyber-physical power systems.

title: Experimental Characterization on Magnetically Tuned Resonance of YIG-Loaded Planar Antenna
authors: Rheyuniarto Sahlendar Asthan, Junas Haidi, Budi Syihabuddin, Asep Sofwan Faturohman Alqap, Mohammad Ridwan Effendi, Achmad Munir
publish: 2026 IEEE International Workshop on Antenna Technology (iWAT)
abstract: This work presents an investigation of the magnetic tuning behavior of Yttrium Iron Garnet (YIG). The study is carried out through experimental characterization, in which a square-shaped YIG element is embedded within a planar antenna configured using RT/Duroid 5880 as the host substrate. This arrangement is designed to demonstrate resonant frequency tuning under the influence of an external bias magnetic field applied by a permanent magnet, owing to magnetic properties of YIG. Different scenarios corresponding to the magnetic saturation conditions, i.e., demagnetized and magnetized states, are implemented to investigate the effects of electromagnetic interaction. The characterization results reveal that the resonant frequency can be tuned by varying the level of external magnetic bias.

title: A Deep Learning Approach for Balancing Accuracy and Efficiency in Handwritten Mathematical Symbol Recognition
authors: Shajnin Haque Aishy, Md Nafis Walid, Sadullah Karimi, Mohiuddin Ahmed
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Handwritten mathematical symbol recognition is a fundamental task in intelligent education systems and digital content processing, yet it remains challenging due to high intraclass variability, inter-class similarity, and dataset imbalance. This work introduces a compact Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) designed for efficient recognition of handwritten symbols under limited computational resources. The model is trained on a large-scale dataset of 3,75,974 images spanning 82 symbol categories. To ensure robust learning, preprocessing techniques such as adaptive binarization, morphological refinement, and normalization were applied, followed by a hybrid resampling and enhancement strategy to address imbalance while preserving symbol integrity. The proposed CNN, which contains only 1.03 million parameters, achieves a classification accuracy of 99.01%, outperforming several existing approaches while significantly reducing computational cost. Furthermore, interpretability is enhanced using Grad-CAM visualization, providing insights into the decision-making process and highlighting potential sources of misclassification. These results demonstrate the effectiveness of lightweight CNNs for real-time handwritten symbol recognition in educational and mobile applications.

title: MPC-Based AGC and Tie-Line Control for Power Systems in the Presence of Renewable Energy Sources
authors: Kia Saedifar, Ali Doust-Mohammadi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Protection and Automation of Power Systems (IPAPS)
abstract: As variable renewable generation becomes more prevalent, the challenge of maintaining real-time control over system frequency and tie-line flows intensifies. This paper proposes an automatic generation control (AGC) scheme based on model predictive control (MPC) that explicitly incorporates tie-line bias control and two distinct time scales for slow conventional generators and fast inverter-based resources. The MPC objective function penalizes Area Control Error (ACE) and tie-line deviations, with hard constraints on frequency, tie-line loading, unit outputs, and ramp rates. Simulation results on a two-area system demonstrate that under realistic disturbances, the proposed AGC significantly outperforms conventional PID-based AGC. It achieves lower mean and peak frequency deviations and maintains tie-line power closer to its scheduled value. The results suggest that the proposed MPC-based two-rate AGC framework could effectively address the critical challenges of frequency and tie-line control in the evolving low-inertia power grid.

title: Schizophrenia Detection from EEG Signals: A Lobe-Centric Approach
authors: Zubair Ibne Kabir, MD Raihan Mahmud, Mahin Ibn Alam, Monjur Morshed
publish: 2025 28th International Conference on Computer and Information Technology (ICCIT)
abstract: Schizophrenia is a complex psychiatric disorder that disrupts cognition and neural processing, making early and reliable diagnosis challenging. Electroencephalography (EEG) offers a non-invasive view of brain activity, yet its high dimensionality and susceptibility to noise demand effective computational approaches. We present a deep learning framework that models EEG signals through five parallel branches, each corresponding to a distinct frequency band. Within each branch, Squeeze-andExcitation (SE) blocks adaptively recalibrate channel-wise responses, while 1D convolutional neural networks (CNNs) extract temporal patterns from the recalibrated signals. The representations from all bands are fused to classify schizophrenia. The novelty of this work lies in integrating a region-specific perspective, where frontal, parietal, occipital, and temporal signals are analyzed independently to uncover localized biomarkers. Unlike global EEG models, our approach highlights how certain regions, particularly frontal and parietal, provide stronger discriminatory power. This region-aware design not only enhances accuracy but also improves interpretability, offering meaningful insights for clinical application in schizophrenia detection.

title: A Practical and Controllable P-type Doping Method for 2D Semiconductors
authors: Yan Hu, Yue Zhang, Yuexi Wang, Shicheng Zeng, Chuming Sheng, Yuxuan Zhu, Xiangqi Dong, Zhejia Zhang, Jinshu Zhang, Yi Wang, Xu Wang, Saifei Gou, Zihan Xu, Yin Wang, Zhengzong Sun, Yin Xia, Wenzhong Bao
publish: 2026 10th IEEE Electron Devices Technology &amp; Manufacturing Conference (EDTM)
abstract: Two-dimensional (2D) semiconductors have emerged as highly promising candidates for future technology nodes. However, achieving effective and reliable doping strategies remains a critical challenge. In this work, we propose a controllable doping process for MoTe2 and WSe2 by employing remote plasma treatment to modulate the threshold voltage (VTH) and enhance the on-state current (Ion). This study establishes a practical and controllable doping strategy for 2D semiconductors, providing valuable insights into their future device applications.

title: Exploring Gemini AI’s Text-to-Image Capabilities for Adaptive Learning in Education
authors: Ronel F. Ramos, Roman M. De Angel, Juan Paulo H. Magcuyao, Alfredo L. Calimbo
publish: 2026 International Conference on Advances in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AAIML)
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) is reshaping education by enabling adaptive and personalized experiences. This study explores the integration of Gemini AI’s text-to-image generation into an adaptive learning environment for undergraduate BSIT students. Using a mixed-method developmental design with 100 participants, we analyzed learning gains through pre/post testing, motivation via the IMMS survey, interaction telemetry, and focus group discussions (FGDs). Quantitative analyses revealed substantial improvement from pre-test to post-test, with a significant paired t-test and an ANOVA indicating differences across performance subgroups. Post-hoc comparisons (Tukey HSD) suggested that lower-performing learners benefited most, pointing to the potential of visual scaffolds to narrow achievement gaps. Qualitative findings supported these results, highlighting enhanced conceptual understanding, stronger motivation through co-creation, and increased teacher trust when explainability accompanied AI-generated visuals. Benchmarking showed a trade-off: Gemini provided higher-fidelity visuals suited to complex concepts, while a lightweight variant (NanoBanana) offered lower latency and broader device accessibility.
